test_cases.title: Report period: advance one month forward
test_cases.preconditions:
Logged in as accountant with OFFICES_EDIT on target salary office.
Current report period = March 2026.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/ttt/v1/offices/{officeId}/periods/report — note current start
2. PATCH /api/ttt/v1/offices/{officeId}/periods/report
   Body: {"start": "2026-04-01"}
3. GET period again to verify
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 200. Report period updated to 2026-04-01.
OfficePeriodChangedApplicationEvent published.
Period cache evicted (CACHE_OFFICE_PERIOD).
Employees can no longer submit reports for March.

test_cases.title: Report period: non-first-day-of-month rejected
test_cases.preconditions: Accountant with OFFICES_EDIT.
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400. Error code: exception.validation.period.not.first.day.of.month.
Period unchanged.
test_cases.notes: Validation enforced at service level, not DTO — mid-month date passes DTO parsing

test_cases.title: Report period: set before approve period rejected
test_cases.preconditions: Report period = April 2026, Approve period = February 2026.
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400. Error: exception.validation.period.report.before.approve.
Invariant: APPROVE_PERIOD_START <= REPORT_PERIOD_START enforced.
test_cases.notes: Key invariant — ensures approve period cannot exceed report period

test_cases.title: Approve period: advance one month forward
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant with OFFICES_EDIT. Current approve = Feb 2026.
No employees have extended periods in this office.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/ttt/v1/offices/{officeId}/periods/approve
2. PATCH /api/ttt/v1/offices/{officeId}/periods/approve
   Body: {"start": "2026-03-01"}
3. GET period again
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 200. Approve period updated to 2026-03-01.
OfficePeriodChangedApplicationEvent published.
If TTT_VACATION_ASYNC enabled: RabbitMQ message to TTT_BACKEND_OFFICE_PERIOD_TOPIC.
If disabled: synchronous vacationClient.recalculateAvailableDays() called.

test_cases.title: Approve period: revert (move backward) triggers reopen event
test_cases.preconditions: Approve period = March 2026.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH /api/ttt/v1/offices/{officeId}/periods/approve
   Body: {"start": "2026-02-01"}
2. Verify period changed
3. Check RabbitMQ events (or vacation days recalculation reverse)
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 200. Approve period moved back to 2026-02-01.
OfficePeriodReopenedApplicationEvent published.
RabbitMQ: TTT_BACKEND_OFFICE_PERIOD_REOPENED_TOPIC.
Vacation days recalculation reverse triggered (restores ConfirmationPeriodDays).
test_cases.notes: Reopen vs change distinction: backward = reopen, forward = change

test_cases.title: Approve period: 2-month backward limit enforced
test_cases.preconditions: Today = March 2026. Approve period = March 2026.
test_cases.expected_result:
Step 1: HTTP 400. Error: exception.validation.period.approve.start.min.
Min = today.minusMonths(2).withDayOfMonth(1) = 2026-01-01.
Step 2: HTTP 200. At boundary — accepted.

test_cases.title: Approve period: max 1-month jump enforced
test_cases.preconditions: Current approve = Feb 2026.
test_cases.expected_result:
Step 1: HTTP 400. Error: exception.validation.period.approve.change.more.than.one.month.
Step 2: HTTP 200. Within 1-month forward/backward change limit.

test_cases.title: Approve period: cannot exceed report period
test_cases.preconditions: Report period = March 2026. Approve period = February 2026.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH approve to {"start": "2026-04-01"}
   (exceeds report period)
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400. Error: exception.validation.period.approve.start.max.
Approve period cannot be after report period start.

test_cases.title: Approve period: blocked when any employee has extended period
test_cases.preconditions: Employee X has extended report period in this office.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH approve to advance one month
2. Verify error
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400. Error: approve.notAllowed.extendedPeriod (via MessageUtil).
Entire office blocked by single employee with extension.
test_cases.notes: Design issue: ANY employee blocks ALL. Must remove all extensions first.

test_cases.title: BUG: Approve period accepts non-first-day-of-month
test_cases.preconditions: Accountant with OFFICES_EDIT.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH approve period to {"start": "2026-03-15"}
2. Verify response
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: HTTP 200 — mid-month date accepted.
Report period enforces first-of-month check but approve period does NOT.
Missing validation in patchApprovePeriod.
test_cases.notes: Confirmed in S52 live testing. Report period has this check, approve does not.

test_cases.title: BUG: NPE on null start in PATCH body
test_cases.preconditions: Accountant with OFFICES_EDIT.
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: HTTP 500 NullPointerException.
PeriodPatchRequestDTO has @NotNull on start field but NPE occurs before validation.
Should return 400 with validation error.
test_cases.notes: Stack trace leakage — security issue

test_cases.title: Period min/max cross-office aggregation
test_cases.preconditions: Multiple salary offices exist with different period settings.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/ttt/v1/offices/periods/report/min
2. GET /api/ttt/v1/offices/periods/report/max
3. GET /api/ttt/v1/offices/periods/approve/min
4. GET /api/ttt/v1/offices/periods/approve/max
test_cases.expected_result:
Each returns the earliest/latest period start across all offices.
Min returns the oldest open period.
Max returns the most advanced period.
test_cases.notes: Used by frontend to determine date picker boundaries

test_cases.title: Report period equals approve period: boundary
test_cases.preconditions: Report = March 2026, Approve = March 2026 (same month).
test_cases.steps:
1. Verify both periods at same month
2. PATCH report period to April (advance)
3. Verify approve still at March
test_cases.expected_result:
Both periods can be at the same month.
Report can advance independently.
Report period must always >= approve period.

test_cases.title: Period advance triggers auto-reject of unapproved reports
test_cases.preconditions:
Reports exist in the period being closed.
Some reports are unapproved (state=REPORTED).
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH approve period to advance one month
2. Check if unapproved reports are auto-rejected
3. Verify email notifications sent to affected employees
test_cases.expected_result:
Unapproved reports in closed period are auto-rejected.
Reject notifications sent via sendRejectNotifications scheduler.
Auto-reject sets state to REJECTED with system comment.
test_cases.notes: Cascading effect — period change affects report states

test_cases.title: Period advance triggers vacation day recalculation
test_cases.preconditions: Approve period at February. Employees have time reports for February.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH approve period to March
2. Check vacation day balances for affected employees
3. Verify norm-based recalculation ran
test_cases.expected_result:
If office NormDeviationType != NONE and AV=true:
AvailableDaysRecalculationServiceImpl recalculates.
Difference = reported - personalNorm.
daysDelta = difference / 8, scale 3, HALF_UP.
ConfirmationPeriodDays records saved for potential reverse.
test_cases.notes: Only runs if NormDeviationType != NONE AND isAdvanceVacation == true

test_cases.title: Period caching: eviction on PATCH, cache hit on GET
test_cases.preconditions: Period already queried (cached).
test_cases.steps:
1. GET period — note response time
2. GET period again — should be from cache
3. PATCH period — evicts cache
4. GET period — fresh from DB
test_cases.expected_result:
Cache key: CACHE_OFFICE_PERIOD.
PATCH explicitly evicts cache entry.
Subsequent GET fetches from database.

test_cases.title: BUG: AUTHENTICATED_USER permission on PATCH endpoints
test_cases.preconditions: Regular employee (no OFFICES_EDIT permission).
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 403 Forbidden.
Controller annotation uses AUTHENTICATED_USER (overly permissive),
but OfficePeriodValidator.validateWriteAccess() provides real access control.
Should still return 403 due to service-level guard.
test_cases.notes: Design issue: controller-level @PreAuthorize is too broad, relies on service-level check

test_cases.title: BUG: Invalid office ID returns 200 with default data
test_cases.preconditions: Valid authentication.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/ttt/v1/offices/99999/periods/report
2. GET /api/ttt/v1/offices/0/periods/report
3. GET /api/ttt/v1/offices/-1/periods/report
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: Returns 200 with default period = previous month 1st day.
Should return 404 for nonexistent office.
InternalOfficePeriodService.findPeriodStart defaults to previousMonthFirstDay.
test_cases.notes: Design issue: default period for non-existent offices masks errors

test_cases.title: Extended period: grant and verify employee can report in approve period
test_cases.preconditions:
Office with report period=April, approve period=March.
Employee X is in this office.
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT extended period for employee X
2. GET employee report period — should return approve period start (March)
3. Verify employee can submit reports for March dates
4. Verify notification sent about extension
test_cases.expected_result:
Employee X can report from March (approve period) instead of April (report period).
getEmployeeReportPeriod returns approve period start when extension active.
Email notification sent on grant.

test_cases.title: Extended period: remove and verify employee reverts to normal
test_cases.preconditions: Employee X has active extended period.
test_cases.steps:
1. DELETE extended period for employee X
2. GET employee report period — should return report period start
3. Verify notification sent about removal
test_cases.expected_result:
Employee X can no longer report in approve period.
getEmployeeReportPeriod returns report period start.
Email notification sent on removal.

test_cases.title: Extended period: cron cleanup of expired extensions
test_cases.preconditions: Extended periods exist with expired deadlines.
test_cases.steps:
1. Trigger ExtendedPeriodScheduler.cleanUp (cron: every 5min)
2. Verify expired extensions removed
3. Verify affected employees notified
test_cases.expected_result:
Expired extensions automatically cleaned up.
ShedLock-protected: only one instance runs.
Affected employees notified via email.
test_cases.notes: Runs every 5 minutes, ShedLock name: ExtendedPeriodScheduler.cleanUp

test_cases.title: Concurrent period modifications by two accountants
test_cases.preconditions: Two accountants have OFFICES_EDIT on same office.
test_cases.steps:
1. Accountant A sends PATCH to advance report period
2. Accountant B sends PATCH to advance report period (simultaneously)
3. Verify one succeeds and the other gets consistent state
test_cases.expected_result:
Both should succeed sequentially (no optimistic locking on periods).
Final state should be consistent (last write wins).
No 500 errors or corrupt state.

test_cases.title: Pay vacation: APPROVED → PAID with correct day split
test_cases.preconditions:
APPROVED vacation (id=V, REGULAR, 10 days) for employee.
Logged in as accountant/chief accountant.
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 200. Status transitions to PAID.
VacationPaymentEntity created with regularDaysPayed=10.
vacation_payment record linked.
VacationStatusChangedEvent published.
Vacation days balance UNCHANGED — deduction happens at approval, not payment.
test_cases.notes: Key insight: days deducted at APPROVAL, payment is accounting-only status change

test_cases.title: Payment days mismatch: regularDays + adminDays != vacation.days
test_cases.preconditions: APPROVED vacation with 5 total days.
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT pay with {"regularDaysPayed": 3, "administrativeDaysPayed": 0}
2. Verify error
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400. Error: exception.vacation.pay.days.not.equal.
checkForPayment validates: regularDaysPayed + administrativeDaysPayed == entity.getDays().
Must submit exact total.

test_cases.title: Payment rejected for non-APPROVED vacation
test_cases.preconditions: Vacation in NEW status (not yet approved).
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT pay vacation in NEW status
2. PUT pay vacation in CANCELED status
3. PUT pay vacation in REJECTED status
test_cases.expected_result:
All return HTTP 400. Error: exception.vacation.status.notAllowed.
Only APPROVED vacations can be paid.

test_cases.title: Payment rejected for APPROXIMATE period type
test_cases.preconditions: APPROVED vacation with periodType=APPROXIMATE (future dates uncertain).
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT pay this vacation
2. Verify error
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400. Error: exception.vacation.period.type.notAllowed.
Only EXACT period vacations can be paid — dates must be finalized.

test_cases.title: Payment rejected for already-PAID vacation
test_cases.preconditions: Vacation already in PAID status.
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT pay this vacation again
2. Verify error
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400. Error: exception.vacation.status.notAllowed.
Already-PAID vacation cannot be paid again (idempotency fail).

test_cases.title: Payment: write lock prevents concurrent duplicate payments
test_cases.preconditions: APPROVED vacation V.
test_cases.steps:
1. Send two simultaneous PUT /pay/{V} requests
2. Verify one succeeds, other fails or is queued
test_cases.expected_result:
Write lock acquired via findByIdAndAcquireWriteLock.
Second request blocks until first completes.
If first succeeds, second gets 400 (already PAID).
No duplicate payment records created.
test_cases.notes: Uses SELECT FOR UPDATE in repository

test_cases.title: Payment validation: regularDaysPayed range 0-366
test_cases.preconditions: APPROVED vacation.
test_cases.expected_result:
Step 1: HTTP 400. @Range(0,366) violation.
Step 2: HTTP 400. @Range(0,366) violation.
Step 3: HTTP 400 (days mismatch unless vacation has 0 days).

test_cases.title: Payment: null/empty body rejected
test_cases.preconditions: APPROVED vacation.
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT pay with empty body {}
2. PUT pay with null body
test_cases.expected_result: HTTP 400. regularDaysPayed and administrativeDaysPayed must not be null (@NotNull).

test_cases.title: BUG: Payment type misalignment allowed
test_cases.preconditions: ADMINISTRATIVE vacation (1 day).
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: HTTP 200 — payment accepted with wrong type split.
checkForPayment validates only total (regular+admin==days),
NOT that the split matches paymentType.
Causes incorrect accounting classification.
test_cases.notes: Confirmed in live testing. ADMINISTRATIVE vac paid with regularDaysPayed=1.

test_cases.title: BUG: Same error code for 5 different payment failures
test_cases.preconditions: Various invalid payment states.
test_cases.steps:
1. Pay non-APPROVED vacation → exception.vacation.status.notAllowed
2. Pay APPROXIMATE period → same code
3. Pay without accountant role → same code
4. Observe that 3 distinct failures return identical error code
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: All 4 scenarios return exception.vacation.status.notAllowed.
Cannot distinguish cause from error response.
Only days mismatch has distinct code: exception.vacation.pay.days.not.equal.
test_cases.notes: Design issue: impossible for frontend/user to know what went wrong

test_cases.title: Partial payment: day return to balance
test_cases.preconditions: REGULAR vacation for 5 days. Employee has 3 nextYear + 2 thisYear days used.
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 200 (if total matches — but this is 3 != 5 → rejected).
NOTE: partial payment is NOT supported by the current validation.
regularDaysPayed + administrativeDaysPayed MUST equal vacation.getDays().
The returnDaysToEmployeeIfPaidLess logic exists but is unreachable for partial.
test_cases.notes: Misleading: code has returnDaysToEmployeeIfPaidLess but validation prevents partial pay

test_cases.title: Administrative vacation: skip day redistribution on payment
test_cases.preconditions: APPROVED administrative vacation, 2 days.
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT pay with {"regularDaysPayed": 0, "administrativeDaysPayed": 2}
2. GET vacation days before and after
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 200. Status → PAID.
ADMINISTRATIVE vacations skip returnDaysToEmployeeIfPaidLess entirely.
Day balance unchanged (admin days not deducted from regular balance).

test_cases.title: Auto-payment cron: payExpiredApproved
test_cases.preconditions: APPROVED vacations older than 2 months exist.
test_cases.steps:
1. Trigger AutomaticallyPayApprovedTask (cron: daily 00:00 NSK)
2. Check vacation statuses after
3. Verify payment records created
test_cases.expected_result:
APPROVED vacations with endDate < today.minusMonths(2).withDayOfMonth(2) are auto-paid.
Each auto-payment: regularDays or adminDays assigned based on paymentType.
ShedLock name: CloseOutdatedTask.run.
Status → PAID.
test_cases.notes: Hardcoded 2-month threshold, Collections.singleton(0L) magic value

test_cases.title: Payment dates endpoint: valid calculation
test_cases.preconditions: Valid vacation dates.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/vacation/v1/paymentdates?vacationStartDate=2026-06-01&vacationEndDate=2026-06-14
2. Verify date list
test_cases.expected_result:
Returns set of 1st-of-month dates between:
(vacStart - 2 months, bounded by report period) and (vacEnd + 6 months).
E.g. 2026-04-01 through 2026-12-01 (array of LocalDate).

test_cases.title: BUG: Payment dates accepts start > end
test_cases.preconditions: Valid authentication.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/vacation/v1/paymentdates?vacationStartDate=2026-04-01&vacationEndDate=2026-03-01
   (start after end)
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: HTTP 200 — returns valid results (same as normal range).
No validation that vacationStartDate <= vacationEndDate.
test_cases.notes: Confirmed in live testing

test_cases.title: Available paid days: binary search mode (newDays=0)
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with vacation days balance.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/vacation/v1/vacationdays/available
   ?employeeLogin={login}&paymentDate=2026-06-01&newDays=0
2. Verify response
test_cases.expected_result:
newDays=0 triggers binary search mode.
Returns maximum safe days (availablePaidDays).
daysNotEnough array lists future vacations that would be affected.
test_cases.notes: Used by payment UI main page mode

test_cases.title: BUG: Available paid days accepts negative newDays
test_cases.preconditions: Valid employee login.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/vacation/v1/vacationdays/available?...&newDays=-5
2. Verify response
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: Returns availablePaidDays=16.0 without error.
Should reject non-positive newDays values.
test_cases.notes: Confirmed in live testing

test_cases.title: BUG: VacationStatusUpdateJob 2-hour orphan window
test_cases.preconditions: Vacation payment creates NEW_FOR_PAID status_updates entry.
test_cases.steps:
1. Create vacation payment (triggers status update job entry)
2. Wait >2 hours (or check DB for stuck entries)
3. Query: SELECT * FROM status_updates WHERE status='NEW_FOR_PAID'
   AND created < now() - interval '2 hours'
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: VacationStatusUpdateJob queries findRecentNew(now.minusHours(2)).
Entries older than 2 hours are permanently orphaned.
Found 6 stuck entries for Saturn office (created 18:22-18:27, >19h old).
No cleanup/retry mechanism exists.
test_cases.notes: Entries are lost forever — no retry, no alerting, no manual recovery

test_cases.title: BUG: DB/API data representation inconsistency for ADMINISTRATIVE vacations
test_cases.preconditions: ADMINISTRATIVE vacation in database.
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: ADMINISTRATIVE vacations store days in DB regular_days column
(e.g., regular_days=1, administrative_days=0),
but API returns them swapped: regularDays=0, administrativeDays=1.
DTO conversion transposes based on payment_type.
DB queries for reporting give wrong day-type breakdowns.
test_cases.notes: Confirmed in live testing

test_cases.title: Payment timeline audit: VACATION_PAID event completeness
test_cases.preconditions: Pay a vacation and check timeline.
test_cases.expected_result:
Timeline event created with type=VACATION_PAID.
BUG: days_used=0, administrative_days_used=0 — event doesn't record
how many days were paid or the regular/admin split.
previous_status=NULL. Audit trail incomplete.
test_cases.notes: Audit gap — cannot reconstruct payment details from timeline alone

test_cases.title: Nonexistent vacation ID payment returns 400 not 404
test_cases.preconditions: Valid accountant auth.
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT /api/vacation/v1/vacations/pay/99999999
2. Verify error code and HTTP status
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400. Error: 'Vacation id not found' (plain text, not standard error code).
Should be 404 per REST conventions.
test_cases.notes: Error handling inconsistency — 400 vs 404

test_cases.title: Bulk payment via UI: 'Pay all checked requests'
test_cases.preconditions: Multiple APPROVED vacations visible on /vacation/payment page.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to /vacation/payment
2. Check multiple regular vacation checkboxes
3. Click 'Pay all checked requests'
4. Verify all selected become PAID
test_cases.expected_result:
All checked vacations paid in batch.
Administrative vacations have no checkbox — excluded from bulk.
Individual VacationPaymentEntity created per vacation.
Page refreshes to show updated statuses.
test_cases.notes: UI observation: admin vacations have no checkbox, no status, no actions

test_cases.title: Manual correction: positive adjustment with comment
test_cases.preconditions:
Logged in as accountant. Employee in accountant's assigned office.
Employee available days = 20.0, AV=true.
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 200. availableDays updated to 21.0.
DAYS_ADJUSTMENT timeline event with days_accrued=1.000.
Comment 'Annual correction' stored in event.

test_cases.title: Manual correction: negative adjustment (AV=true office)
test_cases.preconditions: Employee in AV=true office. Available days = 20.0.
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 200. Days reduced by 2.
Negative adjustment allowed for AV=true offices.
Zeroes out previous years' balances, sets current year to correction value.
DAYS_ADJUSTMENT event with negative delta.

test_cases.title: Manual correction: negative adjustment rejected for AV=false office
test_cases.preconditions: Employee in AV=false office (e.g., Venera). Available days = 88.0.
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400. InvalidVacationDaysCorrectionException.
Error: exception.invalid.vacation.days.correction.
AV=false offices cannot have negative corrections.
test_cases.notes: Confirmed in live testing with abaymaganov on Venera

test_cases.title: Manual correction: comment required (1-255 chars)
test_cases.preconditions: Accountant with correct permissions.
test_cases.expected_result:
Steps 1-3: HTTP 400 — comment @NotNull @Size(min=1).
Step 4: HTTP 400 — @Size(max=255) exceeded.
Comment is required for audit trail.

test_cases.title: Manual correction: no numeric limit on availableDays
test_cases.preconditions: AV=true office employee.
test_cases.expected_result:
All accepted (no @Range on availableDays in DTO).
Step 2: accepted for AV=true (no range validation).
Step 3: accepted (zero balance valid).
Only AV=false blocks negative at service level, not DTO level.
test_cases.notes: No upper or lower bound validation in DTO — extreme values accepted

test_cases.title: Manual correction: permission check — accountant for correct office only
test_cases.preconditions: Accountant assigned to office A. Employee is in office B.
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT correction for employee in office B
2. Verify error
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 403. VacationSecurityException.
statusManager.isAccountant(getCurrent()) must be true AND
employee's officeId must be in accountant's assigned offices.

test_cases.title: BUG: pastPeriodsAvailableDays drift on net-zero corrections
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with pastPeriodsAvailableDays = 5.625.
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT correction +1 day
2. PUT correction -1 day (back to original total)
3. GET vacation days — check pastPeriodsAvailableDays
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: pastPeriodsAvailableDays drifts downward.
After net-zero cycle: 5.625 → 0.
Root cause: increases don't add to sub-components,
but decreases subtract from pastPeriodsAvailableDays first (oldest-first).
Total availableDays correct but sub-component breakdown drifts.
Bulk recalculate does NOT fix this.
test_cases.notes: Confirmed in live testing: abpopov 5.625→0 after 4 net-zero corrections

test_cases.title: BUG: double arithmetic for financial day calculations
test_cases.preconditions: Positive adjustment where daysChangedByAccounting > availableDays.
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT correction with fractional value near floating point boundary
   e.g., availableDays=0.1+0.2 (JavaScript: 0.30000000000000004)
2. Verify storage precision
test_cases.expected_result:
Potential precision issue: code uses double arithmetic.
daysToSubract variable uses doubleValue() for BigDecimal operations.
Risk of floating point rounding errors in financial calculations.
test_cases.notes: Design issue: double for financial math. Impact depends on specific values.

test_cases.title: Bulk recalculate for salary office
test_cases.preconditions: Office with multiple employees.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/vacationdays/recalculate?officeId={id}&date={today}
2. Check for DAYS_ADJUSTMENT events for affected employees
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 200. Recalculation runs for all employees in office.
Creates DAYS_ADJUSTMENT events for employees with norm differences.
Only affects employees who started before recalculation month.
Only runs if NormDeviationType != NONE and AV=true.
test_cases.notes: Tested: POST recalculate created +1.313 days for amatiushin

test_cases.title: Norm recalculation: skip conditions
test_cases.preconditions: Office with NormDeviationType=NONE or AV=false.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST recalculate for office with NormDeviationType=NONE
2. POST recalculate for office with AV=false
3. POST recalculate for employee who started after recalculation month
test_cases.expected_result:
No DAYS_ADJUSTMENT events created.
All three conditions must be met:
1. NormDeviationType != NONE
2. isAdvanceVacation == true
3. Employee started before recalculation month.

test_cases.title: BUG: Norm recalculation double comparison (== 0) unreliable
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with reported hours very close to personalNorm.
test_cases.steps:
1. Set reported hours = personalNorm (e.g., both 160.0)
2. Trigger recalculation
3. Check if recalculation is skipped
test_cases.expected_result:
Code uses: if (difference == 0) skip recalculation.
BUG: double equality comparison. If difference is 1e-15 due to
floating point, recalculation runs for effectively-zero difference.
daysDelta = 1e-15 / 8 ≈ 0 but may create spurious events.
test_cases.notes: Design issue: should use epsilon comparison or BigDecimal

test_cases.title: Recalculation reverse on period reopen
test_cases.preconditions: Approve period advanced, norm recalculation ran, then period reopened (reverted).
test_cases.steps:
1. Advance approve period → norm recalculation runs, saves ConfirmationPeriodDays
2. Revert approve period (move backward)
3. Verify days restored from ConfirmationPeriodDays
4. Verify MonthlyRecalculationReverseEvent published
test_cases.expected_result:
Days restored to pre-recalculation values.
ConfirmationPeriodDays records deleted after restore.
MonthlyRecalculationReverseEvent published if delta != 0.

test_cases.title: Days grouped by years breakdown
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with multi-year vacation day balances.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/vacation/v1/vacationdays/{login}/years
2. Verify yearly breakdown
test_cases.expected_result:

draft → ACTIVE (special case in PmToolProjectSynchronizer).
name and accountingName both set to PM Tool project name.
Type: uppercased before valueOf(). Status: uppercased except 'draft'.
preSalesIds: ticket IDs joined with comma separator.
test_cases.notes: Draft → ACTIVE is a deliberate design decision, not a bug

test_cases.title: PM Tool sync: sales filtering removes sales employees
test_cases.preconditions: Project with sales-type references in PM, owner, supervisor, watchers.

3. Check PM, owner, supervisor, watchers fields post-sync
test_cases.expected_result:
Sales employees exist in PM Tool but not in TTT.
removeSalesFromProject() strips sales from: PM, owner, supervisor, watchers.
Null watcher entries also filtered out.

test_cases.title: PM Tool sync: missing employee validation triggers HTTP 500
test_cases.preconditions: Project referencing a CompanyStaff ID that doesn't exist in TTT.
test_cases.steps:

2. Verify validateEmployeesExist() throws IllegalStateException
3. Verify HTTP 500 error (not a proper business exception)
test_cases.expected_result:
IllegalStateException thrown with missing employee details.
Results in HTTP 500 — no proper error handling.
Design issue: should be a business exception with meaningful error code.
test_cases.notes: Design issue: IllegalStateException → HTTP 500 instead of business error

test_cases.title: PM Tool sync: failed entity retry with batching
test_cases.preconditions: Sync encounters timeout/error for some entities.
test_cases.steps:
1. Simulate entity sync timeout (TIMEOUT=10000ms)
2. Verify failed entity ID saved to PmToolSyncFailedProjectRepository
3. Verify retry in batches (configurable batch size, default 10)
4. On retry success: verify removed from failed repository
5. Verify PmToolSyncStatusRepository tracks status
test_cases.expected_result:
Failed entities stored and retried after main sync.
Batch size: pmTool.sync.retry-batch-size (default 10).
Timeout per entity: 10000ms.
Successful retry removes from failed table. Status tracked.

test_cases.title: PM Tool sync: cache eviction after project sync

3. Verify observer sync via batchChangeObservers()
4. Verify postProcess() called after entities synced
test_cases.expected_result:

Observers batch-synced via InternalProjectObserverService.
postProcess() called if any entities were synced.

test_cases.title: Employees page: default view with search

test_cases.title: BUG: Employee PATCH has no @PreAuthorize annotation

Relies entirely on service-level security.
All other employee endpoints have explicit EMPLOYEES_VIEW/AUTHENTICATED_USER.

test_cases.title: CompanyStaff sync: employee data synchronization

CS sync updates: name, department, salary office, active status.
ShedLock-protected.

test_cases.title: Employee search: keyboard layout auto-correction

test_cases.title: Calendar page: default view with year picker and calendar dropdown

test_cases.expected_result:

'Setting up calendars' shows event list: date, duration, reason.
Events: holidays (0h), pre-holiday (7h), etc.
'Add calendar' and 'Create event' buttons available.

test_cases.title: Calendar CRUD: create new calendar
test_cases.preconditions: Admin or Chief Accountant. Navigate to /admin/calendar.
test_cases.steps:
1. Click 'Add calendar'
2. Enter unique calendar name
3. Submit
4. Verify calendar appears in dropdown
5. Try duplicate name — verify rejection
test_cases.expected_result:
Step 3: HTTP 201. Calendar created with audit fields (createdAt, createdBy).
Step 5: @CalendarNameExists validator rejects duplicate.
Frontend: case-insensitive uniqueness check (Yup calendarExists test).
Backend: uniqueness check via CalendarNameExistsValidator.

test_cases.title: Calendar CRUD: case sensitivity gap in name uniqueness
test_cases.preconditions: Admin creating calendars.
test_cases.steps:
1. Create calendar 'TestCalendar'
2. Try creating 'testcalendar' (lowercase)
3. Frontend: case-insensitive check should reject
4. Backend: check if CalendarNameExistsValidator is case-sensitive
test_cases.expected_result:
Frontend does case-insensitive check (Yup custom test).
Backend CalendarNameExistsValidator may be case-sensitive.
Gap: if backend is case-sensitive, bypass frontend creates near-duplicate.
test_cases.notes: Known gap: frontend case-insensitive but backend may be case-sensitive

test_cases.title: Calendar CRUD: update calendar name
test_cases.preconditions: Admin with existing calendar.
test_cases.steps:
1. Select calendar from dropdown
2. PATCH /api/calendar/v2/calendars/{calendarId} with new name
3. Verify name updated, updatedAt/By set
4. Try updating to name of another existing calendar — verify conflict
test_cases.expected_result:
PATCH updates name and updatedAt/updatedBy.
CalendarExistsValidator uses WebUtil.getPathValue('calendarId')
to exclude current calendar from uniqueness check.
Design issue: update() contains calendar.setId(calendar.getId()) — dead code.
test_cases.notes: Design issue: redundant self-assignment in update()

test_cases.title: Calendar CRUD: delete calendar
test_cases.preconditions: Admin with existing calendar.
test_cases.steps:
1. DELETE /api/calendar/v2/calendars/{calendarId}
2. Verify calendar removed from dropdown
3. Verify @CalendarIdExists on non-existent calendarId → 404
4. Try deleting calendar assigned to a salary office — verify behavior
test_cases.expected_result:
Step 1: HTTP 200/204. Calendar deleted.
Step 3: @CalendarIdExists validator returns 404.
Step 4: May fail if calendar has active salary office assignments (FK constraint).

test_cases.title: Calendar CRUD: permission check — ADMIN or CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT only
test_cases.preconditions: Regular employee, manager, accountant (non-chief).
test_cases.steps:
1. POST calendar create as regular employee — verify 403
2. PATCH calendar update as manager — verify 403
3. DELETE calendar as regular accountant — verify 403
4. GET calendars as any CALENDAR_VIEW user — verify 200
5. Verify role naming: hasAnyRole('ADMIN', 'ROLE_CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT')
test_cases.expected_result:
Create/Update/Delete: restricted to ADMIN or CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT.
GET (list): available to any user with CALENDAR_VIEW.
Design issue: inconsistent role naming — 'ADMIN' vs 'ROLE_CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT'.
test_cases.notes: Inconsistent role naming: 'ADMIN' (no prefix) vs 'ROLE_CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT'

test_cases.title: Calendar events: create event with validation
test_cases.preconditions: Admin on /admin/calendar. Calendar selected.
test_cases.steps:
1. Click 'Create event'
2. Set date (YYYY-MM-DD), duration (0-12), reason (min 1 char)
3. Submit and verify event appears in list
4. Verify @DateUniqueOnCreate — duplicate date in same calendar rejected
5. Test boundary: duration=0 (holiday), duration=12 (max), duration=13 (invalid)
test_cases.expected_result:
Event created with CalendarUpdatedEvent published.
Validation: calendarId exists, date not null, duration 0-12 (@Min(0)/@Max(12)),
reason not null + min 1 char, date unique per calendar.
duration=13: HTTP 400. duration=-1: HTTP 400.

test_cases.title: Calendar events: PATCH only updates reason (asymmetric update)
test_cases.preconditions: Admin with existing calendar event.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH /api/calendar/v2/days/{dayId} with new reason
2. Verify reason updated
3. PATCH with new duration — verify duration NOT updated
4. PATCH with new date — verify date NOT updated
5. PATCH with empty reason — verify @Size(min=1) validation
test_cases.expected_result:
Only reason field can be patched.
CalendarDaysPatchRequestDTO only has reason with @Size(min=1).
Design issue: duration and date are immutable after creation.
To change duration/date: delete + re-create.
test_cases.notes: Design asymmetry: PATCH only updates reason, not duration/date

test_cases.title: Calendar events: delete triggers cascade to vacation service
test_cases.preconditions: Admin deleting a calendar event that affects existing absences.
test_cases.steps:
1. DELETE /api/calendar/v2/days/{dayId}
2. Verify CalendarUpdatedEvent published (with diff calculation)
3. Verify CalendarDeletedEvent published
4. Verify vacation service handles cascade: day-off deletion, vacation day recalculation
test_cases.expected_result:
Delete publishes both CalendarUpdatedEvent and CalendarDeletedEvent.
CalendarDeletedEvent triggers absence conflict resolution in vacation service.
Cascade: existing vacations/day-offs may be affected by calendar change.
test_cases.notes: Cross-service cascade: calendar → vacation service for absence conflict resolution

test_cases.title: Calendar events: findByDate returns null instead of 404
test_cases.preconditions: API user querying calendar day by date.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/calendar/v2/days/by-date?date=2026-03-15&calendarId=1
2. Verify response for existing date
3. GET with date that has no event
4. Verify null/empty response (not 404)
test_cases.expected_result:
Existing date: HTTP 200 with calendar day data.
Non-existent date: returns null/empty body — NOT 404.
Design issue: inconsistent REST convention. Client must handle null response.
test_cases.notes: Design issue: returns null instead of 404 for missing entries

test_cases.title: Working days calculation: base + holiday compensation
test_cases.preconditions: Calendar with holidays and pre-holidays for a given period.
test_cases.steps:
1. Query working days for a month with known holidays
2. Verify base calculation: Mon-Fri working days in period
3. Verify holiday compensation: hours adjustment from calendar entries
4. Verify weekend compensation: working weekends, non-working weekdays
5. Test cross-year period (e.g., Dec 2025 - Jan 2026)
test_cases.expected_result:
calculateWorkingDaysInPeriod():
1. Base working days = Mon-Fri count.
2. Holiday compensation = sum of hour adjustments.
3. Weekend compensation = working weekends added, non-working weekdays removed.
Constants: DAYS_IN_WEEK=7, WORKING_DAYS_IN_WEEK=5, START_WEEK_COMPENSATION=8.
Cross-year: queries different calendars per year via OfficeCalendar mapping.

test_cases.title: Salary office calendar mapping (Calendars for SO tab)

test_cases.expected_result:
Calendars for SO tab shows office → calendar assignments.
Design issue: RUSSIAN_CALENDAR_ID hardcoded as default.
Each office can be assigned a different production calendar.
test_cases.notes: Legacy: RUSSIAN_CALENDAR_ID hardcoded constant

test_cases.title: Calendar event: date format and timezone handling

3. Verify time/timezone information is ignored per Javadoc
test_cases.expected_result:

Javadoc: 'All time and timezone information will be ignored.'
Only date portion used.

test_cases.title: TTT Parameters page: view and edit 18 settings

test_cases.title: User Account: Export tab — CSV format settings

Available to all users for personal CSV export preferences.

test_cases.title: User Account: Trackers tab — view configured trackers

test_cases.steps:

2. Verify list of configured tracker credentials
3. Verify supported types: GITLAB, REDMINE, JIRA_TOKEN, JIRA_LOGPASS,
   ASANA, CLICK_UP, PRESALES, YOU_TRACK
test_cases.expected_result:
Trackers tab shows per-user tracker configurations.
8 tracker types defined in enum.
Live data: 19 credentials for 14 employees.
Distribution: GITLAB(7), JIRA_LOGPASS(5), REDMINE(2), CLICK_UP(2),
JIRA_TOKEN(2), PRESALES(1). No ASANA or YOU_TRACK credentials in use.

test_cases.title: Tracker credentials: create with connectivity validation
test_cases.preconditions: User on Trackers tab. No existing credential for target tracker.
test_cases.steps:
1. Click 'Add tracker'
2. Select type (e.g., GITLAB)
3. Enter tracker URL (required, trimmed)
4. Enter credentials (required for GITLAB, REDMINE, YOU_TRACK, JIRA_TOKEN)
5. Submit — verify getCurrentUser() connectivity check
6. Verify credential stored encrypted in employee_tracker_credentials table
test_cases.expected_result:
POST /api/ttt/v1/employees/current/settings/trackers.
Validates via getCurrentUser() on the external tracker.
If tracker unreachable: TRACKER_NOT_AVAILABLE error.
If auth fails: TRACKER_NOT_AUTHORIZED error.
Credentials stored encrypted in DB.

test_cases.title: Tracker credentials: conditional field requirements by type
test_cases.preconditions: User creating tracker credentials for each type.
test_cases.steps:
1. Type=JIRA_LOGPASS: verify login + password required (no credentials field)
2. Type=JIRA_TOKEN: verify login + credentials required (no password)
3. Type=GITLAB: verify credentials required (no login, no password)
4. Type=REDMINE: verify credentials required
5. Type=YOU_TRACK: verify credentials required
6. Type=CLICK_UP: verify credentials required
7. Type=PRESALES: verify URL required only
test_cases.expected_result:
Frontend TrackerValidationSchema conditional logic:
- login: required for JIRA_TOKEN, JIRA_LOGPASS
- credentials: required for GITLAB, REDMINE, YOU_TRACK, JIRA_TOKEN
- password: required only for JIRA_LOGPASS.
TrackerEditValidationSchema: only enforces if value changed from initial.

test_cases.title: Tracker credentials: update with change detection
test_cases.preconditions: User with existing tracker credential.
test_cases.steps:
1. Open edit for existing tracker
2. Change only URL — verify no credential re-validation
3. Change credentials — verify re-validation via getCurrentUser()
4. Verify TrackerEditValidationSchema only enforces changed fields
test_cases.expected_result:
PATCH /api/ttt/v1/employees/current/settings/trackers.
TrackerEditValidationSchema accepts initialValues — 
only enforces requirements on fields that differ from initial.
Connectivity re-checked only if credentials changed.

test_cases.title: Tracker credentials: delete
test_cases.preconditions: User with existing tracker credential.
test_cases.steps:
1. DELETE /api/ttt/v1/employees/current/settings/trackers
2. Verify credential removed
3. Verify encrypted data purged from employee_tracker_credentials table
test_cases.expected_result:
DELETE removes the credential entry.
Only the current user can manage their own credentials.
Existing work log data preserved (tracker_work_log table unaffected).

test_cases.title: Tracker error codes: all 9 error types
test_cases.preconditions: User with various tracker misconfigurations.
test_cases.steps:
1. No tracker configured → TRACKER_NOT_CONFIGURED
2. Tracker server down → TRACKER_NOT_AVAILABLE
3. Invalid credentials → TRACKER_NOT_AUTHORIZED
4. Insufficient permissions → TRACKER_NOT_PERMITTED
5. HTTP URL (not HTTPS) → TRACKER_HTTPS_REQUIRED
6. Unsupported tracker type → TRACKER_NOT_SUPPORTED
7. Proxy issue → PROXY_NOT_AVAILABLE
8. Unknown error → TRACKER_UNKNOWN
9. Resource naming issue → TRACKER_RESOURCE_NAME
test_cases.expected_result:
9 distinct error codes returned by tracker integration.
Each maps to specific conditions in TrackerClient implementations.
TRACKER_HTTPS_REQUIRED: enforces secure connections.
PROXY_NOT_AVAILABLE: proxy configuration for VPN-restricted trackers.

test_cases.title: Tracker credential resolution: fallback chain
test_cases.preconditions: Project with manager and owner tracker credentials.
test_cases.steps:
1. Current user has credentials → verify used directly
2. Current user has no credentials, project manager has → verify PM fallback
3. Neither user nor PM has credentials → verify owner fallback (read-only)
4. None have credentials → verify TRACKER_NOT_CONFIGURED error
test_cases.expected_result:
Resolution chain: current user → project manager → project owner.
Owner fallback is read-only (no work log push).
IssueTrackerService handles credential resolution.

test_cases.title: Work log sync: bidirectional TTT ↔ tracker
test_cases.preconditions: Project with tracker configured. User with credentials.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/ttt/v1/projects/{id}/tracker-work-log/sync with direction=TO_TRACKER
2. Verify SendToTrackerCommand execution
3. POST sync with direction=FROM_TRACKER
4. Verify LoadFromTrackerCommand execution
5. GET /api/ttt/v1/projects/{id}/tracker-work-log/info — verify sync status
test_cases.expected_result:
Bidirectional sync via Command pattern.
TO_TRACKER: pushes TTT work logs to external tracker.
FROM_TRACKER: pulls tracker work logs into TTT.
TrackerSyncStartEvent/FinishEvent published for lifecycle.
DB: 222K rows in tracker_work_log, 41K unique tasks.

test_cases.title: GraalVM custom scripts: task mapping sandbox
test_cases.preconditions: Project with custom taskNameScript configured.
test_cases.steps:
1. Verify CustomScriptService executes taskNameScript in GraalVM sandbox
2. Verify beforeScript.js blocks: while, for, do, goto loops
3. Verify Java reflection blocked (ILLEGAL_JAVA_CALL_ERROR)
4. Verify host access blocked
5. Test boundEmployeeScript and workLogScript execution
test_cases.expected_result:
3 script types: taskNameScript, boundEmployeeScript, workLogScript.
GraalVM JavaScript sandbox with security:
- Loop prevention via string matching (not AST)
- Java interop blocked
- Host access blocked.
Error codes: ILLEGAL_OPERATOR_ERROR, ILLEGAL_FUNCTION_CALL_ERROR, ILLEGAL_JAVA_CALL_ERROR.
test_cases.notes: Design issue: loop prevention via string matching is fragile — not AST-based

test_cases.title: Tracker type: ASANA listed but not implemented
test_cases.preconditions: User attempting to configure ASANA tracker.
test_cases.steps:
1. Verify ASANA exists in EmployeeTrackerCredentialsType enum
2. Try creating ASANA credential
3. Verify behavior (error or phantom success)
test_cases.expected_result:
ASANA enum value exists but Confluence says 'NOT supported'.
No ASANATrackerClient implementation found.
TrackerClientFactory may throw TRACKER_NOT_SUPPORTED.
No ASANA or YOU_TRACK credentials exist in live data.
test_cases.notes: Design issue: enum value for unsupported tracker type

test_cases.title: Admin project: Edit Tracker Data validation
test_cases.preconditions: Admin editing tracker data for a project.
test_cases.steps:
1. Enter invalid URL in 'Link to task tracker' field
2. Verify IsUrl validator rejects non-URL
3. Verify OnlyEnAndNumbers regex: /^[a-zA-Z0-9.]*$/g
4. Verify LessThanTwoChars: length >= 2
5. Enter URL without https:// prefix
test_cases.expected_result:
Frontend admin validators (validation.js):
- Required: non-empty
- OnlyEnAndNumbers: /^[a-zA-Z0-9.]*$/g
- LessThanTwoChars: minimum 2 characters
- IsUrl: domain/path regex
- IsSelect: object with label+value OR string.

test_cases.title: Permission hierarchy: PROJECTS_ALL vs AUTHENTICATED_USER
test_cases.preconditions: Users with different authority levels.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/ttt/v1/projects as AUTHENTICATED_USER — verify access
2. GET /api/ttt/v1/projects as PROJECTS_ALL — verify access
3. DELETE project as AUTHENTICATED_USER (no DELETE permission) — verify service rejection
4. Verify controller @PreAuthorize allows both, service layer enforces
test_cases.expected_result:
Controller uses PROJECTS_ALL or AUTHENTICATED_USER for all endpoints.
Service layer provides real security: PermissionClassType.PROJECTS VIEW.
Design issue: AUTHENTICATED_USER on DELETE endpoint — service guard critical.

test_cases.title: Calendar API: unauthorized access attempts
test_cases.preconditions: Regular employee (no ADMIN/CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT role).
test_cases.steps:
1. POST calendar create as employee — verify 403
2. PATCH calendar update as employee — verify 403
3. DELETE calendar as employee — verify 403
4. GET calendar list as employee with CALENDAR_VIEW — verify 200
test_cases.expected_result:
Create/Update/Delete: ADMIN or CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT only → HTTP 403 for others.
GET: CALENDAR_VIEW authority required → 200 for authorized viewers.
hasAnyRole('ADMIN', 'ROLE_CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT') guard.

test_cases.title: Calendar day: @CalendarDaysIdExists validation

test_cases.title: Calendar day: @DateUniqueOnCreate — duplicate date rejection

3. Verify @DateUniqueOnCreate validator rejects duplicate
4. Create event for 2026-01-01 in different calendar — verify accepted

test_cases.title: Salary office period: DatePeriod validation edge cases
test_cases.preconditions: Admin managing salary office period assignments.
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT period with startDate > endDate — verify @DatePeriodValid rejects
2. PUT period with startDate = endDate — verify accepted (same day period)
3. PUT period with both dates null — verify accepted (null pass-through)
4. PUT period with one date null, one set — verify accepted
test_cases.expected_result:
DatePeriodValidValidator: validates endDate >= startDate only if BOTH non-null.
If either is null, validation passes (no constraint).
Null pass-through is intentional — partial period assignment allowed.
@OfficeIdExists and @CalendarIdExists validators also applied.
test_cases.notes: DatePeriod null pass-through: both null → passes validation

test_cases.title: Employee API: @EmployeeLoginExists validation

4. GET /api/ttt/v1/employees/nonexistent_user/work-periods — verify 404

test_cases.title: Calendar event validation: boundary values for duration field
test_cases.preconditions: Admin creating calendar events with edge-case durations.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST event with duration=0 (holiday) — verify accepted
2. POST event with duration=12 (max) — verify accepted
3. POST event with duration=13 — verify rejected (@Max(12))
4. POST event with duration=-1 — verify rejected (@Min(0))
5. POST event with duration=null — verify rejected (@NotNull)
6. POST event with duration=7 (typical pre-holiday) — verify accepted
test_cases.expected_result:
Duration validation: @NotNull, @Min(0), @Max(12).
Valid range: 0-12 inclusive.
0 = holiday (full non-working day).
7 = typical pre-holiday (reduced day).
12 = theoretical max.
null, negative, >12: HTTP 400.

test_cases.title: Calendar event validation: reason field boundary
test_cases.preconditions: Admin creating/patching calendar events with edge-case reasons.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST event with reason='' (empty) — verify rejected (@Size(min=1))
2. POST event with reason=null — verify rejected (@NotNull)
3. POST event with reason='X' (1 char) — verify accepted
4. PATCH event with reason='' — verify rejected (@Size(min=1))
5. PATCH event without reason field — verify accepted (optional on PATCH)
test_cases.expected_result:
Create: reason is @NotNull + @Size(min=1) — required, min 1 char.
Patch: reason is @Size(min=1) only — optional, but if provided must be 1+ chars.
No max length constraint found — test very long reasons.

test_cases.title: PM Tool sync — rate limiter boundary (50 req/min)
test_cases.preconditions:
PM Tool sync enabled (feature toggle PM_TOOL_SYNC-{env} = ON).
PM Tool has 60+ projects (more than rate limit allows per minute).
test_cases.steps:
1. Trigger manual sync: POST /v1/test/project/sync.
2. Monitor sync duration.
3. Verify all projects synced (not just first 50).
4. DB: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM project WHERE pm_tool_id IS NOT NULL.
5. Check pm_sync_status for completion record.
6. Verify no projects dropped due to rate limiting.
test_cases.expected_result:
All projects synced despite rate limit — sync takes longer but completes.
RateLimiter.acquire() blocks thread until permit available (no timeout).
pm_sync_status shows successful completion.
No projects missing from sync.
test_cases.notes: Guava RateLimiter at 50 req/min. acquire() blocks — verify no OOM or timeout.

test_cases.title: PM Tool sync — API unavailable (connection timeout)
test_cases.preconditions:
PM Tool sync enabled.
PM Tool API is unreachable (simulate by changing pmTool.url to invalid host).
test_cases.steps:
1. Update PM Tool URL config to unreachable host.
2. Trigger manual sync: POST /v1/test/project/sync.
3. Check application logs for error handling.
4. DB: Verify pm_tool_sync_failed_entity table has entries.
5. Verify existing project data unchanged.
6. Check retry batch mechanism activates.
test_cases.expected_result:
Sync fails gracefully — no crash or data corruption.
Failed entities recorded in pm_tool_sync_failed_entity.
Retry mechanism attempts to re-sync failed entities.
Existing project data preserved (no deletions on sync failure).
test_cases.notes: Feign client timeout behavior. Check for proper error logging.

test_cases.title: PM Tool sync — partial failure (some projects fail, others succeed)
test_cases.preconditions:
PM Tool has 10 projects.
1 project references an employee not in TTT DB.
test_cases.steps:
1. Trigger sync: POST /v1/test/project/sync.
2. Check application logs for IllegalStateException.
3. DB: Verify successfully synced projects are updated.
4. DB: Check pm_tool_sync_failed_entity for the failing project.
5. Verify failure is isolated — doesn't abort entire sync.
6. Check retry: failed project retried in next batch.
test_cases.expected_result:
9 of 10 projects sync successfully.
1 project fails with 500 (missing employee → IllegalStateException).
Failed project recorded for retry.
Other projects not affected by the single failure.
test_cases.notes: PmToolProjectSynchronizer validates employee existence — throws IllegalStateException if missing. Should be business error, not 500.

test_cases.title: PM Tool sync — concurrent sync attempts (ShedLock contention)
test_cases.preconditions:
PM Tool sync enabled.
Cron schedule: every 15 minutes.
ShedLock configured for sync job.
test_cases.steps:
1. Trigger manual sync: POST /v1/test/project/sync.
2. Immediately trigger second manual sync.
3. Check application logs for both sync attempts.
4. Verify ShedLock prevents concurrent execution.
5. DB: Check shedlock table for lock status.
6. Verify data consistency — no duplicate projects.
test_cases.expected_result:
First sync acquires ShedLock and runs.
Second sync either waits or skips (ShedLock behavior).
No concurrent execution — data integrity maintained.
No duplicate project entries created.
test_cases.notes: ShedLock prevents concurrent cron execution. Manual trigger may bypass.

test_cases.title: PM Tool sync — draft project silent ACTIVE conversion
test_cases.preconditions:
PM Tool has a project in 'draft' status.
Project does not exist in TTT yet.
test_cases.steps:

2. Trigger sync: POST /v1/test/project/sync.
3. GET /api/ttt/v1/project?name=DraftProject.
4. Check project status in TTT.
5. Verify no notification sent about status conversion.
test_cases.expected_result:
Draft project synced as ACTIVE in TTT.
No notification to PM/owner about draft→ACTIVE conversion.
Project appears in employee project lists immediately.
Design issue: draft projects should not be visible to employees.
test_cases.notes: Design issue: PmToolProjectSynchronizer silently converts draft→ACTIVE. No feature flag to exclude drafts.

test_cases.title: PM Tool sync — pmtId null edge case in UI

Cache evicted after observer update.

test_cases.title: PM Tool sync — sales employee filtering

ownerId = null or previous value (sales person excluded).
managerId and seniorManagerId set correctly (employee type).

test_cases.title: PM Tool sync — rate limiter custom configuration
test_cases.preconditions: Spring property pmTool.sync.fetch-rate-per-minute set to 30 (custom value, below default 50).
test_cases.steps:
1. Set pmTool.sync.fetch-rate-per-minute=30 in application config
2. Restart application
3. Trigger PM Tool sync
4. Monitor timing between consecutive API calls to PM Tool
test_cases.expected_result: Rate limiter enforces ~2 seconds between requests (30 RPM = 0.5 permits/sec). Each fetchRateLimiter.acquire() blocks until permit available. Sync completes slower but without 429 errors.
test_cases.notes: Default: 50 RPM (0.833/sec). Config: @Value("${pmTool.sync.fetch-rate-per-minute:50}"). Not overridden in any config file currently.

test_cases.title: PM Tool sync — shared rate limiter across concurrent entity syncs
test_cases.preconditions: Multiple entity types (projects, employees) sync concurrently through the same PmToolEntitySyncLauncher instance.
test_cases.steps:
1. Trigger full PM Tool sync (projects + related entities)
2. Monitor total API calls per minute to PM Tool
3. Verify calls don't exceed configured limit
test_cases.expected_result: Total API calls across all concurrent sync operations stay within 50 RPM. The RateLimiter instance is shared (singleton bean), so concurrent threads compete for the same rate budget. Guava RateLimiter is thread-safe — serializes acquire() calls.
test_cases.notes: Single RateLimiter instance on singleton Spring bean. Thread-safe token-bucket algorithm.

test_cases.title: Cross-service office consistency — verify after CS sync
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee exists in both ttt_backend and ttt_vacation.
CS sync has run recently.
test_cases.steps:
1. Pick 5 employees from different offices.
2. DB (ttt_backend): SELECT login, salary_office FROM employee WHERE login IN (...).
3. DB (ttt_vacation): SELECT login, office_id FROM employee WHERE login IN (...).
4. Compare salary_office vs office_id for each employee.
5. If mismatch found, check employee_office table for year records.
test_cases.expected_result:
salary_office (ttt_backend) matches office_id (ttt_vacation) for all employees.
If mismatch: document which service has correct value.
NOTE: 62% historical mismatch rate documented — test newly synced employees.
test_cases.notes: Known issue: 736/1190 employees mismatched. Focus on employees synced AFTER the fix was deployed.

test_cases.title: Cross-service office — impact on vacation day calculation
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with different offices in ttt_backend vs ttt_vacation.
Offices have different annual leave norms (e.g., 28 vs 24 days).
test_cases.steps:
1. Find mismatched employee in DB.
2. GET /api/vacation/v1/vacation/days/{employeeId} — available days.
3. GET /api/ttt/v1/statistic/report?employeeId={id} — check norm used.
4. Compare: vacation uses ttt_vacation office norm, statistics uses ttt_backend office norm.
5. Verify which norm is displayed to the user on the main page.
test_cases.expected_result:
Vacation service uses its own office_id for day calculation.
Statistics service uses ttt_backend salary_office for norm calculation.
If offices differ, employee sees inconsistent norms across pages.
Document the discrepancy for each environment.
test_cases.notes: The practical impact: employee might see '28 days norm' in statistics but get '24 days accrual' in vacation.

test_cases.title: Cross-service office — accounting period assignment consistency
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with mismatched office between services.
Accountant opens/closes periods per salary office.
test_cases.steps:
1. Find employee with office mismatch.
2. Check accountant's period management page.
3. Verify which office the employee appears under.
4. Open period for one office, close for the other.
5. Check if employee can submit reports / create vacations.
6. Verify period constraints are consistent.
test_cases.expected_result:
Employee may appear in wrong office for period management.
If accounting uses ttt_backend office → different from vacation office.
Period open/close may affect wrong employee group.
Document which service drives period assignment.
test_cases.notes: Accounting period management uses ttt_backend data. Vacation service uses its own office_id. Potential for period conflicts.

test_cases.title: Cross-service office — new employee first sync consistency
test_cases.preconditions:
New employee created in CompanyStaff.
First CS sync runs for both services.
test_cases.steps:
1. Identify a recently created employee (or create test employee in CS).
2. Trigger CS sync for ttt_backend.
3. Trigger CS sync for ttt_vacation.
4. DB (ttt_backend): SELECT salary_office FROM employee WHERE login = 'new_emp'.
5. DB (ttt_vacation): SELECT office_id FROM employee WHERE login = 'new_emp'.
6. Compare — should match for newly synced employee.
test_cases.expected_result:
Both services assign the same office from CS data.
employee_office year record created in ttt_vacation.
No divergence for fresh sync.
test_cases.notes: If first sync produces a match, the divergence is caused by subsequent office changes — confirms the EmployeeOfficeChangedProcessor is the fix point.

test_cases.title: Cross-service office — production calendar norm consistency
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with mismatched office between services.
Each office uses a different production calendar.
test_cases.steps:
1. Find employee with office mismatch where calendars differ.
2. GET /api/ttt/v1/employee/{id}/report-period — check working hours norm.
3. GET /api/vacation/v1/vacation/days/{employeeId} — check vacation norm.
4. Compare production calendar used for working hours vs vacation duration.
5. Verify holidays differ between calendars.
6. Document impact on vacation spanning holiday periods.
test_cases.expected_result:
Working hours norm uses ttt_backend office → calendar A.
Vacation duration uses ttt_vacation office → calendar B.
Different holidays may cause vacation spanning a calendar A holiday to count differently than calendar B holiday.
Employee sees inconsistent 'norm' across UI sections.
test_cases.notes: Russian vs Cyprus calendars have different holidays. A vacation over Orthodox Christmas (Jan 7) counts differently.

test_cases.title: Cross-service office — employee_office year record vs employee.office_id
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee in ttt_vacation service.
Multiple years of employee_office records exist.
test_cases.steps:
1. DB: SELECT year, office_id FROM employee_office WHERE employee_id = X ORDER BY year.
2. DB: SELECT office_id FROM employee WHERE id = X.
3. Compare: employee.office_id should match the latest employee_office year.
4. Check for gaps (missing year records).
5. Verify all years have valid office references.
test_cases.expected_result:
employee.office_id matches employee_office[current_year].office_id.
No gaps in year records.
All office_id values reference valid offices.
Within ttt_vacation: internal consistency confirmed.
test_cases.notes: Vault note confirms internal consistency within vacation service. The divergence is cross-service only.

test_cases.title: Calendar change → vacation working day recalculation
test_cases.preconditions:
Office with active production calendar.
Employees have approved vacations spanning a working day.
Admin access to Calendar service.
test_cases.steps:
1. DB (ttt_vacation): Note vacation working_days for employee with vacation spanning target date.
2. Admin: Change a working day to a holiday in the production calendar (Calendar service).
3. Wait 5s for RabbitMQ CalendarChangedEvent to propagate.
4. DB (ttt_vacation): Re-query vacation working_days for the same employee.
5. Verify working_days decreased by 1 (holiday no longer counted).
6. GET /api/vacation/v1/vacation/{id} — verify API reflects updated working days.
test_cases.expected_result:
CalendarChangedEvent published to ttt.calendar.topic with routing key 'calendar-changed'.
Vacation service CalendarChangedEventHandler processes the event.
CalendarUpdateProcessor detects working day → holiday change.
VacationCalendarUpdateService.recalculateVacations() updates affected vacations.
working_days count decreases by 1 for vacations spanning that date.
API response reflects the recalculated value.
test_cases.notes: Exchange: ttt.calendar.topic. Handler: CalendarChangedEventHandler. Key flow: CalendarUpdateProcessor.process() with day.diff check.

test_cases.title: Calendar day deletion → day-off cascade delete and notification
test_cases.preconditions:
Office calendar has a non-working day (holiday).
Employee(s) have NEW or APPROVED day-offs on that date.
test_cases.steps:
1. DB (ttt_vacation): Identify day-off requests with status NEW or APPROVED on the holiday date.
2. Note day-off IDs and associated parent vacation IDs.
3. Admin: Delete the calendar day entry (remove the holiday) via Calendar service.
4. Wait 5s for RabbitMQ CalendarDeletedEvent to propagate.
5. DB (ttt_vacation): Verify day-off entities deleted, request statuses updated.
6. DB (ttt_vacation): Verify parent vacations recalculated (+1 working day).
7. DB (ttt_email): Check for notification email to affected employees.
test_cases.expected_result:
CalendarDeletedEvent published to ttt.calendar.deleted.topic.
EmployeeDayOffCalendarUpdateService.deleteDayOffs() executes:
  - Day-off entities with status NEW/APPROVED for that date are deleted.
  - Parent vacations recalculated (working days +1 per deleted day-off).
  - EmployeeDayOffDeletedFromCalendarEvent published → notification sent.
Employee receives notification email about the deleted day-off.
test_cases.notes: Exchange: ttt.calendar.deleted.topic. Handler: CalendarDeletedEventHandler. Cascade: delete day-offs → recalc parent vacation → notify employee.

test_cases.title: Period advance → vacation payment marking and day-off rejection
test_cases.preconditions:
Office with open APPROVE period.
Employees have approved vacations in the current period.
Employee(s) have day-offs past the new period boundary.
test_cases.steps:
1. Note current APPROVE period dates for the office.
2. DB (ttt_vacation): Identify approved day-offs past the period boundary.
3. Accountant: Advance the APPROVE period via TTT Backend.
4. Wait 5s for PeriodChangedEvent to propagate via RabbitMQ.
5. DB (ttt_vacation): Check vacation_status_updates table for NEW_FOR_PAID entries.
6. DB (ttt_vacation): Verify day-offs past boundary have status REJECTED_BY_SYSTEM.
7. GET /api/vacation/v1/vacation/days/{employeeId} — verify available days recalculated.
test_cases.expected_result:
PeriodChangedEvent published to ttt.backend.officePeriod.topic.
Vacation PeriodChangedEventHandler processes (APPROVE type only):
  - AvailableDaysRecalculationService.recalculate() runs for the office.
  - EmployeeDayOffService.rejectedBySystem() rejects day-offs past boundary.
  - VacationStatusUpdatesRepository inserts NEW_FOR_PAID entries.
Available vacation days recalculated. Day-offs past boundary rejected.
test_cases.notes: Exchange: ttt.backend.officePeriod.topic. Only APPROVE type triggers processing. REPORT period changes do NOT trigger vacation recalculation.

test_cases.title: Period reopen → reverse available days recalculation
test_cases.preconditions:
Office APPROVE period was recently advanced.
Available vacation days were recalculated during advance.
test_cases.steps:
1. DB (ttt_vacation): Note available vacation days for employees in the office.
2. Accountant: Reopen the previously advanced APPROVE period.
3. Wait 5s for PeriodReopenedEvent to propagate via RabbitMQ.
4. DB (ttt_vacation): Verify available vacation days reverted to pre-advance values.
5. GET /api/vacation/v1/vacation/days/{employeeId} — confirm API reflects reversal.
test_cases.expected_result:
PeriodReopenedEvent published to ttt.backend.officePeriod.reopened.topic.
PeriodReopenedEventHandler processes (APPROVE type only):
  - AvailableDaysRecalculationService.recalculationReverse() runs.
  - Vacation day changes from the advance are rolled back.
Available days return to pre-advance values.
test_cases.notes: Exchange: ttt.backend.officePeriod.reopened.topic. Reverse of TC-XSV-009.

test_cases.title: Employee change → cache invalidation in vacation service
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee exists in both services.
Employee data (e.g., name, department) changes in CS or admin panel.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/vacation/v1/employee/{login} — note cached employee data.
2. DB (ttt_backend): UPDATE employee SET name = 'Test Name' WHERE login = '{login}'.
3. Trigger EmployeeChangedEvent via CS sync or manual edit.
4. Wait 3s for RabbitMQ propagation.
5. GET /api/vacation/v1/employee/{login} — verify cache invalidated and fresh data returned.
test_cases.expected_result:
EmployeeChangedEvent published to ttt.backend.employee.topic.
Vacation EmployeeChangedEventHandler evicts employee cache by login.
Next API call to vacation service returns fresh employee data.
Note: Event carries routing key 'employee-changed'.
test_cases.notes: Exchange: ttt.backend.employee.topic. Handler: EmployeeChangedEventHandler. Cache eviction only — no data mutation.

test_cases.title: Month norm recalculation — Vacation → TTT Backend propagation
test_cases.preconditions:
Calendar change or office sync triggers month norm recalculation.
Employee has reports in the affected month.
test_cases.steps:
1. DB (ttt_backend): SELECT month_norm, reported_effort FROM statistic_report WHERE employee_id = X AND period = '{affected_month}'.
2. Trigger a calendar change that affects the working day count for a month.
3. Wait 5s for the cascade: CalendarChanged → Vacation recalc → EmployeeMonthNormContextCalculatedEvent → TTT Backend.
4. DB (ttt_backend): Re-query statistic_report — verify month_norm updated.
5. GET /api/ttt/v1/statistic/report?employeeId={id}&period={month} — verify API reflects new norm.
test_cases.expected_result:
EmployeeMonthNormContextCalculatedEvent published by Vacation to ttt.backend.employee.topic.
TTT EmployeeMonthNormContextCalculatedEventHandler processes it.
StatisticReportSyncService.saveMonthNormAndReportedEffortForEmployees() updates:
  - statistic_report.month_norm recalculated
  - statistic_report.reported_effort preserved
API reflects the new norm value for the affected period.
test_cases.notes: Reverse direction flow: Vacation publishes back to TTT Backend. Triggered by MonthNormContextCalculator.prepareMonthNormCalculationContextForEmployees().

test_cases.title: Async email event — TTT/Vacation → Email service delivery
test_cases.preconditions:
Email async feature toggle enabled (email-async).
Action triggers notification (e.g., vacation approval).
test_cases.steps:
1. DB (ttt_email): Note current email count.
2. Perform a notification-triggering action (e.g., approve a vacation request).
3. Wait 5s for SendEmailEvent to propagate via RabbitMQ.
4. DB (ttt_email): Verify new email record created with correct template and recipient.
5. GET /api/email/v1/email?to={recipient} — verify email in queue.
6. Check email subject and body match expected template.
test_cases.expected_result:
SendEmailEvent published to ttt.email.topic with routing key 'send-email'.
Email SendEmailEventHandler processes:
  - Converts SendEmailMQ → EmailBO
  - Saves email record for batch sending
Email record appears in ttt_email database with correct template, recipient, subject.
test_cases.notes: Exchange: ttt.email.topic. Feature toggle 'email-async' must be enabled. Without toggle, email is sent synchronously (no RabbitMQ).

test_cases.title: No DLQ — message loss on handler failure (negative test)
test_cases.preconditions:
RabbitMQ configured with no Dead Letter Queue.
Handler will fail (e.g., database constraint violation during processing).
test_cases.steps:
1. Identify a scenario that causes handler failure:
   - Option A: Corrupt data that fails deserialization.
   - Option B: Calendar event referencing non-existent office.
2. Trigger the event.
3. Monitor RabbitMQ management UI or logs for the message.
4. Verify the message is rejected after exception (ack_mode=AUTO).
5. Verify no DLQ queue exists — message is permanently lost.
6. DB: Verify no state change occurred (handler failed before commit).
test_cases.expected_result:
Message is rejected by the handler (IllegalStateException or similar).
No DLQ configured — message is silently dropped.
No retry occurs (requeue_on_rejection=false).
Database state remains unchanged.
EXPECTED: Message is permanently lost — this is a KNOWN DESIGN GAP.
No alerting or logging of the lost message beyond the exception stack trace.
test_cases.notes: KNOWN DESIGN ISSUE: No DLQ. Spring AMQP config: max_attempts=3, exponential backoff 2s initial, requeue_on_rejection=false. After 3 failed attempts, message is silently lost.

test_cases.title: Duplicate event processing — no idempotency guard (negative test)
test_cases.preconditions:
RabbitMQ delivers the same event twice (e.g., network glitch, requeue).
Handler has no idempotency check.
test_cases.steps:
1. Trigger a CalendarChangedEvent that adds a holiday.
2. DB (ttt_vacation): Note vacation working_days count.
3. Manually publish the same CalendarChangedEvent again to the queue.
4. Wait 5s for second processing.
5. DB (ttt_vacation): Check if working_days was decremented twice.
6. Compare expected (1 decrement) vs actual (potentially 2 decrements).
test_cases.expected_result:
EXPECTED BEHAVIOR (bug): Handler processes the event again.
working_days may be decremented twice (double processing).
No idempotency key or event deduplication exists.
KNOWN DESIGN GAP: All handlers process every message exactly as received,
with no check for prior processing of the same event.
Note: Actual impact depends on whether recalculation is idempotent by nature
(if vacation.recalculate() recomputes from source data, result may be correct).
test_cases.notes: KNOWN DESIGN ISSUE: No idempotency guards. Some handlers may be naturally idempotent if they recompute from source data rather than incrementing.

test_cases.title: SystemClockChangedEvent broadcast to all services
test_cases.preconditions:
Timemachine environment with clock manipulation enabled.
All 3 services connected to the same RabbitMQ.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/ttt/v1/test/clock — note current clock across services.
2. PATCH clock to a new date/time (e.g., advance 1 day).
3. Wait 3s for SystemClockChangedEvent via ttt.fanout exchange.
4. GET /api/ttt/v1/test/clock — verify TTT Backend clock updated.
5. DB queries to vacation/calendar: verify operations use the new clock.
6. Reset clock to original time.
test_cases.expected_result:
SystemClockChangedEvent published to ttt.fanout (fanout exchange).
All 3 services receive via their dedicated queues:
  - ttt.fanout.ttt-queue → TTT Backend
  - ttt.fanout.email-queue → Email
  - ttt.fanout.calendar-queue → Calendar
  - ttt.fanout.vacation-queue → Vacation
All services apply the new clock. Cron jobs and date-based logic use updated time.
test_cases.notes: Fanout exchange: ttt.fanout. Timemachine-only. CrudMessageListener processes the clock event in all services.

test_cases.title: CS sync — new employee creation across both services
test_cases.preconditions:
New employee created in CompanyStaff (working=true, active=true).
CS sync not yet run.
test_cases.steps:
1. Trigger CS sync for TTT Backend: POST /api/ttt/v1/test/cs/sync.
2. Trigger CS sync for Vacation: POST /api/vacation/v1/test/cs/sync.
3. DB (ttt_backend): Verify employee record created with correct salary_office.
4. DB (ttt_vacation): Verify employee record created with correct office_id.
5. Compare salary_office vs office_id — should match for new employee.
6. DB (ttt_vacation): Verify employee_office year record created.
7. Verify EmployeeHiredEvent was published by vacation service.
test_cases.expected_result:
Employee created in both services with matching office assignments.
TTT Backend: employee record + 7 role post-processors execute:
  - DeptMgrRole, ProjMgrRole, Cache, Token, OfficeDirector, Accountant, OfficeHR.
Vacation: employee record + EmployeeHiredEvent published.
  - employee_office year record created for current year.
  - Initial vacation day accrual setup triggered.
Office assignments match between services.
test_cases.notes: CSEmployeeSynchronizer detection: isHired = (employee null or !working) AND csEmployee.working AND csEmployee.active.

test_cases.title: CS sync — employee fired lifecycle event
test_cases.preconditions:
Active employee in both services.
Employee marked as not working (working=false) in CompanyStaff.
test_cases.steps:
1. DB (ttt_vacation): Note employee status (working=true).
2. Note any pending vacation requests for the employee.
3. Update employee in CS: set working=false.
4. Trigger CS sync for vacation service.
5. DB (ttt_vacation): Verify employee.working = false.
6. Verify EmployeeFiredEvent was published.
7. Check downstream effects: are pending vacations canceled? Day-offs rejected?
test_cases.expected_result:
Vacation CSEmployeeSynchronizer detects: employee.working AND !csEmployee.working.
EmployeeFiredEvent published.
Employee marked as not working in vacation service.
Downstream behavior: document actual impact on pending requests.
Note: Contractor termination (#2842) is a separate unimplemented flow.
test_cases.notes: Detection: isFired = employee.working AND !csEmployee.working. Separate from contractor blocking flow.

test_cases.title: CS sync — maternity start/end event processing
test_cases.preconditions:
Active employee in vacation service.
Employee's maternity flag will be toggled in CompanyStaff.
test_cases.steps:
1. DB (ttt_vacation): Verify employee.maternity = false.
2. Note employee's current vacation day balance.
3. Update CS: set maternity = true for the employee.
4. Trigger CS sync for vacation service.
5. DB: Verify employee.maternity = true. Verify EmployeeMaternityBeginEvent published.
6. Check: proportional vacation days recalculated? Accrual paused?
7. Reverse: set maternity = false in CS, trigger sync.
8. Verify EmployeeMaternityEndEvent published. Accrual resumes.
test_cases.expected_result:
Maternity start:
  - Detection: !employee.maternity AND csEmployee.maternity
  - EmployeeMaternityBeginEvent published
  - Vacation day accrual calculation may be affected (proportional days)
Maternity end:
  - Detection: employee.maternity AND !csEmployee.maternity
  - EmployeeMaternityEndEvent published
  - Accrual resumes from the end date
V2.1.25 known bug: double accrual possible during maternity period.
test_cases.notes: See vault: maternity-leave-lifecycle.md. V2.1.25 bug: proportional days can double-count if maternity spans year boundary.

test_cases.title: CS sync — office change with same production calendar
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee transfers to new office that uses the SAME production calendar.
EmployeeOfficeChangedProcessor handles the change.
test_cases.steps:
1. DB (ttt_vacation): Note employee's current office_id and office_calendar_id.
2. Identify target office with the same calendar.
3. Change employee's office in CS to the target office.
4. Trigger CS sync for vacation service.
5. DB: Verify office_id updated to new office.
6. DB: Verify employee_office year record updated for current year.
7. Verify vacation day balance unchanged (same calendar = same norms).
test_cases.expected_result:
EmployeeOfficeChangedProcessor.createOrUpdate() processes immediately:
  - Same calendar → immediate update of employee_office year record.
  - office_id updated in employee table.
  - Vacation days unchanged (production calendar norms identical).
  - No deferred processing needed.
test_cases.notes: EmployeeOfficeChangedProcessor: when old and new offices share the same calendar, update is immediate. Contrast with TC-XSV-021.

test_cases.title: CS sync — office change with different calendar (deferred mid-year)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee transfers to new office that uses a DIFFERENT production calendar.
Transfer happens mid-year (not Jan 1).
test_cases.steps:
1. DB (ttt_vacation): Note employee's current office_id and calendar.
2. Identify target office with a different calendar (e.g., Russian → Cyprus).
3. Change employee's office in CS to the target office.
4. Trigger CS sync for vacation service.
5. DB: Check if office_id was updated or deferred.
6. DB: Check employee_office year records — was current year updated?
7. Verify vacation day calculation uses the correct calendar.
test_cases.expected_result:
EmployeeOfficeChangedProcessor.createOrUpdate() detects different calendars:
  - Mid-year change: conditionally skips year record update.
  - BUG: createOrUpdate() may silently skip the update when calendars differ.
  - This is the ROOT CAUSE of the 62% office divergence.
  - employee.office_id may not match employee_office[current_year].office_id.
Expected: Document whether the deferred update is applied or skipped.
test_cases.notes: ROOT CAUSE of 62% divergence. EmployeeOfficeChangedProcessor.createOrUpdate() conditionally skips mid-year changes when calendars differ. See vault: cross-service-office-sync-divergence.md.
