test_cases.title: ProjectManagerRolePostProcessor — wrong role removed on demotion (CRITICAL BUG)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee has ROLE_PROJECT_MANAGER in TTT.
Employee is demoted from project manager in CompanyStaff.
test_cases.steps:
1. DB (ttt_backend): Verify employee has ROLE_PROJECT_MANAGER.
2. DB (ttt_backend): Note if employee also has ROLE_DEPARTMENT_MANAGER.
3. Demote employee from PM in CS (remove project manager flag).
4. Trigger CS sync for TTT Backend.
5. DB: Check which role was removed.
6. Expected BUG: ROLE_DEPARTMENT_MANAGER removed instead of ROLE_PROJECT_MANAGER.
test_cases.expected_result:
KNOWN CRITICAL BUG (confirmed in code, line 39):
ProjectManagerRolePostProcessor.removeRole() calls:
  employeeRoleService.removeRole(employee.getId(), Role.DEPARTMENT_MANAGER)
instead of:
  employeeRoleService.removeRole(employee.getId(), Role.PROJECT_MANAGER)

Result: PM demotion removes DEPARTMENT_MANAGER role.
If employee was both PM and DM, they lose DM role but keep PM role.
If employee was PM only, the removeRole call has no effect (no DM to remove).
test_cases.notes: CRITICAL: Copy-paste bug in ProjectManagerRolePostProcessor line 39. Wrong enum constant used. Affects ALL PM demotions.

test_cases.title: OfficeHRRolePostProcessor — sticky role never removed
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee has ROLE_OFFICE_HR assigned via CS sync.
Employee is removed from HR duties in CompanyStaff.
test_cases.steps:
1. DB (ttt_backend): Verify employee has ROLE_OFFICE_HR.
2. Remove HR assignment in CS.
3. Trigger CS sync for TTT Backend.
4. DB: Check if ROLE_OFFICE_HR was removed.
5. Expected BUG: ROLE_OFFICE_HR remains — only add logic exists, no removal.
test_cases.expected_result:
KNOWN BUG: OfficeHRRolePostProcessor only implements role assignment.
No removal logic exists — role is 'sticky'.
Once assigned, ROLE_OFFICE_HR persists even after CS HR flag is removed.
Manual DB intervention required to remove the role.
Compare with DepartmentManagerRolePostProcessor which has both add AND remove.
test_cases.notes: Contrast: DeptMgrRole has add+remove. OfficeDirectorRole has add+remove. OfficeHRRole has add ONLY. Sticky role accumulation over time.

test_cases.title: CS sync feature toggle gate — disabled sync behavior
test_cases.preconditions:
CS sync feature toggle cs-sync-{env} exists in Unleash.
Toggle can be disabled.
test_cases.steps:
1. Check current Unleash toggle state for cs-sync-{env}.
2. If enabled: trigger CS sync, verify it processes employees.
3. Disable the cs-sync-{env} toggle.
4. Trigger CS sync again.
5. Verify sync silently does nothing — no employees processed.
6. Check logs: is a warning logged about disabled sync?
7. Re-enable toggle after test.
test_cases.expected_result:
CSSyncLauncher.sync() checks feature toggle first.
If toggle disabled: sync exits immediately with no processing.
DESIGN ISSUE: No warning or alert logged when sync is disabled.
Silent no-op — could mask configuration errors where sync is accidentally disabled.
Employees/offices will progressively drift from CS data.
test_cases.notes: Unleash toggle: cs-sync-{env} with env-qualified naming. See vault: feature-toggles-unleash.md. 6 toggles total, all infrastructure.

test_cases.title: CS sync — concurrent execution across services (ShedLock isolation)
test_cases.preconditions:
All 3 services have CS sync scheduled every 15 minutes.
ShedLock prevents concurrent execution within a service.
test_cases.steps:
1. Trigger CS sync for all 3 services simultaneously:
   - POST /api/ttt/v1/test/cs/sync
   - POST /api/vacation/v1/test/cs/sync
   - POST /api/calendar/v1/test/cs/sync (if available)
2. Monitor all 3 services for concurrent processing.
3. DB: Verify ShedLock entries — each service has its own lock.
4. Verify no cross-service lock contention.
5. Verify each service processes its own entity scope independently.
6. Check: do all 3 finish successfully without interfering?
test_cases.expected_result:
Each service uses ShedLock with independent lock names.
All 3 syncs can execute concurrently without interference:
  - TTT Backend: employees + contractors + offices (7 post-processors)
  - Vacation: employees + offices (2 post-processors)
  - Calendar: offices only (startup sync)
No cross-service lock contention. Each service processes independently.
Thread pool: 10s timeout per entity in all services.
test_cases.notes: ShedLock ensures single execution per service. Cross-service concurrency is expected and safe. Page size: 50 entities per request.

test_cases.title: Report CRUD → WebSocket event delivery to subscribed clients
test_cases.preconditions:
User logged in with WebSocket connection established.
Subscribed to /topic/employees/{login}/reports/{period}.
test_cases.steps:
1. Open TTT in browser with DevTools Network/WS tab.
2. Navigate to My Tasks / Planner (establishes WebSocket connection).
3. Create a new time report for the current period.
4. Monitor WebSocket frames for ADD event on reports topic.
5. Edit the report (change hours).
6. Monitor for PATCH event.
7. Delete the report.
8. Monitor for DELETE event.
9. Verify each event contains: EventType, emitterLogin, timestamp, payload.
test_cases.expected_result:
Each CRUD operation triggers a @TransactionalEventListener (fires after commit).
TaskReportEventService sends to /topic/employees/{executorLogin}/reports/{period}:
  - ADD: Event<TaskReportEvent> with report data
  - PATCH: Event<TaskReportEvent> with updated fields
  - DELETE: Event<TaskReportEvent> with deleted report ID
Event envelope: {type, emitterLogin, timestamp, value}.
All events delivered via SimpMessagingTemplate (STOMP).
test_cases.notes: Topic: /topic/employees/{login}/reports/{period}. Listener: WsTaskReportEventListener (@Async @TransactionalEventListener).

test_cases.title: Task rename → cascade to 3 WebSocket channels
test_cases.preconditions:
Task exists in planner with reports and assignments.
Multiple users subscribed to affected topics.
test_cases.steps:
1. Open browser with WS monitoring.
2. Subscribe to:
   - /topic/projects/{projectId}/tasks
   - /topic/employees/{login}/reports/{period}
   - /topic/employees/{login}/assignments/{period}
3. Rename a task in the planner.
4. Monitor all 3 channels for TASK_RENAME events.
5. Verify the task topic receives TASK_RENAME with new name.
6. Verify reports topic receives TASK_RENAME (task name displayed in reports).
7. Verify assignments topic receives TASK_RENAME (task name displayed in assignments).
test_cases.expected_result:
WsTaskEventListener handles task rename:
  1. Sends TASK_RENAME to /topic/projects/{projectId}/tasks
  2. Extracts sub-events for affected employees
  3. Re-publishes to /topic/employees/{login}/reports/{period}
  4. Re-publishes to /topic/employees/{login}/assignments/{period}
All 3 channels receive the rename event. Payload includes old and new task names.
Event type: TASK_RENAME in all 3 messages.
test_cases.notes: Complex event: single rename triggers events on 3 different topic patterns. WsTaskEventListener extracts sub-events and re-publishes.

test_cases.title: Cell lock/unlock → STOMP event propagation between users
test_cases.preconditions:
Two users viewing the same employee's planner.
Both subscribed to /topic/employees/{login}/locks.
test_cases.steps:
1. User A: Open planner for employee X. Subscribe to locks topic.
2. User B: Open planner for employee X in another browser/session.
3. User A: Click on a cell to lock it (start editing).
4. User B: Monitor for LOCK event on locks topic.
5. Verify LOCK event contains: locked cell coordinates, locker login.
6. User A: Release the cell (finish editing or click elsewhere).
7. User B: Monitor for UNLOCK event.
8. Verify UNLOCK event received.
test_cases.expected_result:
WsLockEventListener (@EventListener — fires immediately, not transactional):
  - LOCK event sent to /topic/employees/{employeeLogin}/locks
  - UNLOCK event sent to same topic
  - Payload: cell coordinates, emitterLogin, timestamp
User B sees the lock/unlock in real time.
DESIGN NOTE: No heartbeat-based cleanup — stale locks persist if user disconnects.
Disconnected user's locks are NOT automatically released.
test_cases.notes: Lock uses @EventListener (immediate fire), not @TransactionalEventListener. Known issue: stale lock risk on client disconnect — no cleanup mechanism.

test_cases.title: Assignment generation → WebSocket event on assignment channel
test_cases.preconditions: Planner page open. Manager generates assignments for an employee/period.
test_cases.steps:
1. Open planner with WS monitoring.
2. Subscribe to /topic/employees/{login}/assignments/{period}.
3. Trigger assignment generation (Planner → Generate button).
4. Monitor for GENERATE event on the assignments topic.
5. Verify event payload contains generated assignment data.
6. Verify the UI updates in real-time without manual refresh.
test_cases.notes: GENERATE event is planner-specific. Related to bugs #3332 and #3314 where frontend state management issues cause stale data after generation.

test_cases.title: Unauthorized topic subscription — cross-user data leakage test
test_cases.preconditions:
User A authenticated via WebSocket (JWT).
User B has a different login.
test_cases.steps:
1. User A: Establish WebSocket connection, authenticate with JWT.
2. User A: Subscribe to /topic/employees/{userB_login}/reports/{period}.
3. User B: Create/edit a time report.
4. Monitor User A's subscription for events from User B's topic.
5. Check: does User A receive User B's report events?
6. Verify: is STOMP subscription validated against the authenticated user?
7. Document whether topic access control exists.
test_cases.expected_result:
KNOWN DESIGN GAP: No STOMP subscription authorization.
WsChannelInterceptorAdapter validates authentication at CONNECT time,
but does NOT validate topic subscriptions against the authenticated user.
EXPECTED: User A CAN subscribe to User B's topics and receive events.
This is an information leakage vulnerability.
Mitigation: STOMP subscription interceptor needed to validate {login} in topic
matches the authenticated user's login.
test_cases.notes: KNOWN DESIGN ISSUE: WebSocket auth validates CONNECT only, not SUBSCRIBE. Topic pattern /topic/employees/{login}/* leaks data across users. See security workbook for related cases.

test_cases.title: Banner visibility — appears for all authenticated users
test_cases.preconditions:
User is logged in to TTT (any role: employee, manager, admin, accountant).
Banner has not been permanently dismissed by user.
test_cases.steps:
1. Log in as employee.
2. Observe top-right corner under the header.
3. Verify collapsed banner (cat illustration + slider icon) is visible.
4. Log out. Log in as admin.
5. Verify banner is also visible for admin.
6. Repeat for accountant and manager roles.
test_cases.expected_result:
Banner appears for ALL authenticated users regardless of role.
Banner is in collapsed state (cat + slider icon).
NOTE: Code hardcodes role='Production' for all users — banner library thinks everyone is Production.
test_cases.notes: The requirement says 'Production position type only' but TTT code hardcodes role='Production' for everyone.

test_cases.title: Banner initial state — starts collapsed on every page load
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in. Banner not permanently dismissed.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to any page in TTT.
2. Observe the banner in top-right corner.
3. Verify it shows: small logo, cat illustration, slider icon.
4. Verify it does NOT show expanded content (title, text, Participate button).
5. Click slider to expand the banner.
6. Refresh the page (F5).
7. Verify banner returns to collapsed state (not expanded).
test_cases.expected_result:
Banner always starts in collapsed state.
Expanded state is NOT persisted across page refresh.
After refresh: cat + slider icon visible, no expanded content.
test_cases.notes: TTT calls banner.collapse() immediately after init. Base spec says 'expanded on first login' but TTT overrides to collapsed.

test_cases.title: Collapsed → Expanded — click slider opens full banner
test_cases.preconditions: Banner is in collapsed state.
test_cases.steps:
1. Click the slider icon on the collapsed banner.
2. Verify banner expands to show: logo, title, descriptive text, 'Participate' button, 'Close' (X) icon.
3. Verify expanded banner is in top-right corner, fixed position.
4. Verify all text is readable and properly formatted.
test_cases.expected_result:
Banner transitions from collapsed to expanded state.
All elements visible: logo, title, text, Participate button, Close icon.
Banner remains in top-right corner.

test_cases.title: Expanded → Choice — Close icon shows dismissal options
test_cases.preconditions: Banner is in expanded state.
test_cases.steps:
1. Click the 'Close' (X) icon on the expanded banner.
2. Verify banner transitions to choice state.
3. Verify three options are visible: 'Collapse', 'Remind later', 'Never show again'.
4. Verify each option is a clickable button with blue (#428bca) styling.
test_cases.expected_result:
Banner shows choice state with three dismissal options.
All three buttons are visible and styled in TTT blue.
'Never show again' has blue border (not filled background).

test_cases.title: Choice → Collapsed — 'Collapse' returns to mini banner
test_cases.preconditions: Banner is in choice state (showing three options).
test_cases.steps:
1. Click the 'Collapse' button.
2. Verify banner returns to collapsed state (cat + slider icon).
3. Verify no expanded content visible.
4. Verify banner is still in top-right corner.
test_cases.expected_result:
Banner transitions from choice to collapsed state.
Cat illustration and slider icon visible.
No expanded content shown.

test_cases.title: 'Participate' — opens InnovationLab landing in new tab
test_cases.preconditions: Banner is in expanded state. Language is set to English.
test_cases.steps:
1. Click the 'Participate' button.
2. Verify a new browser tab opens.
3. Verify the URL is https://innovationlab.noveogroup.com/en/
4. Return to TTT tab.
5. Verify banner is no longer visible on the page.
6. Navigate to another page. Verify banner is still hidden.
test_cases.expected_result:
New tab opens with InnovationLab landing page.
URL matches language setting (/en/ for English).
Banner disappears from TTT after clicking Participate.
Banner remains hidden across page navigation.

test_cases.title: 'Participate' URL is language-specific (RU → /ru/, EN → /en/)
test_cases.preconditions: Banner is in expanded state.
test_cases.steps:
1. Set TTT language to Russian (via nav dropdown).
2. Expand banner and click 'Participate'.
3. Verify new tab URL: https://innovationlab.noveogroup.com/ru/
4. Close tab. Wait for banner to reappear (or use test user without prior state).
5. Set TTT language to English.
6. Expand banner and click 'Participate'.
7. Verify new tab URL: https://innovationlab.noveogroup.com/en/
test_cases.expected_result:
Russian language → /ru/ URL.
English language → /en/ URL.
Language for URL is determined by TTT's current language, not CS.

test_cases.title: 'Remind later' — banner hidden, reappears after 3 calendar months
test_cases.preconditions: Banner is in choice state.
test_cases.steps:
1. Click 'Remind later' button.
2. Verify banner disappears completely.
3. Navigate to multiple pages. Verify banner remains hidden.
4. Log out and log in again. Verify banner still hidden.
5. [MANUAL/Long-term] After 3 calendar months, log in. Verify banner reappears in expanded state.
test_cases.expected_result:
Banner disappears immediately after clicking 'Remind later'.
Remains hidden across navigation and re-login.
After 3 calendar months: banner reappears in expanded state.
NOTE: 3 calendar months, not 90 days — verify month boundary behavior.
test_cases.notes: Long-term verification requires system clock manipulation or waiting. On timemachine env, use clock API to advance 3 months.

test_cases.title: 'Never show again' — banner permanently hidden
test_cases.preconditions: Banner is in choice state.
test_cases.steps:
1. Click 'Never show again' button.
2. Verify banner disappears completely.
3. Navigate to multiple pages. Verify banner remains hidden.
4. Log out and log in again. Verify banner still hidden.
5. Clear browser cache/cookies. Log in again.
6. Check if banner reappears (depends on state storage mechanism).
test_cases.expected_result:
Banner disappears immediately after clicking 'Never show again'.
Remains hidden across navigation and re-login.
If state is stored per-userId in banner library (not just localStorage), clearing cookies should NOT bring it back.
If state is localStorage-only, clearing storage will reset state.

test_cases.title: State persistence — 'Never show' survives logout and re-login
test_cases.preconditions: User has previously clicked 'Never show again'.
test_cases.steps:
1. Log out of TTT.
2. Log in as the SAME user.
3. Navigate to several pages.
4. Verify banner does not appear on any page.
5. Log in as a DIFFERENT user (who has not dismissed banner).
6. Verify banner appears for the different user.
7. Verify per-user isolation: one user's dismissal does not affect another.
test_cases.expected_result:
Same user: banner remains hidden after re-login.
Different user: banner appears normally.
State is per-userId (companyStaffId), not global.
test_cases.notes: Banner uses userId param for persistence — verify isolation between users.

test_cases.title: Banner re-collapses on page refresh even if expanded
test_cases.preconditions: Banner is in expanded state (user clicked slider).
test_cases.steps:
1. Click slider to expand the banner.
2. Verify banner is expanded (title, text, Participate visible).
3. Press F5 to refresh the page.
4. Wait for page to load.
5. Check banner state.
6. Navigate to a different page. Check banner state again.
test_cases.expected_result:
After refresh: banner returns to collapsed state (cat + slider).
After navigation: banner is in collapsed state.
Expanded state is transient — never persisted.
This is by TTT design: banner.collapse() called on every init.

test_cases.title: Language switch mid-session — banner reinitializes with new language
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in. Banner is visible (collapsed). Language is English.
test_cases.steps:
1. Expand the banner. Note the text language (English).
2. Switch TTT language to Russian via nav dropdown.
3. Observe the banner after language switch.
4. If banner auto-reinitializes: verify text is now in Russian.
5. If banner does not update: collapse and re-expand.
6. Verify banner content language matches TTT language.
test_cases.expected_result:
Banner text updates to match the selected TTT language.
The useEffect dependency on currentLanguage triggers reinit.
Both expanded text and button labels should reflect the new language.
test_cases.notes: useEffect in AppContainer depends on currentLanguage — should trigger banner reinit.

test_cases.title: Banner fixed position — stays visible during page scroll
test_cases.preconditions: Banner is visible (collapsed or expanded). Page has scrollable content.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to a page with long scrollable content (e.g., reports table).
2. Verify banner is in top-right corner.
3. Scroll down the page.
4. Verify banner remains fixed in top-right corner of viewport.
5. Scroll to bottom of page.
6. Verify banner is still visible in the same position.
7. Repeat with expanded banner state.
test_cases.expected_result:
Banner position is fixed (not absolute) — stays in viewport during scroll.
Both collapsed and expanded states remain fixed.

test_cases.title: Banner non-blocking — page elements remain interactable
test_cases.preconditions: Banner is visible (collapsed) in top-right corner.
test_cases.steps:
1. Open a page where content may be near the top-right corner.
2. Verify all page buttons, links, and inputs are clickable.
3. Expand the banner.
4. Try to interact with page elements near/behind the banner.
5. Verify the 'Add task' button (narrow screens) is not blocked.
6. Open dropdown menus near the banner area.
7. Verify dropdowns display above/below the banner without being blocked.
test_cases.expected_result:
Page content remains fully interactable.
Banner does not capture clicks intended for page elements.
Dropdown menus and buttons near the banner area work correctly.
On narrow screens, 'Add task' button is accessible.
test_cases.notes: Concern raised in issue: narrow screens may have Add task button blocked by banner.

test_cases.title: Collapsed banner — entire area clickable (expanded hit area)
test_cases.preconditions: Banner is in collapsed state.
test_cases.steps:
1. Hover over the collapsed banner. Note the cursor change area.
2. Click on the cat illustration (not the slider icon).
3. Verify banner expands (the ::before pseudo-element extends hit area).
4. Collapse banner again.
5. Click on the logo area of the collapsed banner.
6. Verify banner expands.
7. Verify the entire 68×372px area acts as clickable.
test_cases.expected_result:
Clicking anywhere on the collapsed banner (not just the slider icon) expands it.
The CSS ::before pseudo-element on the slider button extends the hit area.
Hit area covers the full collapsed banner (68×372px).
test_cases.notes: style-overrides.css adds ::before on slider-button with 68x372px transparent area.

test_cases.title: Footer displays build number from ttt-backend actuator
test_cases.preconditions:
TTT application is deployed and running.
User is logged in.
test_cases.steps:
1. Observe the app footer.
2. Note the displayed 'Build #' value.
3. Call GET /api/ttt/actuator/info.
4. Extract `build.app.version` from the response.
5. Compare footer value with actuator response value.
test_cases.expected_result:
Footer 'Build #' matches `build.app.version` from /api/ttt/actuator/info.
Format: <maven-version>.<CI_PIPELINE_ID> (e.g., '2.1.26-SNAPSHOT.290485').

test_cases.title: Build number matches CI pipeline ID
test_cases.preconditions:
A recent CI pipeline has run on release/2.1.
The build has been deployed to the test environment.
test_cases.steps:
1. Check GitLab CI — note the latest pipeline ID on release/2.1 that was deployed.
2. Call GET /api/ttt/actuator/info on the deployed environment.
3. Extract `build.ci.build-number` from the response.
4. Compare with the pipeline ID from GitLab.
5. Verify `build.app.version` ends with the same pipeline ID.
test_cases.expected_result:
build.ci.build-number = CI pipeline ID from GitLab.
build.app.version ends with .<pipeline_id>.
The footer shows this exact pipeline ID, making it traceable.
test_cases.notes: Core goal of #3036: traceability from deployed build to CI pipeline.

test_cases.title: Build date displays formatted Maven build timestamp
test_cases.preconditions: TTT application is deployed and running.
test_cases.steps:
1. Observe footer 'Build date' value.
2. Call GET /api/ttt/actuator/info.
3. Extract `build.maven.buildtime` from the response.
4. Verify the footer date is a formatted version of the raw buildtime.
5. Verify the date corresponds to when the CI pipeline ran (not stale).
test_cases.expected_result:
Footer 'Build date' is a human-readable format of build.maven.buildtime.
Date is recent (matches the latest deployment, not an old build).

test_cases.title: Build number updates when non-TTT service changes (CI always rebuilds TTT)
test_cases.preconditions:
A code change is made ONLY in vacation service (no ttt-backend changes).
CI pipeline runs on release/2.1.
test_cases.steps:
1. Note current build number on the environment.
2. Make a change only to vacation service code (or verify from CI history).
3. After CI pipeline runs, verify TTT service was also rebuilt.
4. Deploy the new build.
5. Check footer — verify build number has updated to the new pipeline ID.
6. Repeat for a change only in calendar or email service.
test_cases.expected_result:
Build number updates even when only non-TTT services changed.
This is because CI always rebuilds the TTT service on key branches.
The actuator always reflects the latest pipeline.
test_cases.notes: The fix in .gitlab-ci-ttt-module.yml ensures TTT service always rebuilds on master, release/*, hotfix/*, pre-release/*, development-ttt branches.

test_cases.title: Footer visible on login page before authentication
test_cases.preconditions: User has not logged in.
test_cases.steps:
1. Open TTT login page.
2. Observe the footer area.
3. Verify 'Build #' and 'Build date' are displayed.
4. Verify the values are not default ('0' / '0').
5. Call GET /actuator/info (frontend-app actuator) — this is unauthenticated.
6. Call GET /api/ttt/actuator/info — also allowed without auth.
test_cases.expected_result:
Footer is visible on the login page with correct build info.
Build # and Build date show real values, not defaults.
/actuator/info is accessible without authentication (nginx allows it).
test_cases.notes: Nginx config (ttt.conf lines 55-58) explicitly allows /actuator/info without access restrictions.

test_cases.title: Actuator endpoint accessible without authentication
test_cases.preconditions: No authentication token/session.
test_cases.steps:
1. Call GET /api/ttt/actuator/info without any auth headers.
2. Verify HTTP 200 response.
3. Verify response contains build.app.version, build.maven.buildtime.
4. Call GET /api/ttt/actuator/health — verify this is also accessible.
5. Call GET /api/ttt/actuator/env — verify this is RESTRICTED (403/401).
6. Verify broader /actuator endpoints are restricted by nginx.
test_cases.expected_result:
GET /api/ttt/actuator/info returns 200 without auth.
Response contains full build metadata.
Other actuator endpoints (env, beans, etc.) are restricted.
Only /actuator/info and /actuator/health are publicly accessible.
test_cases.notes: Nginx allows specific /actuator/info but blocks broader /actuator paths.

test_cases.title: Default footer values when actuator is unavailable
test_cases.preconditions: ttt-backend service is temporarily down or unreachable.
test_cases.steps:
1. [Simulated] Block /api/ttt/actuator/info (e.g., service stopped).
2. Load the login page or refresh a logged-in page.
3. Observe the footer.
4. Verify default values: Build #: 0 | Build date: 0.
5. Restore ttt-backend service.
6. Refresh page. Verify build info loads correctly.
test_cases.expected_result:
When actuator is unavailable, footer shows defaults: Build #: 0, Build date: 0.
No error message or broken UI.
After service recovery, footer shows correct values on next page load.
test_cases.notes: Footer default props: build='0', date='0'. Saga handles API failure gracefully.

test_cases.title: Create day-off request — happy path
test_cases.preconditions:
Active employee with office calendar containing a public holiday
Test data: SELECT c.date FROM calendar_day c JOIN office o ON c.calendar_id=o.calendar_id WHERE c.day_type='PUBLIC_HOLIDAY' AND c.date > CURRENT_DATE LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff
2. Body: {employeeLogin, publicDate: <future holiday>, personalDate: <future workday>, originalDate: <same as publicDate>, duration: 8, reason: 'Test day-off'}
3. Verify response status 200
4. GET /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff?searchType=MY to confirm
test_cases.expected_result:
Day-off request created with status=NEW
Approver auto-assigned (employee's manager)
Optional approvers synced from employee defaults
EmployeeDayOffCreatedEvent published
test_cases.notes: Core happy path. Verify via DB: SELECT * FROM employee_dayoff_request WHERE id=<new_id>

test_cases.title: Create day-off — CPO self-approves on creation
test_cases.preconditions:
CPO/Department Manager employee
Test data: SELECT e.login FROM employee e JOIN employee_global_roles r ON e.id=r.employee_id WHERE r.role='ROLE_DEPARTMENT_MANAGER' AND e.status='ACTIVE' LIMIT 3
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff as CPO user
2. Body: valid day-off request
3. Check status and approver in response
4. DB: SELECT status, approver_id FROM employee_dayoff_request WHERE id=<new_id>
test_cases.expected_result:
Request created with status=APPROVED (auto-approved)
Approver = self (CPO's own ID)
Ledger entries created immediately (credit + debit)
Manager added as optional approver with ASKED status
test_cases.notes: CPO pattern: self-approve + manager as optional. Same as vacation CPO flow.

test_cases.title: Create day-off — duplicate publicDate rejected
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with existing day-off request for a specific publicDate
Test data: SELECT employee_login, original_date FROM employee_dayoff_request WHERE status IN ('NEW','APPROVED') LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff
2. Body: publicDate = same as existing request's originalDate
3. Check error response
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400
errorCode: validation.EmployeeDayOffPublicDateExists.message
Duplicate request for same public date blocked by @EmployeeDayOffPublicDateExists validator
test_cases.notes: Custom validator checks for existing request with same publicDate

test_cases.title: Create day-off — publicDate not in calendar
test_cases.preconditions: Active employee, date that is NOT in office calendar as public holiday
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff
2. Body: publicDate = a regular workday (not in calendar)
3. Check error response
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400
errorCode: validation.PublicDateNotFoundInCalendar.message
Date not found in office calendar OR employee's day-off calendar
test_cases.notes: Validator checks date exists in office calendar OR employee's dayoff calendar

test_cases.title: Create day-off — personalDate already used
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with existing day-off using a specific personalDate
Test data: SELECT personal_date FROM employee_dayoff_request WHERE status='APPROVED'
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff
2. Body: personalDate = date already used by another day-off request
3. Check error response
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400
PersonalDateValidator rejects: personalDate already used by another request
Error in personalDate field validation
test_cases.notes: @EmployeeDayOffPersonalDateExists custom validator

test_cases.title: Create day-off — all fields null (missing @NotNull)
test_cases.preconditions: Active employee
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff
2. Body: {} (empty object, all fields null)
3. Check response
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: No @NotNull on any DTO field
Custom validators treat null as valid (return true for null)
Possible NPE in service layer when processing null fields
May create request with all null values
test_cases.notes: All DTO fields lack @NotNull. Both custom validators return true for null input.

test_cases.title: Create day-off — null duration (no validation)
test_cases.preconditions: Active employee with valid publicDate and personalDate
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff
2. Body: valid publicDate/personalDate, duration: null
3. Check response and DB
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: duration has no @NotNull, no @Min/@Max
Null duration accepted — stored as null in DB
Ledger entries may have null duration
Downstream calculations may NPE on null duration
test_cases.notes: No validation annotations on duration field at all

test_cases.title: Create day-off — negative duration
test_cases.preconditions: Active employee with valid publicDate and personalDate
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff
2. Body: duration: -8
3. Check response and DB
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: No @Min on duration — negative value accepted
Negative duration creates inverted ledger entries
Credit becomes debit and vice versa
Corrupts vacation day balance calculation
test_cases.notes: No @Min/@Max annotations. Negative duration inverts credit/debit logic.

test_cases.title: Create day-off — zero duration
test_cases.preconditions: Active employee with valid dates
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff
2. Body: duration: 0
3. Check response and ledger
test_cases.expected_result:
Duration 0 accepted (no validation)
In ledger: duration=0 means day-off taken (debit)
But on credit side, duration=0 means no credit given
Net effect: takes a day off without earning credit for worked holiday
test_cases.notes: Boundary: zero duration has semantic meaning in ledger (debit marker)

test_cases.title: Create day-off — very long reason (no size limit)
test_cases.preconditions: Active employee with valid dates
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff
2. Body: reason: 'A' * 10000 (10K character string)
3. Check response
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: No @Size on reason field
Very long string accepted by DTO validation
DB column type determines actual limit
May cause truncation or DB error if column is VARCHAR(N)
test_cases.notes: No size limit on reason. DB column type is the only constraint.

test_cases.title: PATCH personalDate — happy path
test_cases.preconditions:
Existing day-off in NEW status owned by current user
Test data: SELECT id FROM employee_dayoff_request WHERE status='NEW' LIMIT 5
test_cases.expected_result:
personalDate updated successfully
Status remains unchanged
Only personalDate field is patchable (EmployeeDayOffPatchRequestDTO)
originalDate remains immutable
test_cases.notes: PATCH only allows personalDate change. originalDate is immutable.

test_cases.title: PATCH personalDate on APPROVED day-off
test_cases.preconditions: Existing APPROVED day-off owned by current user
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: EDIT permission is unconditional for owner
PATCH succeeds even on APPROVED request
Ledger entries NOT updated (still reference old personalDate)
Creates data inconsistency between request and ledger
test_cases.notes: EDIT has no status check. Owner can PATCH any status including terminal ones.

test_cases.title: PATCH personalDate on DELETED day-off
test_cases.preconditions:
Existing DELETED day-off owned by current user
Test data: SELECT id FROM employee_dayoff_request WHERE status='DELETED'
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: EDIT unconditional — succeeds on DELETED request
Can modify soft-deleted records
No business logic prevents editing terminal states
test_cases.notes: Bug: owner can PATCH even DELETED/DELETED_FROM_CALENDAR requests

test_cases.title: PATCH personalDate — date already used
test_cases.preconditions: Existing NEW day-off, another day-off using target personalDate
test_cases.notes: Same validator used for create and patch

test_cases.title: DELETE day-off — happy path (NEW status)
test_cases.preconditions: Existing day-off in NEW status owned by current user
test_cases.expected_result:
Status changed to DELETED
Soft-delete (record remains in DB)
No ledger changes (no ledger entries for NEW requests)
canBeCancelled was true (status != APPROVED)
test_cases.notes: DELETE permission requires canBeCancelled = true

test_cases.title: DELETE day-off — APPROVED with personalDate >= report period
test_cases.preconditions:
APPROVED day-off with personalDate in current or future report period
Test data: SELECT id, personal_date FROM employee_dayoff_request WHERE status='APPROVED' AND personal_date >= CURRENT_DATE
test_cases.expected_result:
canBeCancelled = true (personalDate >= report period start)
Status changed to DELETED
Ledger entries remain (not cleaned up on DELETE)
Vacation day balance should be recalculated
test_cases.notes: canBeCancelled: status != APPROVED || personalDate >= reportPeriod

test_cases.title: DELETE day-off — APPROVED with personalDate < report period (blocked)
test_cases.preconditions: APPROVED day-off with personalDate in past closed report period
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 403 or permission denied
canBeCancelled = false (APPROVED AND personalDate < report period)
DELETE permission not granted
Day-off in closed period cannot be cancelled
test_cases.notes: Prevents deletion of day-offs already accounted for in closed periods

test_cases.title: DELETE day-off — not owner (permission denied)
test_cases.preconditions: Day-off owned by another employee, authenticated as non-owner non-approver
test_cases.steps:
1. DELETE /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff/{id} (someone else's request)
2. Check error response
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 403
errorCode: exception.employee.dayOff.no.permission
DELETE permission only granted to owner
test_cases.notes: Only owner gets DELETE permission (via canBeCancelled check)

test_cases.title: Create day-off — day-off crossing vacation
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with existing approved vacation on the personalDate
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff
2. Body: personalDate = date within employee's existing vacation range
3. Check error response
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400
errorCode: exception.day.off.crossing.vacation
DayOffCrossingVacationException thrown
Cannot take day-off on a date covered by vacation
test_cases.notes: Cross-module validation: day-off vs vacation date overlap

test_cases.title: Upsert on creation — silent overwrite risk
test_cases.preconditions: Manipulate data so upsert condition is met (same employee + originalDate in ledger)
test_cases.steps:
1. Create day-off request for publicDate X
2. Verify ledger entry created
3. Manipulate: create condition where upsert finds existing record
4. Check if original record silently overwritten
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: Upsert on creation can silently overwrite records
If matching record exists in employee_dayoff table, it gets updated
No conflict detection or error raised
Data integrity risk: original record data lost
test_cases.notes: Upsert pattern: INSERT ON CONFLICT UPDATE — silently replaces data

test_cases.title: Approve NEW day-off — happy path with ledger creation
test_cases.preconditions:
Day-off in NEW status, authenticated as assigned approver
Test data: SELECT r.id, r.approver_login FROM employee_dayoff_request r WHERE r.status='NEW' LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff/approve/{id}
2. Verify response
3. DB: SELECT * FROM employee_dayoff WHERE employee_id=<emp_id> AND original_date=<date>
4. Check vacation balance change
test_cases.expected_result:
Status changed to APPROVED
Two ledger entries created:
  - Credit: lastApprovedDate slot, duration from calendar/norm
  - Debit: personalDate slot, duration=0 + reason from request
RecalculateVacationDaysHandler fires (diff=+1/-1)
UpdateMonthNormHandler recalculates norms for both months
test_cases.notes: Core approval flow. Verify both ledger entries and vacation balance.

test_cases.title: Approve REJECTED day-off — re-approval
test_cases.preconditions:
Day-off in REJECTED status, authenticated as approver
REJECTED is in approvableStatuses set
test_cases.expected_result:
Status REJECTED -> APPROVED
Approvable statuses: {NEW, REJECTED}
Ledger entries created fresh
Vacation balance recalculated
test_cases.notes: Re-approval after rejection. Both NEW and REJECTED are approvable.

test_cases.title: Approve already APPROVED day-off (invalid)
test_cases.preconditions: Day-off in APPROVED status
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff/approve/{id}
2. Check error response
test_cases.notes: APPROVED is not in approvable set — cannot double-approve

test_cases.title: Approve DELETED day-off (invalid)
test_cases.preconditions: Day-off in DELETED status
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff/approve/{id}
2. Check error response
test_cases.expected_result:
DELETED not in approvableStatuses
HTTP 403: exception.employee.dayOff.no.permission
Cannot approve a deleted request
test_cases.notes: Terminal status — no APPROVE permission granted

test_cases.title: Reject NEW day-off — happy path
test_cases.preconditions: Day-off in NEW status, authenticated as approver
test_cases.expected_result:
Status changed to REJECTED
No ledger entries created or modified
Rejectable statuses: {NEW, APPROVED}
Simple status change only
test_cases.notes: Reject is simpler than approve — no ledger operations

test_cases.title: Reject APPROVED day-off — personalDate >= report period
test_cases.preconditions:
APPROVED day-off with personalDate in current/future period
Authenticated as approver
test_cases.expected_result:
Status APPROVED -> REJECTED
REJECT permission granted: NEW/APPROVED AND personalDate >= report period
Ledger entries remain (not cleaned up)
Vacation balance should be recalculated to reverse the day-off effect
test_cases.notes: Rejecting APPROVED requires personalDate >= report period start

test_cases.title: Reject APPROVED day-off — personalDate < report period (blocked)
test_cases.preconditions: APPROVED day-off with personalDate in past closed report period
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff/reject/{id}
2. Check error response
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 403: exception.employee.dayOff.no.permission
REJECT not granted: APPROVED + personalDate < report period
Cannot reject day-off already in closed accounting period
test_cases.notes: Report period gate prevents rejecting accounted day-offs

test_cases.title: Reject REJECTED day-off (already rejected)
test_cases.preconditions: Day-off already in REJECTED status
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff/reject/{id}
2. Check error response
test_cases.notes: Rejectables are {NEW, APPROVED}, not REJECTED

test_cases.title: Reject as non-approver (permission denied)
test_cases.preconditions: Day-off in NEW status, authenticated as non-approver non-owner
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff/reject/{id}
2. Check error response
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 403: exception.employee.dayOff.no.permission
REJECT permission only for assigned approver
Owner cannot reject own request
test_cases.notes: Only the assigned approver can reject

test_cases.title: Change approver — happy path
test_cases.preconditions:
Day-off in NEW status, authenticated as current approver
Valid new approver login
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff/change-approver/{id}/{newApproverLogin}
2. Verify response
3. DB: SELECT approver_login FROM employee_dayoff_request WHERE id=<id>
4. Check optional approver cascade
test_cases.expected_result:
Approver changed to newApproverLogin
Old approver moved to optional approvers with ASKED status
New approver removed from optional approvers (if was optional)
Cascade: approver change triggers optional approver reshuffle
test_cases.notes: Approver change cascades to optional approvers

test_cases.title: Change approver on DELETED day-off (unconditional permission)
test_cases.preconditions: Day-off in DELETED status, authenticated as approver
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff/change-approver/{id}/{newLogin}
2. Check response
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: EDIT_APPROVER is unconditional — no status check
Change approver succeeds even on DELETED request
Can modify approver on DELETED_FROM_CALENDAR too
No business value — approver change on terminal status
test_cases.notes: Bug: EDIT_APPROVER has no status check, always granted to approver

test_cases.title: Change approver on DELETED_FROM_CALENDAR day-off
test_cases.preconditions: Day-off in DELETED_FROM_CALENDAR status
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff/change-approver/{id}/{newLogin}
2. Check response
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: Succeeds — EDIT_APPROVER unconditional
DELETED_FROM_CALENDAR is a terminal status
Approver change has no meaningful effect
Optional approver cascade still executes unnecessarily
test_cases.notes: Same bug as TC-DO-031 on different terminal status

test_cases.title: System rejection — bulk reject when calendar day removed
test_cases.preconditions:
Multiple NEW day-off requests for the same public holiday date
Admin removes that date from production calendar
test_cases.steps:
1. Find office with public holiday having NEW day-off requests
2. Admin: remove public holiday from calendar
3. System calls rejectedBySystem(officeId, date)
4. Check all affected requests
test_cases.expected_result:
All NEW requests with matching date set to REJECTED
System rejection (not manual reject)
Only NEW status affected — APPROVED untouched by rejectedBySystem
Different from deleteDayOffs which sets DELETED_FROM_CALENDAR
test_cases.notes: System rejection targets NEW only. deleteDayOffs targets NEW+APPROVED.

test_cases.title: Approve — ledger credit duration from calendar
test_cases.preconditions: NEW day-off for a date that exists in office calendar with known duration
test_cases.expected_result:
Credit ledger entry duration = calendar day duration (typically 8 or 7)
Duration sourced from: existing ledger OR calendar OR reporting-norm fallback
Fallback chain: ledger -> calendar -> norm (8h default)
test_cases.notes: Verify credit duration matches calendar. 8h=full day, 7h=half-working day.

test_cases.title: Approve — ledger debit entry verification
test_cases.preconditions: NEW day-off request with specific personalDate and reason
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff/approve/{id}
2. DB: SELECT * FROM employee_dayoff WHERE personal_date=<personalDate> AND duration=0
test_cases.expected_result:
Debit ledger entry:
  - duration = 0 (marks day-off taken)
  - reason copied from request
  - personal_date matches request personalDate
  - employee matches request employee
test_cases.notes: duration=0 is the debit marker. Reason propagated from request.

test_cases.title: Approve — non-atomic transaction risk
test_cases.preconditions: Simulate concurrent access during approve
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff/approve/{id}
2. Monitor: ledger write and status update are separate calls
3. If interruption between them: check for orphaned ledger entries
4. DB: compare employee_dayoff entries vs request status
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: No @Transactional on approve
Uses performInTransaction wrapper instead
Ledger entries written separately from status update
Failure between steps = ledger entries without APPROVED status
Risk: orphaned ledger records corrupt vacation balance
test_cases.notes: Non-atomic: changeDayOffDaysAfterApprove + changeDayOffStatus are separate

test_cases.title: Calendar removal — DELETED_FROM_CALENDAR for NEW+APPROVED requests
test_cases.preconditions:
Multiple day-off requests (NEW and APPROVED) for same originalDate
Admin removes that date from production calendar
test_cases.steps:
1. Identify date with both NEW and APPROVED requests
2. Admin: remove date from production calendar
3. System calls deleteDayOffs(date)
4. DB: SELECT status FROM employee_dayoff_request WHERE original_date=<date>
test_cases.expected_result:
All NEW and APPROVED requests: status -> DELETED_FROM_CALENDAR
Ledger entries physically deleted (not soft-delete)
Vacation days recalculated for each affected employee
EmployeeDayOffDeletedFromCalendarEvent published
test_cases.notes: Physical deletion of ledger entries. Different from system rejection.

test_cases.title: Calendar removal — REJECTED requests unaffected
test_cases.preconditions: REJECTED day-off request for a date being removed from calendar
test_cases.steps:
1. Ensure REJECTED request exists for target date
2. Remove date from calendar
3. Check REJECTED request status
test_cases.expected_result:
REJECTED request status unchanged
deleteDayOffs only targets NEW and APPROVED
REJECTED, DELETED, DELETED_FROM_CALENDAR are not affected
test_cases.notes: Only NEW/APPROVED targeted. Terminal statuses excluded.

test_cases.title: Calendar removal — ledger entries physically deleted
test_cases.preconditions: APPROVED day-off with ledger entries, calendar date being removed
test_cases.steps:
1. Verify ledger entries exist: SELECT * FROM employee_dayoff WHERE original_date=<date>
2. Remove date from calendar
3. SELECT * FROM employee_dayoff WHERE original_date=<date>
test_cases.expected_result:
Ledger entries physically deleted from employee_dayoff table
Not soft-delete — records are gone
This is different from request table (soft-delete via status)
Vacation balance recalculated after ledger deletion
test_cases.notes: Physical DELETE on ledger table vs status change on request table

test_cases.title: Office change — auto-delete all day-offs for year
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with multiple NEW/APPROVED day-offs changes office
AutoDeleteHelper.update(employeeId, year) triggered
test_cases.steps:
1. Note employee's day-offs for current year
2. Change employee's office
3. AutoDeleteHelper.update fires
4. Check all day-off requests and ledger entries
test_cases.expected_result:
All NEW and APPROVED day-offs: status -> DELETED_FROM_CALENDAR
ALL ledger entries for the year deleted (physical delete)
Employee notified of changes
Office calendar mismatch resolved
test_cases.notes: Cascading cleanup on office change. Affects entire year.

test_cases.title: Office change — PAGE_SIZE=100 hard limit
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with >100 day-off requests for a year (edge case)
Requires test data setup
test_cases.steps:
1. Create >100 day-off requests for one employee in one year
2. Trigger office change -> AutoDeleteHelper.update
3. Count remaining requests not set to DELETED_FROM_CALENDAR
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: PAGE_SIZE=100 hard limit
Only first 100 day-offs processed per page
Requests 101+ remain in NEW/APPROVED status
Partial cleanup — data integrity violation
No pagination loop to process all records
test_cases.notes: Hard limit. No loop. >100 records = incomplete cleanup.

test_cases.title: Office change — ledger entries for entire year deleted
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with ledger entries across multiple months, changes office
test_cases.steps:
1. DB: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employee_dayoff WHERE employee_id=<id> AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM original_date)=2026
2. Trigger office change
3. Repeat count query
test_cases.expected_result:
ALL ledger entries for the year physically deleted
Not limited to matched requests — entire year wiped
Includes entries for REJECTED/DELETED requests
Full recalculation triggered after deletion
test_cases.notes: Deletes ALL ledger entries for year, not just NEW/APPROVED ones

test_cases.title: CalendarUpdateHasDayOffConflictEvent — diff=0 (half-working day)
test_cases.preconditions: Calendar change creates half-working day that intersects existing day-off
test_cases.steps:
1. Admin: modify calendar day to half-working (diff=0 in event)
2. CalendarUpdateHasDayOffConflictEvent fires
3. Check notification sent to employee
test_cases.expected_result:
diff=0: half-working day notification sent
No vacation recalculation (diff=0 means no full-day change)
Informational notification only
Employee informed of calendar change affecting their day-off
test_cases.notes: diff=0 means calendar change is informational, not destructive

test_cases.title: CalendarUpdateHasDayOffConflictEvent — diff!=0 (full day change)
test_cases.preconditions: Calendar change creates full-day change intersecting existing day-off
test_cases.steps:
1. Admin: modify calendar day type (diff!=0)
2. CalendarUpdateHasDayOffConflictEvent fires
3. Check notification and vacation recalculation
test_cases.expected_result:
diff!=0: full day-off notification sent
Vacation recalculation triggered
Employee notified of material change
May trigger DELETED_FROM_CALENDAR if day is removed entirely
test_cases.notes: diff!=0 triggers both notification and recalculation

test_cases.title: Concurrent calendar removal and manual approval
test_cases.preconditions: Day-off in NEW status while admin removes calendar date simultaneously
test_cases.steps:
1. Start two concurrent operations:
   a. Admin removes calendar date (triggers deleteDayOffs)
   b. Approver approves the day-off
2. Check final state of request and ledger
test_cases.expected_result:
Race condition risk:
Scenario A wins: status=DELETED_FROM_CALENDAR, no ledger
Scenario B wins: status=APPROVED, ledger exists but calendar gone
No locking mechanism to prevent concurrent state mutation
Could result in APPROVED day-off for non-existent calendar date
test_cases.notes: Race between deleteDayOffs and approve. No optimistic locking.

test_cases.title: Add optional approver — happy path
test_cases.preconditions:
Existing day-off, authenticated as owner or approver
Valid colleague login who is not creator or main approver
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff-approvers
2. Body: {dayOffId: <id>, approverLogin: <colleague>}
3. Verify response
4. DB: SELECT * FROM employee_dayoff_approval WHERE request_id=<id>
test_cases.expected_result:
Optional approver added with status=ASKED
Record created in employee_dayoff_approval table
FYI notification sent to optional approver
test_cases.notes: Optional approvers are FYI — their approval/rejection is informational

test_cases.title: Add optional approver — constraint: day-off must exist
test_cases.preconditions: Non-existent dayOffId
test_cases.expected_result:
Error: day-off not found
Constraint 1 of 4: day-off must exist
HTTP 400 or 404
test_cases.notes: First of 4 create constraints validated

test_cases.title: Add optional approver — constraint: cannot add creator
test_cases.preconditions: Existing day-off, try to add the day-off creator as optional approver
test_cases.expected_result:
Error: cannot add creator as optional approver
Constraint 2 of 4: self-approval prevention
test_cases.notes: Cannot add request owner as their own optional approver

test_cases.title: Add optional approver — constraint: cannot add main approver
test_cases.preconditions: Existing day-off, try to add the main approver as optional approver
test_cases.expected_result:
Error: cannot add main approver as optional
Constraint 3 of 4: main approver already reviews
Prevents duplicate notification
test_cases.notes: Main approver is already the decision-maker — no optional role needed

test_cases.title: Add optional approver — constraint: cannot add duplicate
test_cases.preconditions: Existing day-off with existing optional approver, add same person again
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff-approvers (add person X)
2. POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff-approvers (add person X again)
3. Check error response on second call
test_cases.expected_result:
Error: duplicate optional approver
Constraint 4 of 4: uniqueness check
Same person cannot be optional approver twice
test_cases.notes: Prevents duplicate entries in approval table

test_cases.title: Add optional approver — access: only owner/approver/manager
test_cases.preconditions: Authenticated as unrelated employee (not owner, approver, or manager)
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff-approvers
2. Body: valid dayOffId, valid approverLogin
3. Check error response
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 403 or permission error
Only owner, main approver, or employee's manager can add optional approvers
Unrelated employees blocked
test_cases.notes: Access validation: owner OR approver OR manager of the employee

test_cases.title: Update optional approver status — approver approves
test_cases.preconditions: Existing optional approval in ASKED status, authenticated as the optional approver
test_cases.expected_result:
Status ASKED -> APPROVED
Only the optional approver themselves can update
FYI only — does not affect main request status
Informational approval recorded
test_cases.notes: Self-service: only the optional approver can change their own status

test_cases.title: Update optional approver status — approver rejects
test_cases.preconditions: Existing optional approval in ASKED status, authenticated as the optional approver
test_cases.expected_result:
Status ASKED -> REJECTED
FYI rejection — does not affect main request status
Main approver still decides independently
test_cases.notes: Optional rejection is informational only

test_cases.title: Update optional approver status — wrong person (not the approver)
test_cases.preconditions: Existing optional approval, authenticated as different employee
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 403 or error
Only the optional approver themselves can update their status
Owner cannot approve on behalf of optional approver
test_cases.notes: Self-service only. No delegation.

test_cases.title: Delete optional approver
test_cases.preconditions: Existing optional approval, authenticated as owner or approver
test_cases.expected_result:
Optional approver record deleted
Physical delete from employee_dayoff_approval table
No cascade effects on main request
test_cases.notes: Removal of optional approver is clean — no side effects

test_cases.title: Optional approver POST uses VACATIONS_VIEW (security gap)
test_cases.preconditions: User with VACATIONS_VIEW but NOT VACATIONS_CREATE
test_cases.steps:
1. Authenticate as user with VIEW-only permission
2. POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff-approvers
3. Check if write operation succeeds
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: POST controller uses @PreAuthorize VACATIONS_VIEW
Write operation protected only by VIEW permission
Any user with view access can add optional approvers
Should require VACATIONS_CREATE or VACATIONS_EDIT
test_cases.notes: Security gap: write operation behind read-only permission

test_cases.title: Optional approver PATCH uses VACATIONS_VIEW (security gap)
test_cases.preconditions: User with VACATIONS_VIEW but NOT VACATIONS_EDIT
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: PATCH controller uses @PreAuthorize VACATIONS_VIEW
Status update protected only by VIEW permission
However, service-level check (only optional approver can update) adds protection
Still: @PreAuthorize should match operation type
test_cases.notes: Defense in depth: @PreAuthorize too permissive, service check compensates

test_cases.title: Optional approver DELETE uses VACATIONS_VIEW (security gap)
test_cases.preconditions: User with VACATIONS_VIEW but NOT VACATIONS_DELETE
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: DELETE controller uses @PreAuthorize VACATIONS_VIEW
Deletion protected only by VIEW permission
Service-level access check (owner/approver/manager) provides some protection
But @PreAuthorize mismatch is a security smell
test_cases.notes: All 3 write operations on optional approver use VIEW permission

test_cases.title: Approver change cascade — old approver becomes optional
test_cases.preconditions: Day-off with optional approvers, change main approver
test_cases.steps:
1. Add optional approver X to day-off
2. PUT /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff/change-approver/{id}/{newLogin}
3. Check optional approvers list
4. DB: SELECT * FROM employee_dayoff_approval WHERE request_id=<id>
test_cases.expected_result:
Old main approver added as optional approver with ASKED status
New main approver removed from optional list (if was optional)
Existing optional approvers unchanged
Cascade maintains referential consistency
test_cases.notes: Cascade: old main->optional(ASKED), new main removed from optional

test_cases.title: Auto-sync optional approvers on creation
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with default optional approvers configured
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff
2. Body: valid day-off request (no explicit optional approvers)
3. Check optional approvers in response
4. DB: SELECT * FROM employee_dayoff_approval WHERE request_id=<new_id>
test_cases.expected_result:
Optional approvers auto-synced from employee's default settings
Defaults pulled from employee configuration
All synced approvers start with ASKED status
Same mechanism as vacation optional approver sync
test_cases.notes: Auto-sync happens during creation, not after

test_cases.title: Owner permissions on NEW request — full access
test_cases.preconditions: Day-off in NEW status, authenticated as owner
test_cases.steps:
1. GET day-off permissions for owned NEW request
2. Check permission set
test_cases.expected_result:
Owner gets: EDIT (unconditional) + DELETE (canBeCancelled=true for NEW)
EDIT: always granted to owner regardless of status
DELETE: granted because NEW != APPROVED
test_cases.notes: Owner gets EDIT always + DELETE when canBeCancelled

test_cases.title: Owner permissions on APPROVED request — EDIT but conditional DELETE
test_cases.preconditions: APPROVED day-off with personalDate in future, authenticated as owner
test_cases.steps:
1. GET permissions for owned APPROVED request
2. Check EDIT and DELETE permissions separately
test_cases.expected_result:
EDIT: granted (unconditional for owner)
DELETE: granted only if personalDate >= report period start
canBeCancelled = (status != APPROVED || personalDate >= reportPeriod)
test_cases.notes: EDIT unconditional, DELETE conditional on canBeCancelled

test_cases.title: Approver permissions on NEW request
test_cases.preconditions: Day-off in NEW status, authenticated as assigned approver
test_cases.steps:
1. GET permissions for day-off where current user is approver
2. Check permission set
test_cases.expected_result:
Approver gets: APPROVE + REJECT + EDIT_APPROVER
APPROVE: NEW in approvableStatuses {NEW, REJECTED}
REJECT: NEW in rejectableStatuses AND personalDate check
EDIT_APPROVER: always (unconditional)
test_cases.notes: Full approver permissions on NEW request

test_cases.title: Approver permissions on APPROVED request
test_cases.preconditions: APPROVED day-off, authenticated as approver
test_cases.steps:
1. GET permissions for APPROVED day-off
2. Check permission set
test_cases.expected_result:
APPROVE: not granted (APPROVED not in approvableStatuses)
REJECT: granted if personalDate >= report period
EDIT_APPROVER: granted (unconditional — design issue)
test_cases.notes: Approver can reject APPROVED but cannot re-approve

test_cases.title: readOnly user — no permissions
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with readOnly=true
Test data: SELECT login FROM employee WHERE read_only=true AND status='ACTIVE'
test_cases.steps:
1. Authenticate as readOnly employee
2. Attempt any write operation on day-off
3. Check permission response
test_cases.expected_result:
Empty permission set — no operations allowed
readOnly flag blocks ALL permissions
Non-ROLE_EMPLOYEE users also get empty permissions
Can still read/view day-offs (GET endpoints)
test_cases.notes: readOnly users and non-ROLE_EMPLOYEE: zero write permissions

test_cases.title: Non-ROLE_EMPLOYEE user — no permissions
test_cases.preconditions: User without ROLE_EMPLOYEE (e.g. external contractor)
test_cases.steps:
1. Authenticate as non-ROLE_EMPLOYEE user
2. Attempt to create day-off
3. Check response
test_cases.expected_result:
No permissions granted
Permission service checks ROLE_EMPLOYEE first
@PreAuthorize AUTHENTICATED_USER passes but permission service blocks
Two-layer access control: auth + permission
test_cases.notes: ROLE_EMPLOYEE is required for any day-off operation

test_cases.title: Shared VACATIONS_* authorities — cross-module permission leak
test_cases.preconditions: User with VACATIONS_VIEW/VACATIONS_APPROVE but no day-off specific permissions
test_cases.steps:
1. Check @PreAuthorize on day-off controllers — uses VACATIONS_* authorities
2. User with vacation-only permissions accesses day-off endpoints
3. Verify access is granted or denied
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: Day-off controllers use VACATIONS_* authorities
No separate DAYOFF_* permission set
User granted vacation permissions automatically gets day-off access
Cross-module permission leak — no granular control
test_cases.notes: All day-off endpoints use VACATIONS_VIEW/CREATE/EDIT/APPROVE/DELETE

test_cases.title: No CANCEL permission type (unlike vacation)
test_cases.preconditions: Compare vacation and day-off permission models
test_cases.steps:
1. Review vacation permission types: includes CANCEL
2. Review day-off permission types: no CANCEL, uses DELETE instead
3. Test cancellation flow via DELETE endpoint
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: No CANCEL permission type for day-offs
Vacation has separate CANCEL permission; day-off uses DELETE
Semantic difference: cancel vs delete
Inconsistent permission model across modules
test_cases.notes: Day-off DELETE = vacation CANCEL. Different semantics, same effect.

test_cases.title: EDIT_APPROVER on terminal status — approver can change approver on DELETED
test_cases.preconditions: DELETED day-off, authenticated as current approver
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff/change-approver/{id}/{newLogin}
2. Verify change succeeds despite DELETED status
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: EDIT_APPROVER is always granted to approver
No status check for EDIT_APPROVER permission
Approver change succeeds on: DELETED, DELETED_FROM_CALENDAR, REJECTED
Functionally meaningless but creates unnecessary DB mutations
test_cases.notes: Bug confirmed: no status check in EDIT_APPROVER permission grant

test_cases.title: EDIT on terminal status — owner can PATCH DELETED_FROM_CALENDAR
test_cases.preconditions: DELETED_FROM_CALENDAR day-off, authenticated as owner
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: EDIT always granted to owner
Can update personalDate on system-deleted request
No business value — request was deleted by calendar system
Creates data inconsistency
test_cases.notes: Owner EDIT has zero status restrictions. Terminal states editable.

test_cases.title: PublicDateValidator — null publicDate returns valid
test_cases.preconditions: Active employee
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff
2. Body: publicDate: null, other fields valid
3. Check if custom validator accepts null
test_cases.expected_result:
Null publicDate passes @EmployeeDayOffPublicDateExists validation
Custom validator returns true for null (standard pattern)
But missing @NotNull means null propagates to service layer
Possible NPE when service processes null publicDate
test_cases.notes: Validator null-pass pattern + missing @NotNull = null reaches service

test_cases.title: PersonalDateValidator — null personalDate returns valid
test_cases.preconditions: Active employee
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff
2. Body: personalDate: null, other fields valid
3. Check if custom validator accepts null
test_cases.expected_result:
Null personalDate passes @EmployeeDayOffPersonalDateExists validation
Same null-pass pattern as PublicDateValidator
Null personalDate propagates to service layer
test_cases.notes: Both custom validators return true for null input

test_cases.title: originalDate — no validation at all
test_cases.preconditions: Active employee
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff
2. Body: originalDate: '1900-01-01' (absurd date)
3. Check if request created with invalid originalDate
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: originalDate has NO validation annotations
No @NotNull, no custom validator, no date range check
Any date value accepted including far past/future
originalDate is supposed to be the public holiday date but is not validated against calendar
test_cases.notes: publicDate is validated but originalDate (which stores the actual date) is not

test_cases.title: Frontend validation — zero client-side checks
test_cases.preconditions: Access day-off creation form in browser
test_cases.steps:
1. Open day-off creation form in browser
2. Inspect form submission code (React components)
3. Submit empty form
4. Submit with invalid dates
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: ZERO frontend validation
No Yup schema, no imperative validator
Empty form submission sends to server with no client feedback
All validation is 100% server-side
UX: user sees no errors until server responds
test_cases.notes: All validation gaps: empty form, past dates, weekend, duplicate dates

test_cases.title: Frontend — empty form submission
test_cases.preconditions: Browser with day-off creation form open
test_cases.steps:
1. Open day-off creation form
2. Do not fill any fields
3. Click submit
4. Observe behavior
test_cases.expected_result:
No client-side error messages
Request sent to server with null/empty fields
Server returns 400 (or possibly 500 due to NPE)
User experience: delayed error feedback from server
test_cases.notes: Frontend sends invalid data, relies entirely on server error handling

test_cases.title: Frontend — past date selection allowed
test_cases.preconditions: Browser with day-off creation form open
test_cases.steps:
1. Select a publicDate in the past
2. Submit form
3. Observe behavior
test_cases.expected_result:
Frontend allows past date selection — no date picker restriction
Server rejects (date not in future calendar)
User sees error only after server response
Better UX: disable past dates in date picker
test_cases.notes: Date picker does not restrict to future dates

test_cases.title: Frontend — weekend/non-holiday date selectable
test_cases.preconditions: Browser with day-off creation form open
test_cases.steps:
1. Select a regular workday (not a public holiday) as publicDate
2. Submit form
3. Observe server rejection
test_cases.expected_result:
Frontend allows selecting any date including non-holidays
Server rejects: validation.PublicDateNotFoundInCalendar.message
Better UX: only show calendar public holidays in picker
test_cases.notes: Calendar data not used to constrain frontend date picker

test_cases.title: Error code: exception.employee.dayOff.no.permission
test_cases.preconditions: User without required permission attempting write operation
test_cases.steps:
1. Attempt approve/reject/delete without proper permission
2. Check error response format
3. Verify error code and HTTP status
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 403
errorCode: exception.employee.dayOff.no.permission
EmployeeDayOffSecurityException thrown
Error body includes exception class name (information disclosure)
test_cases.notes: Verify consistent error format across all permission failures

test_cases.title: Error code: exception.day.off.crossing.vacation
test_cases.preconditions: Day-off personalDate overlapping existing vacation
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff
2. Body: personalDate within approved vacation date range
3. Check error response
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400
errorCode: exception.day.off.crossing.vacation
DayOffCrossingVacationException
Cross-module validation between day-off and vacation
test_cases.notes: Vacation-dayoff overlap protection

test_cases.title: Error code: validation.EmployeeDayOffPublicDateExists.message
test_cases.preconditions: Duplicate publicDate for same employee
test_cases.steps:
1. Create day-off for publicDate X
2. Create another day-off for same publicDate X
3. Check error response
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400
errorCode: validation.EmployeeDayOffPublicDateExists.message
Duplicate check per employee (not global)
Different employees can have requests for same publicDate
test_cases.notes: Per-employee uniqueness, not system-wide

test_cases.title: Error code: validation.PublicDateNotFoundInCalendar.message
test_cases.preconditions: Date not in office calendar
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff
2. Body: publicDate = date not in any calendar
3. Check error response
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400
errorCode: validation.PublicDateNotFoundInCalendar.message
Validator checks: office calendar OR employee's day-off calendar
Both must fail for this error
test_cases.notes: Two-source calendar lookup: office calendar + employee dayoff calendar

test_cases.title: CANCELED status — dead code in enum
test_cases.preconditions: Review EmployeeDayOffStatusType enum
test_cases.steps:
1. Search codebase for CANCELED usage in day-off context
2. Attempt to set status to CANCELED via any means
3. Check if any code path assigns CANCELED
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: CANCELED exists in enum but is never assigned
No code path sets status to CANCELED
Dead code — status value unreachable
DB may have zero records with CANCELED status
test_cases.notes: Enum value exists, never used. Verify with DB: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employee_dayoff_request WHERE status='CANCELED'

test_cases.title: Duplicate setStatus call in entity conversion
test_cases.preconditions: Create or update day-off request and monitor conversion
test_cases.steps:
1. Trace code path through entity conversion
2. Check if setStatus called twice during conversion
3. Verify final status is correct despite duplicate call
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: Duplicate setStatus call in conversion code
Status set once by business logic, then overwritten by converter
Net effect: usually no bug (second call wins with same value)
Risk: if values differ, converter silently overrides business logic
test_cases.notes: Cosmetic issue unless converter and service disagree on status

test_cases.title: Misleading field name: last_approved_date
test_cases.preconditions: Examine day-off request data in DB and API response
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: last_approved_date actually stores the public holiday date
Name suggests last approval timestamp
Actually equals originalDate (the public holiday)
Confusing for developers and testers
test_cases.notes: Field name does not reflect actual content. Documentation needed.

test_cases.title: Null return from findApprovedByEmployeeAndDate
test_cases.preconditions: Query for employee+date combination with no approved day-off
test_cases.steps:
1. Call service method that uses findApprovedByEmployeeAndDate
2. Pass employee/date with no matching APPROVED record
3. Check return value handling
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: Method returns null instead of empty Optional/list
Callers must null-check
Missing null check = NPE
Should return Optional.empty() or Collections.emptyList()
test_cases.notes: Repository returns null for no results. Service layer may NPE.

test_cases.title: Hardcoded production URL in notifications
test_cases.preconditions: Trigger notification for day-off event, inspect email content
test_cases.steps:
1. Create/approve day-off to trigger notification
2. Check email content via email MCP
3. Inspect URLs in notification body
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: Notification contains hardcoded production URL
All environments send emails with production URL
Testing env emails link to production app
User clicking link goes to wrong environment
test_cases.notes: Notifications have production URL regardless of environment

test_cases.title: Random.nextLong() for synthetic IDs in findSoonDayOffs
test_cases.preconditions: Call endpoint that uses findSoonDayOffs (upcoming day-offs list)
test_cases.steps:
1. GET endpoint that returns upcoming day-offs
2. Call multiple times
3. Check if synthetic IDs change between calls
test_cases.expected_result:
DESIGN ISSUE: Random.nextLong() generates synthetic IDs
IDs are non-deterministic across calls
Cannot use ID for caching, deduplication, or reference
Risk: negative IDs possible (Random.nextLong range includes negatives)
test_cases.notes: Non-deterministic IDs break caching and client-side deduplication

test_cases.title: Search type MY — own requests with calendar and ledger merge
test_cases.preconditions: Authenticated employee with day-off requests
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff?searchType=MY
2. Verify response includes own requests
3. Check calendar and ledger data merged into response
test_cases.expected_result:
Returns employee's own day-off requests
Merged with calendar day data and ledger entries
Includes all statuses (NEW, APPROVED, REJECTED, etc.)
Most common search type for regular employees
test_cases.notes: MY search is the primary user-facing view

test_cases.title: Search type ALL — admin view
test_cases.preconditions: Authenticated as admin/HR with VACATIONS_VIEW permission
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff?searchType=ALL
2. Verify response includes all employees' requests
3. Check pagination
test_cases.expected_result:
Returns all day-off requests across all employees
Admin-level view
Paginated response
Requires VACATIONS_VIEW permission
test_cases.notes: Admin search for cross-organization visibility

test_cases.title: Search type APPROVER — requests pending approval
test_cases.preconditions: Authenticated as manager with day-offs pending their approval
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff?searchType=APPROVER
2. Verify response includes requests where current user is approver
test_cases.expected_result:
Returns day-off requests where current user is assigned approver
Typically filtered to actionable statuses (NEW, APPROVED for reject)
Used by approver dashboard
test_cases.notes: Approver view for managing pending requests

test_cases.title: Search type OPTIONAL_APPROVER
test_cases.preconditions: Authenticated as optional approver for some day-offs
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff?searchType=OPTIONAL_APPROVER
2. Verify response includes requests where user is optional approver
test_cases.expected_result:
Returns requests where user is an optional (FYI) approver
Includes all approval statuses (ASKED, APPROVED, REJECTED)
Used for informational review
test_cases.notes: Optional approver view — informational, not action-required

test_cases.title: Search type DELEGATED_TO_ME
test_cases.preconditions: Authenticated as delegate for another manager
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff?searchType=DELEGATED_TO_ME
2. Verify response includes delegated requests
test_cases.expected_result:
Returns day-off requests delegated to current user
Delegation: acting on behalf of absent manager
Includes requests where delegation chain leads to current user
test_cases.notes: Delegation view for substitute approvers

test_cases.title: GET list endpoint — approved day-offs only
test_cases.preconditions: Authenticated employee
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff/list
2. Check response contains only APPROVED requests
test_cases.expected_result:
Returns only APPROVED day-off entries
Different from main GET / which returns all statuses
Used for display in calendar/summary views
@PreAuthorize: AUTHENTICATED_USER || VACATIONS_VIEW
test_cases.notes: Separate endpoint for approved-only list

test_cases.title: Approve flow — RecalculateVacationDaysHandler integration
test_cases.preconditions: NEW day-off, approve and verify vacation balance impact
test_cases.steps:
1. GET current vacation days balance for employee
2. PUT /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff/approve/{id}
3. GET vacation days balance again
4. Compare before/after
test_cases.expected_result:
RecalculateVacationDaysHandler fires after approve
Vacation day balance adjusts by diff (+1 credit, -1 debit)
Net effect depends on duration values
Cross-module: day-off approval affects vacation balance
test_cases.notes: Day-off approve -> vacation recalculation. Critical cross-module test.

test_cases.title: Approve flow — UpdateMonthNormHandler integration
test_cases.preconditions: NEW day-off with personalDate in different month than originalDate
test_cases.steps:
1. Approve day-off where months differ
2. Check monthly norm recalculation for both months
3. DB: SELECT * FROM employee_month_norm WHERE employee_id=<id>
test_cases.expected_result:
UpdateMonthNormHandler recalculates norms for both months:
  - Month of lastApprovedDate (credit month)
  - Month of personalDate (debit month)
Both months' norms adjusted
Important when months cross reporting periods
test_cases.notes: Two-month norm update when credit and debit are in different months

test_cases.title: Search type MY_DEPARTMENT
test_cases.preconditions: Authenticated as department manager
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff?searchType=MY_DEPARTMENT
2. Verify response includes department members' requests
test_cases.expected_result:
Returns day-off requests from all employees in user's department
