Department scope — broader than APPROVER (which is direct reports only)
Used by department managers for team overview
test_cases.notes: Department-level visibility for managers

test_cases.title: Search type RELATED
test_cases.preconditions: Authenticated employee on same project/team as others
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff?searchType=RELATED
2. Verify response includes related employees' requests
test_cases.expected_result:
Returns day-off requests from related employees
Related = same project, same team, or organizational connection
Used for coordination and awareness
test_cases.notes: Relationship-based search for team coordination

test_cases.title: Create day-off — originalDate vs publicDate mismatch
test_cases.preconditions: Active employee, send different originalDate and publicDate
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff
2. Body: originalDate: '2026-05-01', publicDate: '2026-05-09'
3. Check which date is validated and stored
test_cases.expected_result:
publicDate validated against calendar (custom validator)
originalDate has NO validation — stored as-is
Mismatch creates inconsistent record
originalDate should equal publicDate but nothing enforces this
test_cases.notes: Two date fields that should match but are validated independently

test_cases.title: Approve — ledger fallback chain for credit duration
test_cases.preconditions: NEW day-off where no existing ledger entry and no calendar entry for date
test_cases.steps:
1. Set up: ensure no existing ledger and no calendar match
2. Approve the day-off
3. Check credit ledger entry duration value
test_cases.expected_result:
Fallback chain for credit duration:
1. Existing ledger entry for employee+date -> use its duration
2. Calendar day for date -> use calendar duration
3. Reporting norm fallback -> use 8h default
Verify correct fallback is used when earlier sources are empty
test_cases.notes: Three-tier fallback: ledger -> calendar -> norm(8h)

test_cases.title: Half-day duration (7h) handling
test_cases.preconditions: Day-off for a half-working day (calendar duration=7h)
test_cases.steps:
1. Identify half-working day in calendar (duration=7)
2. Create day-off with that date as publicDate
3. Approve and check ledger entries
test_cases.expected_result:
Credit ledger entry: duration=7 (half-day)
Debit ledger entry: duration=0 (standard debit marker)
Vacation balance impact differs from full-day
DB: 27 half-day entries found in timemachine env
test_cases.notes: Half-day (7h) vs full-day (8h) credit handling

test_cases.title: Approve — vacation recalculation diff calculation
test_cases.preconditions: Approve day-off and verify exact diff passed to vacation recalculation
test_cases.steps:
1. Approve day-off
2. Trace RecalculateVacationDaysHandler parameters
3. Verify diff value (+1 for credit, -1 for debit)
test_cases.expected_result:
Diff calculation: credit creates +1, debit creates -1
Net diff determines vacation balance change
Correct diff is critical for accurate vacation tracking
Incorrect diff leads to vacation balance drift over time
test_cases.notes: Diff accuracy is critical for vacation balance integrity

test_cases.title: Bulk operations — data patterns from timemachine env
test_cases.preconditions: Use timemachine env data for verification
test_cases.steps:
1. DB: SELECT status, COUNT(*) FROM employee_dayoff_request GROUP BY status
2. DB: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employee_dayoff
3. DB: SELECT status, COUNT(*) FROM employee_dayoff_approval GROUP BY status
4. Compare with expected distributions
test_cases.expected_result:
Expected distribution (timemachine):
  Requests: 89.6% APPROVED, 6.9% DELETED, 2.5% DELETED_FROM_CALENDAR, 0.6% NEW, 0.4% REJECTED
  Ledger: 2,853 credit (8h), 2,454 debit (0h), 27 half-day (7h)
  Approvals: 62% ASKED, 38% APPROVED, 1 REJECTED
  Credit > debit: ~399 unused credits
test_cases.notes: Baseline data pattern verification against known distribution

test_cases.title: Pagination on GET all endpoint
test_cases.preconditions: Authenticated user with many day-off requests
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff?page=0&size=10
2. GET /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff?page=1&size=10
3. Verify pagination metadata and content
test_cases.expected_result:
Paginated response with totalElements, totalPages
Page content matches requested size
No duplicates across pages
Sort order consistent across pages
test_cases.notes: Standard Spring pagination. Verify no data loss between pages.

test_cases.title: GET day-off by non-existent ID
test_cases.preconditions: Non-existent day-off ID
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff with filter for id=999999999
2. Check response
test_cases.expected_result:
Empty result set or 404
No NPE or 500 error
Graceful handling of non-existent resources
test_cases.notes: Basic robustness: non-existent resource handling

test_cases.title: Approve day-off — notification to optional approvers
test_cases.preconditions: Day-off with optional approvers in ASKED status, approve the request
test_cases.steps:
1. Add optional approvers to day-off
2. Approve the day-off as main approver
3. Check email notifications sent
4. Check optional approver statuses remain ASKED
test_cases.expected_result:
Main request approved
Optional approvers notified of approval
Optional approver statuses NOT changed (remain ASKED)
FYI notification only — optional approvers can still respond
test_cases.notes: Optional approvers get notification but their status is independent

test_cases.title: Create day-off for employee in different office (cross-office calendar)
test_cases.preconditions: Employee whose office calendar does NOT have the specified publicDate
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff
2. Body: publicDate from a different office's calendar
3. Check error response
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400: validation.PublicDateNotFoundInCalendar.message
Validator checks employee's office calendar specifically
Public holidays are office-specific
Cross-office dates not interchangeable
test_cases.notes: Calendar validation is office-scoped. Different offices = different holidays.

test_cases.title: Concurrent approve and delete on same request
test_cases.preconditions: Day-off in NEW status, two concurrent requests
test_cases.steps:
1. Simultaneously:
   a. PUT /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff/approve/{id} (as approver)
   b. DELETE /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff/{id} (as owner)
2. Check final state
test_cases.expected_result:
Race condition: no optimistic locking
Scenario 1: approve wins -> APPROVED + ledger, then delete fails (if canBeCancelled)
Scenario 2: delete wins -> DELETED, then approve fails (DELETED not approvable)
Risk: both succeed -> DELETED with orphaned ledger entries
test_cases.notes: No optimistic locking or version field to prevent concurrent mutations

test_cases.title: Approve day-off — verify event publishing
test_cases.preconditions: NEW day-off, approve and check event chain
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff/approve/{id}
2. Check event: EmployeeDayOffCreatedEvent (on create)
3. Verify handlers: RecalculateVacationDaysHandler, UpdateMonthNormHandler
4. Verify downstream effects
test_cases.expected_result:
Events published:
  - Status change triggers downstream handlers
  - RecalculateVacationDaysHandler: vacation balance update
  - UpdateMonthNormHandler: monthly norm update
  - Notification events: email to employee
test_cases.notes: Verify complete event chain from approve through all handlers

test_cases.title: Search assignments by employee and date range

test_cases.steps: 1. GET /v1/assignments?startDate=2026-03-03&endDate=2026-03-03&employeeLogin=diborisov
test_cases.expected_result: 200 OK. Response contains employees[] with single entry. assignments[] includes generated (with id, position, nextAssignmentId) and non-generated (id=null) assignments.
test_cases.notes: Verified on timemachine: 257 assignments for this employee

test_cases.expected_result: 400 Bad Request. Error: 'startDate must not be null'
test_cases.notes: Both startDate and endDate are @NotNull

test_cases.title: Search assignments — missing required endDate

test_cases.steps: 1. GET /v1/assignments?startDate=2026-03-03&employeeLogin=diborisov
test_cases.expected_result: 400 Bad Request. Error: 'endDate must not be null'

test_cases.title: Search assignments — response includes permissions

Internal

'
