test_cases.title: Generate assignments — overwrites existing ordering
test_cases.preconditions: Employee has manually reordered assignments via drag-drop on target date
test_cases.steps:
1. Note current assignment order (custom DnD order)
2. POST /v1/assignments/generate for same employee+date
3. GET assignments for same employee+date
test_cases.expected_result: Step 3: All positions and nextAssignmentId values have been overwritten. Manual ordering is destroyed.
test_cases.notes: KNOWN ISSUE: generate() resets all ordering. This is by design but causes UX confusion.

test_cases.title: Generate assignments — with projectId filter
test_cases.preconditions: Employee works on multiple projects
test_cases.steps: 1. POST /v1/assignments/generate with body: {employeeLogin: '{login}', date: '2026-03-20', projectId: {id}}
test_cases.expected_result: 200 OK. Only assignments for the specified project are generated. Other projects' assignments remain unchanged.

test_cases.title: Generate assignments — excludes closed assignments
test_cases.preconditions: Employee has some closed assignments
test_cases.steps:
1. Close assignment X via PATCH (closed=true)
2. POST /v1/assignments/generate for next day
test_cases.expected_result: 200 OK. Closed assignment X is NOT included in generated assignments.

test_cases.title: Generate assignments — missing required employeeLogin
test_cases.preconditions: Valid API token
test_cases.steps: 1. POST /v1/assignments/generate with body: {date: '2026-03-20'}
test_cases.expected_result: 400 Bad Request. Validation error: employeeLogin must not be null.

test_cases.title: Generate assignments — missing required date
test_cases.preconditions: Valid API token
test_cases.steps: 1. POST /v1/assignments/generate with body: {employeeLogin: '{login}'}
test_cases.expected_result: 400 Bad Request. Validation error: date must not be null.

test_cases.title: Generate assignments — WebSocket GENERATE event emitted
test_cases.preconditions: WebSocket client subscribed to /topic/employees/{login}/assignments/{period}
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /v1/assignments/generate
2. Observe WebSocket events
test_cases.expected_result: GENERATE event received on the assignments channel with updated assignment data.
test_cases.notes: Verify via WebSocket subscription or browser dev tools

test_cases.title: Generate assignments — idempotent on re-call (no duplicates)
test_cases.preconditions: Assignments already generated for employee+date
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /v1/assignments/generate (first call)
2. POST /v1/assignments/generate (second call, same params)
3. Compare assignment counts
test_cases.expected_result: No duplicate assignments created. Second call returns same set (may re-order).
test_cases.notes: generate() should skip existing assignments

test_cases.title: Move assignment via PATCH with nextAssignmentId
test_cases.preconditions: Employee has 3+ generated assignments A, B, C (positions 0, 1, 2)
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH /v1/assignments/{A.id} with body: {nextAssignmentId: {C.id}}
2. GET assignments for same date
test_cases.expected_result: Assignment A moved to position before C. New order: B, A, C. Both position and nextAssignmentId updated consistently.
test_cases.notes: Drag-drop core mechanism

test_cases.title: Move assignment to end of list (nextAssignmentId=0)
test_cases.preconditions: Employee has 3+ assignments
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH /v1/assignments/{A.id} with body: {nextAssignmentId: 0}
2. GET assignments
test_cases.expected_result: Assignment A moved to last position. nextAssignmentId of A becomes null (end of chain).
test_cases.notes: nextAssignmentId=0 means 'move to bottom'

test_cases.title: Move propagates to future dates
test_cases.preconditions: Employee has same assignments on day D and D+1
test_cases.steps:
1. Reorder assignment on day D via PATCH with nextAssignmentId
2. GET assignments for day D+1
test_cases.expected_result: Day D+1 assignments reflect the same ordering change as day D (propagated by moveFutureAssignmentsAccordingly).
test_cases.notes: Propagation to future dates is critical for consistency

test_cases.title: Move propagation — target task absent on future date
test_cases.preconditions: Assignment A exists on D and D+1, but assignment B only on D (not D+1)
test_cases.steps:
1. Move A to position before B on day D
2. GET assignments for day D+1
test_cases.expected_result: Day D+1 remains unchanged (silent return when target task doesn't exist on future date).
test_cases.notes: No error handling — silent skip

test_cases.title: Dual ordering consistency — position matches linked-list
test_cases.preconditions: Employee has generated assignments
test_cases.steps:
1. GET assignments for a date
2. Verify: for each assignment, position N should have nextAssignmentId pointing to assignment at position N+1
3. Last assignment should have nextAssignmentId=null
test_cases.expected_result: Position values and nextAssignmentId chain are fully consistent.
test_cases.notes: DB query: SELECT id, position, next_assignment FROM task_assignment WHERE date=D AND assignee=E ORDER BY position

test_cases.title: NULL position assignments float to top
test_cases.preconditions: Database has legacy assignments with position=NULL (pre-migration V2_1_2_202101191720)
test_cases.steps:
1. Insert test assignment with position=NULL directly in DB
2. GET assignments for same employee+date
test_cases.expected_result: NULL-position assignments appear at the top of the list, sorted by task name, before all positioned assignments.
test_cases.notes: Legacy data handling — no backfill migration exists

test_cases.title: Multiple new assignments — all get position=0
test_cases.preconditions: Employee has no assignments for target date
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /v1/assignments — create assignment A (position=0)
2. POST /v1/assignments — create assignment B (position=0)
3. GET assignments for date
test_cases.expected_result: Both A and B have position=0. Ordering relies on secondary sort by taskName until move() or generate() is called.
test_cases.notes: Design debt: ambiguous ordering for new assignments

test_cases.title: DB ordering: ORDER BY position, task.name
test_cases.preconditions: Assignments exist with various positions
test_cases.steps:
1. Query DB: SELECT ta.id, ta.position, t.name FROM task_assignment ta JOIN task t ON t.id = ta.task WHERE ta.date=D ORDER BY ta.position, t.name
2. Compare with API response order
test_cases.expected_result: DB order matches API response order (repository uses ORDER BY ta.position, t.name).
test_cases.notes: Linked-list ignored for retrieval, only position+name used

test_cases.title: Sorting: alphabetical within same position
test_cases.preconditions: Multiple assignments with same position value
test_cases.steps:
1. Create assignments for tasks 'Zebra' and 'Alpha' both with position=0
2. GET assignments
test_cases.expected_result: Assignments sorted alphabetically by task name within same position group.

test_cases.title: List close-tags for project
test_cases.preconditions: Project {projectId} exists. User authenticated.
test_cases.steps: 1. GET /v1/projects/{projectId}/close-tags
test_cases.expected_result: 200 OK. Array of PlannerCloseTagDTO objects [{id, projectId, tag}, ...]
test_cases.notes: Any authenticated user can list tags

test_cases.title: Create close-tag as project manager
test_cases.preconditions: User is project manager for project {projectId}
test_cases.steps: 1. POST /v1/projects/{projectId}/close-tags with body: {tag: '[closed]'}
test_cases.expected_result: 200 OK. Returns PlannerCloseTagDTO with generated id, projectId, and tag '[closed]'.

test_cases.title: Create close-tag — idempotent (duplicate returns existing)
test_cases.preconditions: Tag '[closed]' already exists for project
test_cases.steps: 1. POST /v1/projects/{projectId}/close-tags with body: {tag: '[closed]'} (same tag again)
test_cases.expected_result: 200 OK. Returns the existing tag with same id (get-or-create semantics, race-condition safe via REQUIRES_NEW transaction).
test_cases.notes: Idempotent create prevents race condition duplicates

test_cases.title: Create close-tag — blank tag rejected
test_cases.preconditions: User is project manager
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /v1/projects/{projectId}/close-tags with body: {tag: ''}
2. POST with body: {tag: '   '}
test_cases.expected_result: Both return 400 Bad Request. Error: 'Tag must not be blank' (@NotBlank validation).

test_cases.title: Create close-tag — max length 255 characters
test_cases.preconditions: User is project manager
test_cases.steps:
1. POST with tag of exactly 255 characters
2. POST with tag of 256 characters
test_cases.expected_result:
Step 1: 200 OK (tag created).
Step 2: 400 Bad Request or DB error (column length = 255).
test_cases.notes: DISCREPANCY: requirements say max 200 chars but DB column is varchar(255)

test_cases.title: Update close-tag via PATCH
test_cases.preconditions: Tag {tagId} exists for project
test_cases.steps: 1. PATCH /v1/projects/{projectId}/close-tags/{tagId} with body: {tag: '[done]'}
test_cases.expected_result: 200 OK. Tag string updated to '[done]'. Returns updated PlannerCloseTagDTO.

test_cases.title: Update close-tag — duplicate tag returns 400
test_cases.preconditions: Two tags exist: '[closed]' and '[done]' for same project
test_cases.steps: 1. PATCH tag '[done]' to update its string to '[closed]'
test_cases.expected_result: 400 Bad Request (ValidationException). Duplicate tag within project not allowed.
test_cases.notes: Unlike create (idempotent), update throws on duplicate

test_cases.title: Update close-tag — no-op when same value
test_cases.preconditions: Tag '[closed]' exists
test_cases.steps: 1. PATCH tag with body: {tag: '[closed]'} (same value)
test_cases.expected_result: 200 OK. Returns immediately without DB write (no-op optimization).

test_cases.title: Delete close-tag
test_cases.preconditions: Tag {tagId} exists for project {projectId}. User is admin.
test_cases.steps:
1. DELETE /v1/projects/{projectId}/close-tags/{tagId}
2. GET /v1/projects/{projectId}/close-tags
test_cases.expected_result:
Step 1: 200 OK.
Step 2: deleted tag no longer in list.

test_cases.title: Close-tag permission — plain employee cannot create/update/delete
test_cases.preconditions: User is a plain employee (not admin, PM, senior manager, or owner)
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /v1/projects/{projectId}/close-tags (should work)
2. POST /v1/projects/{projectId}/close-tags with body: {tag: '[test]'}
3. PATCH existing tag
4. DELETE existing tag
test_cases.expected_result:
Step 1: 200 OK (list is allowed).
Steps 2-4: 403 Forbidden (permission denied by PlannerCloseTagPermissionService).
test_cases.notes: Authorized: admin, PM, senior manager, owner. Denied: everyone else.

test_cases.title: Close-tag permission — senior manager can manage tags
test_cases.preconditions: User is senior manager of the project
test_cases.steps: 1. POST /v1/projects/{projectId}/close-tags with body: {tag: '[sm-tag]'}
test_cases.expected_result: 200 OK. Senior manager gets all 3 permissions (CREATE+EDIT+DELETE).
test_cases.notes: All-or-nothing permission grant

test_cases.title: Close-tag permission — project owner can manage tags
test_cases.preconditions: User is project owner
test_cases.steps: 1. POST /v1/projects/{projectId}/close-tags with body: {tag: '[owner-tag]'}
test_cases.expected_result: 200 OK. Owner gets CREATE+EDIT+DELETE permissions.

test_cases.title: Close-tag — cross-project manipulation rejected
test_cases.preconditions: Tag belongs to project A. User tries to delete via project B endpoint.
test_cases.steps: 1. DELETE /v1/projects/{projectB_id}/close-tags/{tagFromProjectA_id}
test_cases.expected_result: 404 Not Found or 400 Bad Request. Cross-project validation prevents manipulation.
test_cases.notes: Cross-project validation in delete/update

test_cases.title: Close-tag — non-existent project returns 404
test_cases.preconditions: Valid API token
test_cases.steps: 1. GET /v1/projects/999999/close-tags
test_cases.expected_result: 404 Not Found. @ProjectIdExists validation on path variable.

test_cases.title: Close-by-tag — auto-close during tracker sync
test_cases.preconditions: Project has close-tag '[closed]'. Task has ticket with '[closed]' in name/info.
test_cases.steps:
1. Trigger tracker sync (Load from Tracker) for the project
2. GET assignments for employee+date
test_cases.expected_result: Assignments matching the tag are auto-set to closed=true. Closed assignments shown only if they have reported hours.
test_cases.notes: 267-line service + 760-line integration test

test_cases.title: Close-by-tag triggered by Refresh button
test_cases.preconditions: Project with close tag 'Done' configured. Assignment exists with ticket_info containing 'Done'. User is project manager.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to Planner for the project
2. Click 'Refresh' button
3. Observe assignment status
test_cases.expected_result: Assignment is auto-closed (closed=true in DB). If no report on that date, assignment disappears from planner view. WebSocket PATCH event sent.
test_cases.notes: CloseByTagService.apply() called from TaskRefreshServiceImpl. Verify both Refresh and Load-from-tracker trigger close-by-tag.

test_cases.title: Close-by-tag — case-insensitive matching
test_cases.preconditions: Project has close tag 'closed'. Assignments exist with ticket_info: '[CLOSED]', 'Closed', 'closed', 'ALREADY-CLOSED'.
test_cases.steps:
1. Create close tag 'closed' for project
2. Create assignments with varied-case ticket_info
3. Trigger Refresh
4. Verify all matched assignments are closed
test_cases.expected_result: All four assignments are closed. StringUtils.containsIgnoreCase matches regardless of case.
test_cases.notes: Core matching: StringUtils.containsIgnoreCase(ticketInfo, tag). Code: CloseByTagServiceImpl line 192.

test_cases.title: Close-by-tag — substring matching (potential false positives)
test_cases.preconditions: Project has close tag 'fix'. Assignments exist with ticket_info: 'fix', 'fixed', 'prefix', 'hotfix-deployed', 'unrelated'.
test_cases.steps:
1. Create close tag 'fix' for project
2. Create assignments with ticket_info containing/not containing 'fix'
3. Trigger Refresh
4. Verify which assignments are closed
test_cases.expected_result: Assignments with 'fix', 'fixed', 'prefix', 'hotfix-deployed' are ALL closed (substring match). 'unrelated' is NOT closed. This is by-design substring matching — potential for false positives.
test_cases.notes: Known design concern: containsIgnoreCase is substring-based, not exact/word-boundary. Tag 'fix' matches 'prefix'.

test_cases.title: Close-by-tag — closed assignment with report stays visible
test_cases.preconditions: Project has close tag 'Done'. Assignment exists with ticket_info 'Done' AND has a time report on the same date.
test_cases.steps:
1. Create assignment, report hours for it on same date
2. Add close tag 'Done'
3. Trigger Refresh
4. Verify assignment status in DB and UI
test_cases.expected_result: Assignment is marked closed=true in DB. BUT no WebSocket event is sent — the row stays visible in planner UI because the user has reported hours on it. GET /assignments?closed=false will NOT return it, but it appears via the report relationship.
test_cases.notes: Key behavior: applyExistingAssignment() checks hasReportOnDate. If true, closes in DB but suppresses WebSocket event.

test_cases.title: Close-by-tag — generated (virtual) assignment closure
test_cases.preconditions: Project has close tag 'Done'. Employee has a task with ticket_info 'Done' but NO assignment record in DB for tomorrow's date (assignment would be generated/virtual).
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to Planner for tomorrow
2. Trigger Refresh (assignments generated on-the-fly)
3. Verify DB state
test_cases.expected_result: A NEW real assignment record is created in DB with closed=true via createForCloseByTag(). WebSocket GENERATE event with nested ADD event is published (unless report exists). Assignment doesn't appear in planner for future dates.
test_cases.notes: Path 2: applyGeneratedAssignment creates real DB record. On creation failure, error is silently swallowed (debug log only).

test_cases.title: Close-by-tag — multiple tags configured, first match triggers closure
test_cases.preconditions: Project has close tags: 'Done', 'Resolved', 'Wontfix'. Assignment has ticket_info '[Done] [Resolved]'.
test_cases.steps:
1. Create 3 close tags for project
2. Create assignment with ticket_info matching multiple tags
3. Trigger Refresh
4. Verify assignment is closed once
test_cases.expected_result: Assignment is closed exactly once. The matching iterates through tags — first match triggers closure, subsequent tags don't cause duplicate operations.
test_cases.notes: Verify no duplicate WebSocket events or DB operations when ticket_info matches multiple configured tags.

test_cases.title: Close-by-tag — no tags configured for project
test_cases.preconditions: Project has NO close tags configured. Assignments exist with various ticket_info values.
test_cases.steps:
1. Ensure project has no close tags
2. Trigger Refresh for the project
3. Verify all assignments remain open
test_cases.expected_result: All assignments remain unchanged (open). CloseByTagServiceImpl skips projects with empty tag lists entirely.
test_cases.notes: Guard check in collectAssignmentsToClose: empty tag list → skip project.

test_cases.title: Close-tag cascade deletion on project removal
test_cases.preconditions: Project has 3 close tags configured. Admin deletes the project.
test_cases.steps:
1. Create project with 3 close tags
2. Delete the project via admin panel
3. Query planner_close_tag table for the project_id
test_cases.expected_result: All 3 close tags are automatically deleted via ON DELETE CASCADE foreign key. No orphaned records in planner_close_tag table.
test_cases.notes: FK constraint: fk_planner_close_tag_project REFERENCES project(id) ON DELETE CASCADE.

test_cases.title: UI — Project settings modal with tabs
test_cases.preconditions: User is project manager. Navigating to Planner.
test_cases.steps:
1. Click the project settings icon (tooltip: 'Project settings' / 'Настройки проекта')
2. Verify modal opens with 2 tabs: 'Project members' and 'Tasks closing'
3. Click 'Tasks closing' tab
4. Verify explanatory text, add form, and tag list table are present
5. Switch language to RU, verify tab names are 'Участники проекта' / 'Закрытие задач'
test_cases.expected_result: Modal opens with correct tab structure. 'Tasks closing' tab shows: explanatory text about auto-closing, text input with 'Add' button, empty tag list table. All labels are translated in both EN and RU.
test_cases.notes: Frontend MR !5301. Modal renamed from 'Project employees'. Tabs: 'project-members' and 'task-closing'.

test_cases.title: UI — Inline tag editing (Enter/Escape)
test_cases.preconditions: User is project manager. Tag 'Done' exists in project's close tags.
test_cases.steps:
1. Click on tag text in the tag list
2. Verify field becomes editable
3. Type new value 'Completed'
4. Press Enter
5. Verify tag updated to 'Completed'
6. Click another tag, type new value, press Escape
7. Verify original value preserved
test_cases.expected_result: Clicking tag enables inline edit mode. Enter saves (PATCH request). Escape cancels edit, restores original value. After save, tag list refreshes with updated value.
test_cases.notes: PlannerTag.js component. Click → edit, Enter → save, Escape → cancel.

test_cases.title: UI — New tag highlighting and auto-scroll
test_cases.preconditions: User is project manager. Multiple tags already exist (enough to require scrolling).
test_cases.steps:
1. Open 'Tasks closing' tab
2. Add a new tag via the form
3. Observe the tag list
test_cases.expected_result: Newly added tag appears with green left border highlight (5px). List auto-scrolls to the new item. Highlight clears after rendering cycle.
test_cases.notes: TableAsyncBody.tsx: newItemId prop, scroll-into-view, .new-item::before CSS.

test_cases.title: Close-tag permission — read-only user gets empty permission set
test_cases.preconditions: User has read-only role on the project.
test_cases.steps:
1. Log in as read-only user
2. Navigate to Planner
3. Open Project settings modal
4. Check 'Tasks closing' tab
test_cases.expected_result: Read-only user can view the modal but the tag list is read-only. No add/edit/delete controls visible (permissions: empty set). API calls to POST/PATCH/DELETE return 403.
test_cases.notes: PlannerCloseTagPermissionService: read-only users → empty permission set, no CREATE/EDIT/DELETE.

test_cases.title: BUG: PATCH close-tag endpoint returns 500 (gateway routing)
test_cases.preconditions: Tag exists for project. User is admin or PM. Tested on timemachine build 2.1.26-SNAPSHOT.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /v1/projects/{projectId}/close-tags to create tag 'test'
2. PATCH /v1/projects/{projectId}/close-tags/{tagId} with body: {tag: 'updated'}
3. PUT /v1/projects/{projectId}/close-tags/{tagId} with same body
4. Check Swagger spec at /v2/api-docs?group=api for PATCH endpoint
test_cases.expected_result:
Step 2: 500 Internal Server Error — HttpRequestMethodNotSupportedException: 'Request method PATCH not supported'.
Step 3: Same 500 error for PUT.
Step 4: Swagger spec only lists GET, POST (collection) and DELETE (item). PATCH is MISSING from spec. Gateway does not route PATCH to this path.
test_cases.notes: CRITICAL BUG verified on timemachine S74. Controller has @PatchMapping but gateway doesn't forward PATCH. Tag editing impossible. Frontend inline edit (TC-PLN-098) will also fail. Workaround: delete + re-create tag.

test_cases.title: Security: Close-tag accepts unsanitized HTML/script content
test_cases.preconditions: User is admin or PM.
test_cases.expected_result:
Steps 1-2: 200 OK — raw HTML/script content stored in DB without sanitization.
Step 3: Tags returned with raw content including <script> tags.
Step 4: React's default JSX escaping SHOULD prevent execution, but verify no innerHTML/dangerouslySetInnerHTML usage in PlannerTag.js. Cyrillic and special chars stored correctly.
test_cases.notes: No server-side HTML sanitization. Relies on frontend escaping. VARCHAR(255) accepts any characters. Verified on timemachine S74.

test_cases.title: Delete non-existent close-tag returns 404 with error code
test_cases.preconditions: Valid project exists. No tag with given ID.
test_cases.steps: 1. DELETE /v1/projects/{projectId}/close-tags/999999
test_cases.expected_result: 404 Not Found. Error body: {errorCode: 'exception.plannerclosetag.not.found', message: 'id = 999999'}. NotFoundException thrown by repository lookup.
test_cases.notes: Verified on timemachine S74. Error code is descriptive.

test_cases.title: Cross-project close-tag manipulation returns ownership error
test_cases.preconditions: Tag ID=X belongs to project A. User has permissions on project B.
test_cases.steps:
1. DELETE /v1/projects/{projectB_id}/close-tags/{tagFromProjectA_id}
2. Observe error response
test_cases.expected_result: 400 Bad Request. Error: {errorCode: 'exception.validation.fail', message: 'Planner close tag does not belong to project {projectB_id}'}. Ownership check in service layer prevents cross-project manipulation.
test_cases.notes: Verified on timemachine S74. Exact error message confirmed. Complements TC-PLN-054 with verified response details.

test_cases.title: Frontend build deployment verification for close-by-tag UI
test_cases.preconditions: Close-by-tag backend API deployed (V2.1.27 migration applied, API endpoints functional).
test_cases.steps:
1. Check Build # in footer of TTT application
2. Verify build date is AFTER merge date of !5301 (frontend MR)
3. Navigate to Planner, select a project where user is PM
4. Look for 'Project settings' gear icon (formerly 'Project employees')
5. Open modal and verify 2 tabs: 'Project members' and 'Tasks closing'
test_cases.expected_result:
If build includes !5301: Modal shows 2 tabs, 'Tasks closing' tab has tag management UI (add form + tag list).
If build predates !5301: Old 'Project employees' modal with single tab. Close-by-tag UI not available despite backend API being functional.
test_cases.notes: S74 finding: timemachine build 2.1.26-SNAPSHOT.290209 (Mar 11) predates !5301 merge. Backend API works but frontend UI not deployed. Verify after next deployment.

test_cases.title: Create lock on assignment field
test_cases.preconditions: User authenticated. Assignment exists.
test_cases.steps: 1. POST /v1/locks with body: Set of LockDTO [{cellKey: 'assignment-{id}-comment', employeeLogin: '{login}', taskId: {id}, field: 'comment'}]
test_cases.expected_result: 200 OK. Returns set of created LockDTO objects. WebSocket LOCK event emitted.
test_cases.notes: Lockable assignment fields: remainingEstimate, comment, internalComment, uiData

test_cases.title: Lock TTL — expires after 1 minute
test_cases.preconditions: Lock created at time T
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /v1/locks to create lock
2. Wait >60 seconds without refreshing
3. Another user: PATCH the same assignment field
test_cases.expected_result: Step 3: 200 OK (lock expired, field editable). No 423 error.
test_cases.notes: Locks held for 1 minute, must re-invoke POST to extend

test_cases.title: Lock refresh — re-POST extends TTL
test_cases.preconditions: Lock exists, about to expire
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /v1/locks with same lock set
2. Verify lock is still active after original TTL
test_cases.expected_result: Lock extended. Subsequent PATCH by another user still returns 423.

test_cases.title: Lock conflict — same field by different user returns 423
test_cases.preconditions: User A holds lock on assignment field 'comment'
test_cases.steps: 1. User B: POST /v1/locks with same cellKey/field
test_cases.expected_result: 423 Locked. Error indicates lock held by User A.

test_cases.title: Lock — POST replaces previous locks for same employee
test_cases.preconditions: User holds locks on fields A and B
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /v1/locks with only field C
2. GET /v1/locks
test_cases.expected_result: Only field C is locked. Previous locks on A and B are released. WebSocket UNLOCK events for A and B.
test_cases.notes: POST is a 'replace all locks' operation, not additive

test_cases.title: Delete all locks for current employee
test_cases.preconditions: User holds multiple locks
test_cases.steps:
1. DELETE /v1/locks
2. GET /v1/locks
3. Another user: PATCH previously locked field
test_cases.expected_result:
Step 2: no locks for this employee.
Step 3: 200 OK (field now editable).
WebSocket UNLOCK events emitted.

test_cases.title: Search locks for employee/date
test_cases.preconditions: Multiple users hold locks
test_cases.steps: 1. GET /v1/locks with search parameters (employeeLogin, dates)
test_cases.expected_result: 200 OK. Returns set of active LockDTO objects matching search criteria.

test_cases.title: Lock on TaskReport field — effort
test_cases.preconditions: Report exists for employee
test_cases.steps: 1. POST /v1/locks with lock for report field 'effort'
test_cases.expected_result: 200 OK. Lock created. Lockable report fields: effort, reportComment, state, stateComment.
test_cases.notes: Locks cover both assignments and reports

test_cases.title: WebSocket LOCK/UNLOCK events
test_cases.preconditions: WebSocket client subscribed to /topic/employees/{login}/locks
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /v1/locks — create lock
2. DELETE /v1/locks — release lock
3. Observe WebSocket events
test_cases.expected_result:
Step 1: LOCK event received with lock details.
Step 2: UNLOCK event received.
test_cases.notes: Events are async (@Async, @EventListener)

test_cases.title: Get assignment history for employee+task
test_cases.preconditions: Employee has assignment changes recorded (show_in_history=true)
test_cases.steps: 1. GET /v1/assignments/history?employeeLogin={login}&taskId={id}&startDate=2026-03-01&endDate=2026-03-15
test_cases.expected_result: 200 OK. Paginated response with TaskAssignmentHistoryItemDTO objects showing change log.
test_cases.notes: Tab 3 in Planner UI

test_cases.title: History — pagination support
test_cases.preconditions: Employee has many history records
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /v1/assignments/history?...&page=0&pageSize=10
2. GET with page=1
test_cases.expected_result:
Step 1: first 10 records, totalCount reflects all records.
Step 2: next 10 records.
test_cases.notes: PageableUtil.correct(request) normalizes pagination params

test_cases.title: History — missing required fields returns 400
test_cases.preconditions: Valid API token
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /v1/assignments/history (no params)
2. GET with only employeeLogin
test_cases.expected_result: 400 Bad Request. Required: employeeLogin (@NotBlank), taskId (@NotNull), startDate, endDate.

test_cases.title: History — only show_in_history=true records appear
test_cases.preconditions: Some assignments have show_in_history=false in DB
test_cases.steps: 1. GET history for a period containing both show_in_history=true and false records
test_cases.expected_result: Only records with show_in_history=true appear in response.
test_cases.notes: DB column controls visibility

test_cases.title: WebSocket connection with JWT token
test_cases.preconditions: Valid JWT token for authenticated user
test_cases.steps:
1. Connect via STOMP/WebSocket (SockJS fallback) with JWT in connection headers
2. Subscribe to /topic/employees/{login}/assignments/{period}
test_cases.expected_result: Connection established. Subscription confirmed. Events received on assignment changes.
test_cases.notes: Dual auth: JWT or API token in headers

test_cases.title: WebSocket — CRUD events for assignments
test_cases.preconditions: WebSocket subscribed to assignments channel
test_cases.steps:
1. Create assignment via POST
2. Patch assignment via PATCH
3. Observe WebSocket events
test_cases.expected_result: ADD event on create, PATCH event on update. Each event contains Event<T> envelope with EventType, initiatorLogin, timestamp, payload.
test_cases.notes: Events are @TransactionalEventListener — fire after commit

test_cases.title: WebSocket — selection events for cursor awareness
test_cases.preconditions: Two users viewing same planner. WebSocket subscribed to /topic/employees/{login}/selections
test_cases.steps:
1. User A selects a cell in planner
2. User B observes selection event
test_cases.expected_result: SELECT event received with cell selection details. User B can see User A's cursor/selection.
test_cases.notes: Immediate @EventListener (not transactional)

test_cases.title: WebSocket — task rename cascades to 3 channels
test_cases.preconditions: Task used in planner has assignments and reports
test_cases.steps:
1. Rename task via API
2. Observe WebSocket events on tasks, assignments, and reports channels
test_cases.expected_result: TASK_RENAME event sent to /topic/projects/{id}/tasks, /topic/employees/{login}/assignments/{period}, and /topic/employees/{login}/reports/{period}.
test_cases.notes: Rename extracts sub-events and re-publishes to all 3 channels

test_cases.title: WebSocket — connection indicator (SocketManagerLed)
test_cases.preconditions: Planner page open in browser
test_cases.steps:
1. Open planner page
2. Verify connection indicator shows 'connected'
3. Disconnect network briefly
4. Reconnect
test_cases.expected_result:
Step 2: LED indicator green/connected.
Step 3: LED shows disconnected.
Step 4: SockJS reconnects, LED green again.
test_cases.notes: SockJS provides automatic reconnection

test_cases.title: BUG #3314: Order resets on 'Open for Editing'
test_cases.preconditions: Employee has manually DnD-reordered assignments on a date
test_cases.steps:
1. Note current custom assignment order
2. In planner UI, click 'Open for Editing' for the same date
3. Observe assignment order
test_cases.expected_result:
KNOWN BUG (Open): Order resets. Backend returns correct order (confirmed by developer jsaidov) but frontend doesn't preserve it when switching to edit mode.
Expected fix: frontend should maintain backend-provided order.
test_cases.notes: Frontend bug — API returns correct order

test_cases.title: BUG #3332: Tasks duplicated after DnD reorder
test_cases.preconditions: Planner open in Chrome or Firefox
test_cases.steps:
1. Open planner for an employee with multiple assignments
2. Drag-drop an assignment to reorder
3. Observe task list
test_cases.expected_result:
KNOWN BUG (Open): Duplicate entries appear in the task list after DnD. Reproducible in both Chrome and Firefox.
Expected: no duplicates after DnD.
test_cases.notes: Pure frontend state management bug

test_cases.title: BUG #3375: Member order in project planner broken
test_cases.preconditions: Project has custom employee order set via 'Project Settings' popup DnD
test_cases.steps:
1. Set custom employee order in Project Settings popup
2. View project planner (Projects tab)
3. Compare employee order with popup order
test_cases.expected_result:
KNOWN BUG (In Progress): After #3258 fix, employee order changed to alphabetical instead of matching Project Settings popup DnD order.
Expected: employee order should match popup order.
test_cases.notes: Regression from #3258 fix

test_cases.title: BUG: System.out.println in production code
test_cases.preconditions: Access to server logs during assignment move
test_cases.steps:
1. Move assignment via PATCH with nextAssignmentId
2. Check server stdout/application logs
test_cases.expected_result: KNOWN DEBT: System.out.println output visible in logs at line 406 of InternalTaskAssignmentService.moveFutureAssignmentsAccordingly(). Should be replaced with proper logging.
test_cases.notes: Technical debt — debug code in production

test_cases.title: BUG: Generate destroys manual DnD ordering
test_cases.preconditions: Employee has manually reordered assignments via DnD
test_cases.steps:
1. DnD-reorder assignments to custom order
2. Navigate away from planner
3. Return and click 'Open for Editing' (triggers generate)
4. Compare order
test_cases.expected_result:
KNOWN BEHAVIOR: generate() overwrites all position and nextAssignmentId values based on current search results. Manual ordering is lost.
Requirements say 'Opening for edit must NOT reorder' — this is violated.
test_cases.notes: Requirements explicitly say verify this doesn't happen

test_cases.title: Verify #3258 fix: assignment order + add-task
test_cases.preconditions: Release/2.1 deployed
test_cases.steps:
1. Open planner for employee
2. DnD reorder assignments
3. Add a new task to planner
4. Verify order is preserved after add
test_cases.expected_result: Assignment order preserved after adding new task (fix from #3258 applied).
test_cases.notes: Verify regression fix is stable

test_cases.title: Verify #3308 fix: DnD order persisted across days
test_cases.preconditions: Release/2.1 deployed
test_cases.steps:
1. DnD reorder assignments on day D
2. Navigate to day D+1
3. Verify same order applies
test_cases.expected_result: DnD order propagated to future days (fix from #3308 applied).
test_cases.notes: moveFutureAssignmentsAccordingly() implementation

test_cases.title: Planner page — 3 tabs visible (Tasks, Reports, History)
test_cases.preconditions: User logged in, planner page accessible
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to planner page
2. Verify 3 tabs are visible
test_cases.expected_result: Tab 1: Tasks (drag-drop assignment table). Tab 2: Reports (embedded report module). Tab 3: History (change log).

test_cases.title: New task highlighted green for 5 seconds
test_cases.preconditions: Planner open on Tasks tab
test_cases.steps:
1. Add a new task to planner
2. Observe highlighting
test_cases.expected_result: New task row highlighted green for 5 seconds, then returns to normal. Auto-scroll to new task if off-screen.

test_cases.title: Project Settings popup — two tabs
test_cases.preconditions: User is PM/admin for a project. Planner open.
test_cases.steps:
1. Click 'Project Settings' (or gear icon)
2. Verify popup has 2 tabs: 'Project Members' and 'Tasks Closing'
test_cases.expected_result:
Tab 1 (Project Members): employee dropdown, role field, DnD reorder.
Tab 2 (Tasks Closing): close-by-tag management, max 200 chars per tag.
test_cases.notes: Renamed from 'Employees on project' to 'Project Settings'

test_cases.title: Project members — add/remove with notification
test_cases.preconditions: User is PM/admin. Project Settings popup open on 'Project Members' tab.
test_cases.steps:
1. Add employee from dropdown
2. Verify employee appears immediately
3. Remove an employee
4. Verify removal
test_cases.expected_result: Add: immediate UI update, notification sent. Remove: immediate, notification sent. DnD reorder available for member list.
test_cases.notes: WebSocket ADD/DELETE events for members

test_cases.title: Planner — localStorage persistence of plannerTasks
test_cases.preconditions: Browser with localStorage enabled
test_cases.steps:
1. Open planner, search/add tasks
2. Close browser tab
3. Reopen planner page
test_cases.expected_result: Previously searched/added tasks restored from localStorage (plannerTasks Redux slice auto-synced).
test_cases.notes: Risk: stale data if not cleared on logout

test_cases.title: Planner — concurrent editing with cell locking UI
test_cases.preconditions: Two users viewing same planner. Both logged in.
test_cases.steps:
1. User A clicks to edit a cell (comment field)
2. User B sees lock indicator on that cell
3. User B tries to edit same cell
4. User A finishes editing
test_cases.expected_result: Step 2: lock indicator visible. Step 3: edit prevented or warning shown. Step 4: lock released, User B can now edit.
test_cases.notes: Core collaborative editing UX

test_cases.title: Planner — DnD reorder assignments in Tasks tab
test_cases.preconditions: Employee has multiple generated assignments
test_cases.steps:
1. Open planner Tasks tab
2. Drag assignment from position 3 to position 1
3. Verify order change persists
test_cases.expected_result: Assignment moves to new position. PATCH sent to backend with nextAssignmentId. WebSocket PATCH event emitted. Order persists on page reload.

test_cases.title: Planner search API — closed parameter filters assignments
test_cases.preconditions: Planner has both open and closed assignments for a project; timemachine env
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /v1/assignments?projectId={id}&startDate=2026-03-01&endDate=2026-03-31
   (no closed param) — count total assignments
2. GET /v1/assignments?projectId={id}&startDate=2026-03-01&endDate=2026-03-31&closed=false
   — count open-only assignments
3. GET /v1/assignments?projectId={id}&startDate=2026-03-01&endDate=2026-03-31&closed=true
   — count closed-only assignments
4. Verify: open + closed = total
test_cases.expected_result:
closed=null → returns ALL assignments (open + closed).
closed=false → returns only open assignments.
closed=true → returns only closed assignments.
Sum of open + closed equals total.
test_cases.notes: Filter applied in-memory after DB fetch, not at SQL level

test_cases.title: Planner UI — Copy table excludes closed/deleted tasks
test_cases.preconditions: Planner open for a project; at least one task has been closed/deleted
test_cases.steps:
1. Open Planner for a project with assigned tasks
2. Enter edit mode
3. Delete a task with 0 hours (close it)
4. Verify deleted task disappears from the grid
5. Click 'Copy the table' button
6. Paste clipboard content into a text editor
7. Verify: deleted/closed task is NOT in the pasted output
test_cases.expected_result:
Closed/deleted tasks excluded from planner grid.
Copy table output does NOT contain closed tasks.
Frontend passes closed=false to GET /v1/assignments.
test_cases.notes: All 3 planner sagas (project, current, add-new) pass closed: false

test_cases.title: Planner UI — auto-refresh after assignment changes
test_cases.preconditions: Planner project view open; user makes assignment changes
test_cases.steps:
1. Open Planner for a project
2. Add a new assignment to an employee
3. Verify project view refreshes automatically
4. Close an assignment (delete task)
5. Verify project view refreshes automatically
6. Verify no stale data shown after any change
test_cases.expected_result:
After adding assignment: project view refreshes with new data.
After closing assignment: project view refreshes, closed task gone.
fetchProjectAssignments() dispatched after handleManageAssignments.
test_cases.notes: Added yield put(fetchProjectAssignments()) after manage

test_cases.title: Create report with minimum required fields
test_cases.preconditions:
Logged in as employee with REPORTS_EDIT on target task.
Report period is OPEN for current date.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/ttt/v1/reports/create
2. Body: {taskId: <valid>, reportDate: '<today>', executorLogin: '<own>', effort: 60}
3. Verify response status 201
4. GET /api/ttt/v1/reports?taskId=<id>&executorLogin=<own>
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 201. Report created with state=REPORTED.
Response includes id, effort=60, executorLogin, reportDate, periodState=OPEN.
permissions array includes EDIT, DELETE.
test_cases.notes: Note: returns 201 (not 200 like vacation module)

test_cases.title: Create report with optional reportComment
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with REPORTS_EDIT. Open report period.
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 201. reportComment='API development' stored and returned.
Comment visible in My Tasks table cell tooltip.

test_cases.title: Create report -- duplicate (same task+date+executor) returns 409
test_cases.preconditions: Existing report for task X on date Y by employee Z.
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 409 Conflict. AlreadyExistsException.
Response contains 'existentObject' field with existing report ID.
Unique constraint: (task_id, executor_login, report_date).
test_cases.notes: DB constraint: unique (task_id, executor_login, report_date)

test_cases.title: Create report -- effort minimum 1 minute (boundary)
test_cases.preconditions: Open report period.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST with effort: 1
2. POST with effort: 0
3. POST with effort: -1
test_cases.expected_result:
effort=1: HTTP 201 (minimum accepted).
effort=0: HTTP 400 @Min(1) violation.
effort=-1: HTTP 400 @Min(1) violation.
Create requires @Min(1), unlike edit which allows @Min(0).
test_cases.notes: Critical difference: create @Min(1), edit @Min(0)

test_cases.title: BUG: No upper bound on effort -- 1500 min (25h) accepted
test_cases.preconditions: Open report period.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST with effort: 1500 (25 hours)
2. POST with effort: 99999
3. Verify both succeed
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: Both succeed. No maximum effort validation.
1500 minutes = 25 hours on a single day accepted.
Daily report limit should be 36h (2160 min) but not enforced on create.
Warning system exists (DATE_EFFORT_OVER_LIMIT) but doesn't prevent creation.
test_cases.notes: No @Max annotation on effort field

test_cases.title: Create report -- closed report period returns 400
test_cases.preconditions: Report period start date is 2026-03-01. Attempt report for 2026-02-15.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/ttt/v1/offices/<id>/periods/report to verify period
2. POST /api/ttt/v1/reports/create with reportDate=2026-02-15
3. Expect error
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400. ReportPeriodValidator rejects date before period start.
Error: constraint.report.period (implicit).
ReportDate must be >= office report period start.
test_cases.notes: Period boundary enforced per executor's office

test_cases.title: BUG: Future date accepted without restriction
test_cases.preconditions: Open report period.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST with reportDate = '2026-12-31' (far future)
2. Verify creation succeeds
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: HTTP 201. Report created for future date.
No forward-date restriction in backend.
Only backward restriction (report period) exists.
test_cases.notes: Missing future date validation

test_cases.title: Create report -- FINISHED project returns 403
test_cases.preconditions: Project with status=FINISHED in project database.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST with taskId belonging to FINISHED project
2. Expect error
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 403. ProjectFinishedException.
Cannot create reports for FINISHED or CANCELED projects.
Code checks project status before creation.
test_cases.notes: Also applies to CANCELED projects

test_cases.title: Create report -- invalid executorLogin returns 400
test_cases.preconditions: Open report period.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST with executorLogin: 'nonexistent_user_xyz'
2. Expect validation error
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400. @EmployeeLoginExists custom validator rejects.
Login must exist in employee registry.
test_cases.notes: @EmployeeLoginExists annotation

test_cases.title: BUG: Cross-employee reporting -- any employee login accepted
test_cases.preconditions: Employee A reporting for Employee B on a shared task.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as employee A
2. POST with executorLogin: '<employee_B>'
3. Verify report created under Employee B
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: Report created with executorLogin=B, reporterLogin=A.
API doesn't restrict reporting to own login only.
Intended for manager-reports-on-behalf but no role check.
Any authenticated user can report for any employee.
test_cases.notes: No executor=currentUser check on create

test_cases.title: PATCH report -- change effort resets state to REPORTED
test_cases.preconditions: Existing report with state=APPROVED.
test_cases.expected_result:
State automatically reset from APPROVED to REPORTED.
approverLogin cleared.
Key rule: effort change ALWAYS resets state to REPORTED.
test_cases.notes: This is the mechanism to 'un-approve' a report

test_cases.title: PATCH report -- set effort=0 deletes the report
test_cases.preconditions: Existing report with effort=60.
test_cases.expected_result:
PATCH returns 200 but report is deleted.
GET returns 500 (no GET by ID endpoint) or report absent from search.
TaskReportDeleteEvent published.
Edit allows @Min(0) — setting to 0 triggers deletion.
test_cases.notes: Deletion via effort=0 is the only way to delete via PATCH

test_cases.title: PATCH report -- update reportComment only
test_cases.preconditions: Existing report with state=REPORTED.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH with {reportComment: 'Updated comment'}
2. Verify state unchanged, comment updated
test_cases.expected_result:
reportComment updated. State remains REPORTED.
Comment-only change does not trigger state reset.
Only effort change triggers state reset.

test_cases.title: DELETE reports -- batch delete by IDs
test_cases.preconditions: 2+ existing reports owned by current user.
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 200. Reports deleted.
Requires REPORTS_EDIT or REPORTS_APPROVE permission.
test_cases.notes: Batch delete with ID array

test_cases.title: Batch create -- PUT /v1/reports (upsert array)
test_cases.preconditions: Open report period. 3 valid task/date combinations.
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT /api/ttt/v1/reports
2. Body: array of 3 report objects
3. Verify all created
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 200. All 3 reports created (not 201 like single create).
Each item validated independently.
Batch PUT skips locking (unlike PATCH).
test_cases.notes: PUT batch returns 200, POST single returns 201

test_cases.title: Batch PATCH -- approve multiple reports at once
test_cases.preconditions: 3 reports in REPORTED state, user has REPORTS_APPROVE.
test_cases.expected_result:
All reports state=APPROVED. approverLogin set.
Batch PATCH supports bulk approve/reject.
Per-item validation applied.
test_cases.notes: Main use case for manager bulk approval

test_cases.title: BUG: Direct create-as-APPROVED bypasses workflow
test_cases.preconditions: Open report period. User has REPORTS_APPROVE.
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: Report created directly with state=APPROVED.
Bypasses REPORTED -> APPROVED workflow entirely.
Initial state should be forced to REPORTED regardless of input.
test_cases.notes: Initial state not forced to REPORTED on create

test_cases.title: Search reports -- at least one filter required
test_cases.preconditions: Authenticated user.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/ttt/v1/reports without any filter parameters
2. GET with taskId filter
3. GET with executorLogin filter
4. GET with projectId filter
test_cases.expected_result:
No filters: HTTP 400 (TaskReportSearchRequestValidator rejects).
With any filter: HTTP 200 with results.
Validator requires at least one of: taskId, executorLogin, executorsProjectId, projectId.
test_cases.notes: Empty search prevented by custom validator

test_cases.title: GET /reports/total -- aggregated by week
test_cases.preconditions: Authenticated user with REPORTS_VIEW.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/ttt/v1/reports/total?type=PROJECT&date=2026-03-01
2. Verify grouped result structure
test_cases.expected_result:
Returns aggregated totals grouped by project or employee.
type param: PROJECT or EMPLOYEE.
Used by Confirmation page week tabs.

test_cases.title: GET /reports/summary -- employee period summary
test_cases.preconditions: Authenticated user.
test_cases.steps: 1. GET /api/ttt/v1/reports/summary?executorLogin=<login>&date=2026-03-01
test_cases.expected_result:
Returns summary with totalEffort, norm, budgetNorm for period.
Used by My Tasks EffortForPeriodContainer.
Displays week + month norms.

test_cases.title: GET report period for office
test_cases.preconditions: Authenticated user with OFFICES_VIEW.
test_cases.steps: 1. GET /api/ttt/v1/offices/<officeId>/periods/report
test_cases.expected_result:
Returns current report period start date (1st of month).
Format: {start: '2026-03-01'}.
Defines earliest date employees can submit reports.

test_cases.title: GET approve period for office
test_cases.preconditions: Authenticated user with OFFICES_VIEW.
test_cases.steps: 1. GET /api/ttt/v1/offices/<officeId>/periods/approve
test_cases.expected_result:
Returns current approve period start date.
Always <= report period (invariant).
Defines earliest date managers can approve reports.
test_cases.notes: Approve period always <= report period

test_cases.title: PATCH report period -- advance forward
test_cases.preconditions: Accountant JWT auth. Office with report period 2026-03-01.
test_cases.expected_result:
Report period advanced to 2026-04-01.
Employees can no longer submit reports before April.
Cache evicted for this office.
test_cases.notes: Must be 1st of month (validated)

test_cases.title: PATCH report period -- must be 1st of month
test_cases.preconditions: Accountant JWT auth.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH with {start: '2026-03-15'} (not 1st)
2. Expect validation error
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400. getDayOfMonth() != 1 check fails.
Report period must always be the 1st of a month.
test_cases.notes: Backend validates day-of-month

test_cases.title: PATCH report period -- cannot precede approve period
test_cases.preconditions: Approve period = 2026-02-01. Attempt report period = 2026-01-01.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH report period to 2026-01-01
2. Expect error
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400. Report period cannot go before approve period.
Invariant: approve <= report maintained.
test_cases.notes: Invariant enforcement

test_cases.title: PATCH report period -- no upper limit (can jump far forward)
test_cases.preconditions: Current report period 2026-03-01.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH with {start: '2026-12-01'}
2. Verify jump accepted
test_cases.expected_result:
Jump forward accepted. No maximum limit on report period.
Contrast: approve period limited to 1-month jumps.
test_cases.notes: Asymmetry: report unlimited, approve limited to 1-month

test_cases.title: PATCH approve period -- advance 1 month
test_cases.preconditions: Accountant. Approve period 2026-02-01, report period 2026-03-01.
test_cases.expected_result:
Approve period advanced to 2026-03-01.
PeriodChangedEvent published.
Triggers auto-reject for REPORTED reports in February.
Vacation recalculation triggered.
test_cases.notes: Side effects: auto-reject + vacation recalc

test_cases.title: BUG: Approve period -- missing first-day-of-month validation
test_cases.preconditions: Accountant JWT auth.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH approve period with {start: '2026-03-15'}
2. Verify response
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: Accepts non-first-day date.
Report period validates getDayOfMonth()!=1 but approve period does NOT.
Missing validation at line 104 of PeriodServiceImpl.
test_cases.notes: Asymmetric validation: report validates, approve doesn't

test_cases.title: PATCH approve period -- max 1-month jump
test_cases.preconditions: Approve period 2026-02-01.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH with {start: '2026-04-01'} (2-month jump)
2. Expect error
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400. Maximum 1-month jump in either direction.
Cannot advance approve period by more than 1 month at a time.
test_cases.notes: Approve period has stricter jump limit than report period

test_cases.title: PATCH approve period -- cannot go back >2 months from today
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH approve period with {start: '2025-12-01'}
2. Expect error
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400. Cannot go back more than 2 months from current date.
Lower bound: today minus 2 months.

test_cases.title: PATCH approve period -- cannot exceed report period
test_cases.preconditions: Report period 2026-03-01.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH approve period with {start: '2026-04-01'}
2. Expect error
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400. Approve period cannot exceed report period.
Invariant: approve <= report.

test_cases.title: PATCH approve period -- blocked by active extended period
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with active extended report period in office.
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT /api/ttt/v1/periods/report/employees/<login> (extend)
2. PATCH approve period to advance
3. Expect error
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400. Blocked: employee has active extended period.
Must wait for extended period to expire or remove it first.
test_cases.notes: Extended periods block approve advancement

test_cases.title: BUG: PATCH approve period with empty body -> NPE
test_cases.preconditions: Accountant JWT auth.
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: HTTP 500 NullPointerException.
start is null, getMonth() fails with NPE.
Missing null check on request body.
test_cases.notes: NPE on null start field

test_cases.title: BUG: Invalid date format leaks stack trace
test_cases.preconditions: Accountant JWT auth.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH approve period with {start: 'not-a-date'}
2. Observe error response body
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: Full Java stack trace (50+ frames) leaked in response.
Exposes internal class names, package structure, framework version.
Should return clean 400 with format hint.
test_cases.notes: Stack trace leakage -- security concern

test_cases.title: Extended report period -- grant to employee
test_cases.preconditions: Accountant. Employee's report period is 2026-03-01.
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT /api/ttt/v1/periods/report/employees/<login>
2. Verify employee can now report before 2026-03-01
test_cases.expected_result:
Employee gets individual extended report period.
Can submit reports before the office report period start.
Auto-cleaned by ExtendedPeriodScheduler every 5 minutes.
test_cases.notes: Schedule: ExtendedPeriodScheduler every 5 min cleanup

test_cases.title: Period endpoints -- JWT only (no API token)
test_cases.preconditions: API token with standard permissions.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH report period with API_SECRET_TOKEN header
2. PATCH approve period with API_SECRET_TOKEN header
test_cases.expected_result:
Both return 403 or equivalent.
Period management requires AUTHENTICATED_USER (JWT only).
BUG-PERIOD-4: report min/max accepts JWT only, approve min/max accepts both.
test_cases.notes: Inconsistent permission model between report and approve

test_cases.title: Period reopen -- PeriodReopenedEvent published
test_cases.preconditions: Approve period was advanced. Revert 1 month back.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH approve period to go back 1 month
2. Verify PeriodReopenedEvent effects
test_cases.expected_result:
Approve period reverted. PeriodReopenedEvent published.
Previously auto-rejected reports remain REJECTED (not restored).
Employees can now submit new reports in reopened period.
test_cases.notes: Auto-rejected reports NOT restored on reopen

test_cases.title: GET period min/max -- non-salary offices return 404
test_cases.preconditions: Office without salary configuration.
test_cases.steps: 1. GET /api/ttt/v1/offices/<non-salary-id>/periods/report
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 404. Non-salary offices don't have period management.
GET returns computed default for salary offices, 404 for others.

test_cases.title: Lock acquisition on PATCH -- field-level locks created
test_cases.preconditions: Report in REPORTED state. User A has REPORTS_EDIT.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH /api/ttt/v1/reports/<id> as User A with {effort: 120}
2. Verify lock created (check response or subsequent attempt)
test_cases.expected_result:
Lock acquired on fields: EFFORT, REPORT_COMMENT, STATE, STATE_COMMENT.
Lock is employee-specific (User A owns it).
Lock auto-expires after 1 minute.
test_cases.notes: 4 lock fields tracked per report

test_cases.title: Lock conflict -- HTTP 423 when field locked by another user
test_cases.preconditions: User A holds lock on report. User B attempts same field.
test_cases.steps:
1. User A: PATCH report effort (acquires lock)
2. Within 1 minute, User B: PATCH same report effort
3. Observe User B's response
test_cases.expected_result:
User B gets HTTP 423 Locked.
TttLockException thrown.
Error code: exception.ttt.lock.
Unique to reports module -- vacation doesn't use locking.
test_cases.notes: HTTP 423 unique to reports module

test_cases.title: Lock auto-expiry after 1 minute
test_cases.preconditions: User A holds lock on report.
test_cases.steps:
1. User A: PATCH report (acquires lock)
2. Wait >1 minute
3. User B: PATCH same report
test_cases.expected_result:
User B succeeds after 1-minute lock expiry.
Locks auto-expire -- no manual release needed.
Must re-create lock to extend duration.
test_cases.notes: 1-minute TTL, no extension mechanism

test_cases.title: Lock owner can edit own locked field
test_cases.preconditions: User A holds lock on report EFFORT field.
test_cases.steps:
1. User A: PATCH report effort again (same user)
2. Verify no 423 error
test_cases.expected_result:
Same user can edit their own locked field.
Locks are employee-specific -- only block OTHER users.

test_cases.title: Batch PUT skips locking
test_cases.preconditions: 2 users concurrently using batch PUT /v1/reports.
test_cases.steps:
1. User A: PUT /api/ttt/v1/reports (batch create)
2. User B: PUT same tasks concurrently
test_cases.expected_result:
No 423 errors. Batch PUT does not acquire locks.
Lock mechanism only applies to PATCH endpoint.
Potential data race on batch operations.
test_cases.notes: Design: batch operations bypass locking

test_cases.title: Lock cleanup -- LockServiceImpl.cleanUpCache runs every 10 min
test_cases.preconditions: Long-running session with expired locks.
test_cases.steps:
1. Check lock cache state via LockService
2. Wait for cleanUpCache cycle (*/10 * * * *)
test_cases.expected_result:
cleanUpCache runs every 10 minutes.
Removes expired locks from in-memory cache.
Individual locks expire at 1 min; cleanup is background garbage collection.
test_cases.notes: Schedule: */10 * * * *

test_cases.title: Lock state field -- blocks state change by other user
test_cases.preconditions: Manager A starts rejection (acquires STATE lock). Manager B attempts approve.
test_cases.expected_result:
Manager B gets HTTP 423 Locked on STATE field.
Both effort and state fields are independently lockable.
Prevents concurrent approve/reject conflict.
test_cases.notes: Independent per-field locking

test_cases.title: Personal norm calculation -- excludes time-offs
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with 5 vacation days in March 2026.
Office calendar has 22 working days in March.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/ttt/v1/reports/summary for March
2. Verify norm = (22 - 5) * 8 * 60 minutes
3. Cross-reference with DB: statistic_report table
test_cases.expected_result:
personalNorm = max(0, calendarNorm - offHours).
Off hours include: vacations + sick leaves + day-offs + maternity.
Overlapping off-periods merged before calculation.
Result in minutes.
test_cases.notes: Norm = (workDays - timeOffDays) * 8h * 60min

test_cases.title: Budget norm -- excludes admin vacations from off-periods
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with both paid vacation (5 days) and admin/unpaid vacation (3 days).
test_cases.steps:
1. GET summary or statistic_report
2. Compare personal vs budget norm
test_cases.expected_result:
budgetNorm = calendarNorm - (offHours EXCLUDING admin vacations).
Employee on unpaid leave still counted in budget.
Display (#3381): '{individual} ({budget})' when admin vacation exists.
test_cases.notes: Budget includes admin vacation days as working

test_cases.title: Norm display format -- with/without admin vacation
test_cases.preconditions: Employee A: has admin vacation. Employee B: no admin vacation.
test_cases.steps:
1. Check norm display for Employee A
2. Check norm display for Employee B
test_cases.expected_result:
Employee A: '{individual} ({budget})' format (two values).
Employee B: just '{budget}' (single value).
Format switches based on presence of admin vacation.
test_cases.notes: #3381 display format

test_cases.title: Forgotten report threshold -- 90% of personal norm
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with personalNorm=9600 min (20 days * 8h).
Reported effort = 8500 min (88.5% of norm).
test_cases.steps:
1. Check if employee receives forgotten notification
2. Trigger sendReportsForgottenNotifications
test_cases.expected_result:
Threshold: 90% of personalNorm = 8640 min.
8500 < 8640 → employee is under-reported.
Forgotten report notification sent.
Schedule: Mon/Fri 16:00.
test_cases.notes: Threshold: 90% of personal norm

test_cases.title: Statistic report table -- nightly sync updates cache
test_cases.preconditions: Timemachine environment.
test_cases.steps:
1. SELECT * FROM ttt_backend.statistic_report WHERE employee_login='<login>' AND report_date='2026-03-01'
2. Verify fields: reported_effort, month_norm, budget_norm
test_cases.expected_result:
Pre-computed cache in ttt_backend.statistic_report.
Nightly sync at 4:00 AM updates current + previous month.
Three update paths: nightly cron, task report @Async, RabbitMQ.
test_cases.notes: 3 update paths can cause race conditions (BUG-STATS-1)

test_cases.title: Deviation formula and ExcessStatus enum
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with reported=9000 min, budgetNorm=8800 min.
test_cases.steps:
1. Calculate: (9000-8800)/8800 * 100 = 2.27%
2. Verify ExcessStatus in API response
test_cases.expected_result:
excess = (reported - budgetNorm) / budgetNorm * 100%.
ExcessStatus: HIGH (>0%), LOW (<0%), NEUTRAL (==0%), NA (budgetNorm=0).
Display: integer %. Exception: (-1,+1) range shows 1 decimal.
NA displays as '+N/A%' and sorts to top.
test_cases.notes: Special display for values near zero

test_cases.title: Over/under-reporting banner on Confirmation page
test_cases.preconditions: PM with over-reported employee in current month.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to /approve (Confirmation page)
2. Observe banner notification
test_cases.expected_result:
Non-dismissible banner visible to ADMIN, PM, SPM.
Triggers: over-reporting today (current month) OR at month end.
Employee names: red=over, purple=under.
Clock icon tooltip: deviation %, month, DM, projects with PM names.
test_cases.notes: Non-dismissible -- always visible when condition exists

test_cases.title: GET /over-reported endpoint
test_cases.preconditions: Authenticated user with REPORTS_VIEW.
test_cases.steps: 1. GET /api/ttt/v1/reports/over-reported
test_cases.expected_result:
Returns employees with over-reported hours.
Legacy endpoint with N+1 pattern, superseded by statistic_report cache.
Still functional but inefficient.
test_cases.notes: Legacy N+1 endpoint

test_cases.title: Employee Reports page access by role
test_cases.preconditions: Users with different roles.
test_cases.steps:
1. ADMIN: access /employee-reports -> sees all employees
2. OFFICE_DIRECTOR: sees their office only
3. DEPARTMENT_MANAGER: sees subordinates only
4. EMPLOYEE: cannot access
test_cases.expected_result:
ADMIN, CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT: all employees.
OFFICE_DIRECTOR, ACCOUNTANT: their office.
DEPARTMENT_MANAGER, TECH_LEAD: subordinates.
EMPLOYEE: no access (no route guard).
test_cases.notes: Role-scoped data access

test_cases.title: BUG: Race condition between MQ and task report event paths
test_cases.preconditions: Concurrent statistic_report update from two sources.
test_cases.steps:
1. Submit report (triggers @Async task report event)
2. Simultaneously process RabbitMQ vacation change
3. Check statistic_report for inconsistency
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: No pessimistic locking between MQ handler and task report event.
Both paths update statistic_report.reported_effort.
Race condition can produce incorrect cached values.
Inconsistency resolves on next nightly sync.
test_cases.notes: Self-healing via nightly sync, but incorrect during day

test_cases.title: Statistic report -- 2-month sync window only
test_cases.preconditions: Historical data older than 2 months.
test_cases.steps:
1. Check statistic_report for dates 3+ months ago
2. Verify nightly sync only covers current + previous month
test_cases.expected_result:
Nightly sync covers current + previous month only.
No historical back-fill mechanism.
Older months may have stale data if never manually refreshed.
test_cases.notes: Design limitation: no historical sync

test_cases.title: Reports changed notification -- manager reported on behalf
test_cases.preconditions: Manager reports hours for employee on employee's task.
test_cases.steps:
1. Manager: POST /api/ttt/v1/reports/create with executorLogin=employee
2. Trigger sendReportsChangedNotifications (daily 07:50)
3. Check email
test_cases.expected_result:
Notification sent to employee: manager reported on their behalf.
Template shows: task name, date, effort, reporter name.
Schedule: daily at 07:50.
test_cases.notes: Triggered when reporterLogin != executorLogin

test_cases.title: Forgotten report delayed notification
test_cases.preconditions: Employee still under-reported after initial notification.
test_cases.steps: 1. Trigger sendReportsForgottenDelayedNotifications (daily 16:30)
test_cases.expected_result:
Retry notification for employees who received initial forgotten notification
but still haven't reached 90% threshold.
Schedule: daily at 16:30 (30 min after initial batch).
test_cases.notes: Retry mechanism for forgotten reports

test_cases.title: REPORTS_VIEW -- search and aggregation endpoints
test_cases.preconditions: User with REPORTS_VIEW permission (any authenticated user on project).
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/ttt/v1/reports?taskId=<id>
2. GET /api/ttt/v1/reports/total
3. GET /api/ttt/v1/reports/summary
4. GET /api/ttt/v1/reports/over-reported
test_cases.expected_result:
All return 200 with data.
REPORTS_VIEW grants read access to report data.
Includes total, summary, and over-reported endpoints.

test_cases.title: REPORTS_EDIT -- create, batch create, PATCH effort, delete
test_cases.preconditions: User with REPORTS_EDIT (has REPORT TaskPermissionType on task).
test_cases.expected_result:
All succeed. REPORTS_EDIT grants full CRUD.
EDIT condition: reportDate >= report period start AND project not FINISHED.
test_cases.notes: EDIT = REPORT permission + date in period + project active

test_cases.title: REPORTS_APPROVE -- state change to APPROVED/REJECTED
test_cases.preconditions: User with REPORTS_APPROVE (PM/SPM/ADMIN on task's project).
test_cases.expected_result:
Both succeed. REPORTS_APPROVE grants state change rights.
APPROVE condition: has APPROVE TaskPermissionType + reportDate >= approve period.
approverLogin set to current user on approve.
test_cases.notes: APPROVE = APPROVE permission + date in approve period

test_cases.title: User without REPORTS_EDIT cannot create reports
test_cases.preconditions: User with REPORTS_VIEW only (no REPORT TaskPermissionType on task).
test_cases.steps: 1. POST /api/ttt/v1/reports/create for a task where user has VIEW only
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 403. TttSecurityException.
Error code: exception.ttt.security.
REPORTS_EDIT requires REPORT permission on the specific task.
test_cases.notes: Per-task permission check

test_cases.title: User without REPORTS_APPROVE cannot change state
test_cases.preconditions: Regular employee (no PM role on project).
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH own report with {state: 'APPROVED'}
2. Expect error
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 403. Requires APPROVE TaskPermissionType.
Only PM, Senior PM, ADMIN on the project can approve.

test_cases.title: EDIT permission lost when report period closes
test_cases.preconditions: Report with reportDate=2026-02-15. Report period advanced to 2026-03-01.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET report -- check permissions array
2. PATCH report with {effort: 120}
test_cases.expected_result:
permissions array: EDIT absent.
PATCH returns 403 or permission denied.
periodState=CLOSED in response.
EDIT requires reportDate >= report period start.
test_cases.notes: Period closure removes EDIT/DELETE permissions

test_cases.title: APPROVE permission lost when approve period closes
test_cases.preconditions: Report with reportDate=2026-01-15. Approve period advanced to 2026-02-01.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH with {state: 'APPROVED'}
2. Expect error
test_cases.expected_result:
APPROVE permission not granted for reports before approve period.
Must be: reportDate >= approve period start.

test_cases.title: BUG: /effort endpoint missing @PreAuthorize
test_cases.preconditions: Unauthenticated or API token request.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/ttt/v1/reports/effort without JWT
2. Observe response
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: Endpoint accessible without authentication.
No @PreAuthorize annotation on /effort endpoint.
Should require AUTHENTICATED_USER at minimum.
test_cases.notes: Missing @PreAuthorize on 2 endpoints

test_cases.title: BUG: /employee-projects endpoint missing @PreAuthorize
test_cases.preconditions: Unauthenticated request.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/ttt/v1/reports/employee-projects without JWT
2. Observe response
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: Accessible without authentication.
Same issue as /effort -- missing @PreAuthorize.
Both endpoints return data without auth check.
test_cases.notes: Same bug as TC-RPT-085

test_cases.title: Accounting endpoints -- AUTHENTICATED_USER only
test_cases.preconditions:
1. JWT authenticated user
2. API token with permissions
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/ttt/v1/reports/accounting with JWT -> verify access
2. POST /api/ttt/v1/reports/send-accounting-notifications with JWT
3. Same endpoints with API_SECRET_TOKEN header
test_cases.expected_result:
JWT: both endpoints accessible.
API token: both return 403.
Accounting endpoints require AUTHENTICATED_USER (JWT only).
test_cases.notes: No API token access to accounting endpoints

test_cases.title: Warning endpoints -- AUTHENTICATED_USER only
test_cases.preconditions: JWT vs API token access.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/ttt/v1/task-report-warnings with JWT
2. GET /api/ttt/v1/task-auto-reject-warnings with JWT
3. Same with API_SECRET_TOKEN
test_cases.expected_result:
JWT: accessible. API token: 403.
Warning endpoints require AUTHENTICATED_USER.
Auto-reject controller returns BO directly (no DTO -- design issue).
test_cases.notes: Auto-reject returns BO instead of DTO (BO leak)

test_cases.title: Permission calculation flow -- per-task, per-report
test_cases.preconditions: User with PM role on Project A, employee on Project B.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET reports for tasks on Project A
2. GET reports for tasks on Project B
3. Compare permissions arrays
test_cases.expected_result:
Project A reports: permissions include EDIT, DELETE, APPROVE.
Project B reports: permissions include EDIT, DELETE only.
Permissions calculated: group by executor → by task → per report.
test_cases.notes: Per-task permission, not global

test_cases.title: Auth polling -- excessive /authentication/check calls
test_cases.preconditions: Open Confirmation page in browser with network monitor.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to /approve
2. Monitor network tab for 60 seconds
3. Count GET /v1/authentication/check calls
test_cases.expected_result:
50+ GET /v1/authentication/check calls per session.
Excessive auth polling pattern.
Performance concern but functionally harmless.
test_cases.notes: Performance: excessive auth polling

test_cases.title: Error code: NOT_FOUND (404) on non-existent report
test_cases.preconditions: Authenticated user.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH /api/ttt/v1/reports/999999999
2. Observe error response
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 404. Error code: NOT_FOUND.
NotFoundException from InternalTaskReportService.
Note: GET /reports/{id} returns 500 (no GET-by-ID endpoint).
test_cases.notes: GET by ID returns 500 -- must use search

test_cases.title: Error code: exception.ttt.lock (423) on lock conflict
test_cases.preconditions: Two concurrent users editing same report.
test_cases.steps:
1. User A: PATCH report (acquires lock)
2. User B (within 1 min): PATCH same report
3. Observe User B error
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 423 Locked.
Error code: exception.ttt.lock.
TttLockException with lock holder details.
test_cases.notes: 423 is unique to reports module

test_cases.title: Error code: exception.ttt.security (403) on permission denied
test_cases.preconditions: User without REPORTS_EDIT on target task.
test_cases.steps: 1. POST /api/ttt/v1/reports/create for task without REPORT permission
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 403. Error code: exception.ttt.security.
TttSecurityException.
Distinct from AccessDeniedException (which gives FORBIDDEN).
test_cases.notes: Different from vacation's exception.vacation.no.permission

test_cases.title: Error code: CONFLICT (409) on duplicate report
test_cases.preconditions: Existing report for same task+date+executor.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST duplicate report
2. Inspect error response
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 409. Error code: CONFLICT.
AlreadyExistsException with 'existentObject' field.
existentObject contains the existing report's ID.
test_cases.notes: Response contains existing report reference

test_cases.title: Error code: FORBIDDEN (403) on FINISHED project
test_cases.preconditions: Task on FINISHED project.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST report for FINISHED project task
2. Inspect error
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 403. ProjectFinishedException.
Error code: FORBIDDEN (mapped from ProjectFinishedException).
test_cases.notes: ProjectFinishedException -> 403

test_cases.title: Error code: BAD_REQUEST (400) with field-level details
test_cases.preconditions: Invalid create request.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST with missing required fields
2. Inspect validation error structure
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400. Error code: BAD_REQUEST.
MethodArgumentNotValidException provides field-level details.
Each invalid field listed with violation message.
test_cases.notes: ConstraintViolationException also maps to 400

test_cases.title: ServiceException maps to 500 (inconsistent with vacation's 400)
test_cases.preconditions: Trigger an internal service error.
test_cases.steps:
1. Create scenario causing ServiceException
2. Observe HTTP status
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 500. ServiceException maps to INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR in TTT.
Inconsistency: same ServiceException maps to 400 in vacation service.
Different RestErrorHandler implementations per service.
test_cases.notes: TTT: 500, Vacation: 400 -- inconsistent across services

test_cases.title: Effort boundary: @Min(0) on PATCH allows negative effect via 0
test_cases.preconditions: Existing report.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH with {effort: 0} -> deletes report
2. PATCH with {effort: -1} -> validation error
test_cases.expected_result:
effort=0: report deleted (valid, @Min(0)).
effort=-1: HTTP 400 @Min(0) violation.
Boundary at 0: deletes. Below 0: rejected.
test_cases.notes: Asymmetry: create @Min(1), edit @Min(0)

test_cases.title: Report with effort displayed in hours (÷60 conversion)

test_cases.title: My Tasks page -- grouped by project vs ungrouped toggle

test_cases.title: Report event system -- add/patch/delete events published

test_cases.title: Hardcoded CEO login in statistics -- ilnitsky

test_cases.title: Reject response shows approverLogin derived from reject.rejector

test_cases.title: By Employee tab shows over/under report notification
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee has over-reported or under-reported hours for approve period;

test_cases.title: By Employee — norm display uses normForDate with fallback
test_cases.preconditions: Employee has statistics data; some months have date-specific norm, others don't
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to Confirmation > By Employee
