4. Select employee with standard full-month norm
5. Verify notification shows general norm when normForDate is null
6. Verify 'Monthly norm' and 'Norm as of {date}' labels displayed correctly
test_cases.expected_result:
normForDate displayed when available.
Fallback to general norm when normForDate is null.
Labels 'Monthly norm' and 'Norm as of {date}' shown correctly.
test_cases.notes: Fix: normForDate || norm fallback in saga

test_cases.title: By Employee — approve period determines notification month
test_cases.preconditions:
Office approve period is for a past month (not current month);
Employee has overwork in both past and current month
test_cases.steps:
1. Set office approve period to a past month (e.g., February when current is March)
2. Ensure employee has over-reported hours in BOTH months
3. Navigate to Confirmation > By Employee
4. Select the employee
5. Verify notification shows deviation for APPROVE MONTH (past), not current month
6. Navigate to Confirmation > By Projects
7. Verify SAME month shown in notification
test_cases.expected_result:
By Employee shows notification for approve period month, not current month.
Both tabs show identical month context.
When approve month < current month: notification uses approvePeriod.periodStart.
test_cases.notes: Bug: By Employee showed current month; By Projects showed approve month

test_cases.title: By Employee — officeId resolution fallback
test_cases.preconditions: Employee DTOs may have officeId at different paths: direct .officeId or .office.id
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to Confirmation > By Employee
2. Select an employee (check that approve period loads correctly)
3. Verify no JavaScript errors in browser console
4. Test with employees from different offices
5. Verify approve period fetched correctly for each office
test_cases.expected_result:
Approve period loaded correctly for all employees.
No errors from undefined officeId.
Fallback: uses employee.officeId || employee.office.id.
test_cases.notes: Different DTO shapes: some have officeId directly, others at office.id

test_cases.title: By Employee — approve button switches to next user correctly
test_cases.preconditions: Multiple employees with pending approvals; By Employee tab open
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to Confirmation > By Employee
2. Select first employee in list with pending approval
3. Click Approve button
4. Verify: a) Approval succeeds b) View switches to next employee
5. Verify notification updates for the new employee
6. Verify employee list refreshes with updated status
test_cases.expected_result:
After approve: view auto-switches to next pending employee.
Notification refreshes with new employee's statistics.
Employee list updates (approved employee removed from pending).
updateEmployeesWeeksAndMonths called with employee parameter.
test_cases.notes: Fix changed spawn to call for updateEmployeesWeeksAndMonths

test_cases.title: Obtain JWT token via CAS login flow
test_cases.preconditions: Valid CAS credentials. Browser session.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to TTT login page
2. Enter username (CAS SSO)
3. Observe redirect flow
4. Check TTT_JWT_TOKEN cookie/header in subsequent requests
test_cases.expected_result: JWT token issued after successful CAS authentication. Token contains claims: csId, login, officeId, managerId, employeeName, authorities. RS256 signed.

test_cases.title: JWT token contains correct authorities for multi-role user
test_cases.preconditions: User with multiple global roles (e.g. ROLE_ADMIN + ROLE_ACCOUNTANT + ROLE_EMPLOYEE).
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as multi-role user (e.g. perekrest)
2. Decode JWT token (base64)
3. Inspect 'authorities' claim array
test_cases.expected_result: All assigned global roles present in authorities claim. AUTHENTICATED_USER authority included. Roles match employee_global_roles table entries.
test_cases.notes: Verify against DB: SELECT role FROM employee_global_roles WHERE employee_id = (SELECT id FROM employee WHERE login = 'perekrest')

test_cases.title: JWT token expiration after 1 day
test_cases.preconditions: Valid JWT token obtained.
test_cases.steps:
1. Obtain JWT token and note issuance time
2. Decode token, verify 'exp' claim = iat + 86400s
3. Wait for expiration (or manipulate clock on timemachine)
4. Make API request with expired token
test_cases.expected_result: Token rejected after expiry. Response: 401 Unauthorized. No automatic refresh — user must re-authenticate via CAS.
test_cases.notes: 1-day hardcoded in JwtTokenProcessor. No refresh mechanism exists.

test_cases.title: JWT token with tampered signature rejected
test_cases.preconditions: Valid JWT token.
test_cases.steps:
1. Take valid JWT token
2. Modify payload (change login to another user)
3. Re-encode with incorrect key
4. Send request with tampered token in TTT_JWT_TOKEN header
test_cases.expected_result: Request rejected. RS256 signature verification fails. 401 Unauthorized returned.
test_cases.notes: RSA key pair — private key signs, public key verifies

test_cases.title: JWT token with wrong header name ignored
test_cases.preconditions: Valid JWT token.
test_cases.steps:
1. Send API request with JWT in 'Authorization: Bearer' header instead of TTT_JWT_TOKEN
2. Check response
test_cases.expected_result: Request not authenticated via JWT filter — falls through to API token filter, then to anonymous. Expect 401 or 403 depending on endpoint.
test_cases.notes: Custom header name TTT_JWT_TOKEN, not standard Authorization

test_cases.title: JWT grants AUTHENTICATED_USER authority
test_cases.preconditions: Valid JWT token for any active employee.
test_cases.expected_result: Access granted. JWT users always have AUTHENTICATED_USER authority per TokenConst design.
test_cases.notes: Key distinction: JWT gets AUTHENTICATED_USER, API tokens do NOT

test_cases.title: Empty JWT token header returns 401
test_cases.preconditions: No authentication.
test_cases.steps:
1. Send API request with TTT_JWT_TOKEN header set to empty string
2. Check response
test_cases.expected_result: 401 Unauthorized. Filter skips empty token, falls through to unauthorized handler.

test_cases.title: Malformed JWT token (invalid base64) returns 401
test_cases.preconditions: No valid credentials.
test_cases.steps:
1. Send API request with TTT_JWT_TOKEN = 'not-a-valid-jwt-token'
2. Check response
test_cases.expected_result: 401 Unauthorized. JWT parsing fails gracefully without server error.
test_cases.notes: Verify no 500 error or stack trace in response

test_cases.title: JWT token for deactivated employee rejected
test_cases.preconditions: Employee account deactivated in DB (employee.active = false or dismissed).
test_cases.steps:
1. Obtain JWT token before deactivation
2. Deactivate employee
3. Make API request with pre-existing token
4. Check response
test_cases.expected_result: EmployeeActivationResolver rejects inactive employee. Request fails with 401 or 403.
test_cases.notes: Tests token revocation on account changes

test_cases.title: Concurrent JWT tokens for same user both valid
test_cases.preconditions: Same user authenticated from two different browsers/sessions.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as userA from Browser 1, get JWT-1
2. Login as userA from Browser 2, get JWT-2
3. Use JWT-1 to make API request
4. Use JWT-2 to make API request
test_cases.expected_result: Both tokens work simultaneously. Stateless session policy — no server-side session invalidation.
test_cases.notes: STATELESS session creation policy

test_cases.title: JWT filter chain order: JWT checked before API token
test_cases.preconditions: Request with both TTT_JWT_TOKEN and API_SECRET_TOKEN headers.
test_cases.steps:
1. Send request with valid JWT in TTT_JWT_TOKEN and valid API token in API_SECRET_TOKEN
2. Check which identity is used
3. Verify authorities
test_cases.expected_result: JWT identity used (first match wins). Authorities include AUTHENTICATED_USER from JWT, not API token permissions. Filter chain: JWT -> API token -> Authorization.
test_cases.notes: Filter chain order matters for dual-header edge case

test_cases.title: readOnly claim in JWT restricts write operations
test_cases.preconditions: User with readOnly=true flag (e.g. VIEW_ALL role or specific config).
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as readOnly user
2. Attempt write operation (e.g. create vacation, create report)
3. Check response
test_cases.expected_result: Write operations blocked. readOnly flag checked in permission providers. VIEW permissions granted, CREATE/EDIT denied.
test_cases.notes: readOnly flag in JWT claims, enforced at permission provider level

test_cases.title: API token via header (API_SECRET_TOKEN) grants specific permissions
test_cases.preconditions: Valid EMPLOYEE-type API token with REPORTS_VIEW permission.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /v1/reports with API_SECRET_TOKEN header
2. Verify 200 response with report data
test_cases.expected_result: Access granted. Only REPORTS_VIEW permission available — other endpoints requiring different permissions return 403.

test_cases.title: API token via URL query param (?token=) accepted
test_cases.preconditions: Valid API token.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /v1/reports?token={secret_key}&startDate=...&endDate=...
2. Verify response
test_cases.expected_result: Access granted. Token accepted from query parameter. SECURITY RISK: token visible in server logs, browser history, HTTP Referer headers.
test_cases.notes: Known issue: DI-46. Token exposed in URL — information disclosure risk.

test_cases.title: API token does NOT grant AUTHENTICATED_USER authority
test_cases.preconditions: Valid API token with all 21 permissions.
test_cases.steps:
1. Use API token to call endpoint requiring hasAuthority('AUTHENTICATED_USER') only — e.g. GET /v1/sick-leaves/{id}
2. Check response
test_cases.expected_result: 403 Forbidden. API tokens never receive AUTHENTICATED_USER — only explicit ApiPermission enum values from token_permissions table.
test_cases.notes: Core security boundary between JWT and API token auth

test_cases.title: EMPLOYEE-type token (21 perms avg) vs APPLICATION-type token (4.7 perms avg)
test_cases.preconditions: One EMPLOYEE token and one APPLICATION token configured.
test_cases.steps:
1. Query token_permissions for each token type
2. Compare permission sets
3. Test endpoint access with each
test_cases.expected_result: EMPLOYEE tokens have broader permissions (avg 21). APPLICATION tokens are narrowly scoped (avg 4.7). Access correctly restricted per token's permission set.
test_cases.notes: DB: 1833 EMPLOYEE tokens, 23 APPLICATION tokens

test_cases.title: Invalid API token returns 401
test_cases.preconditions: No valid token.
test_cases.steps:
1. Send request with API_SECRET_TOKEN = 'invalid-uuid-token'
2. Check response
test_cases.expected_result: 401 Unauthorized. DatabaseApiTokenResolver finds no matching token in DB.

test_cases.title: API token with VACATIONS_CREATE can create vacation but not sick leave
test_cases.preconditions: API token with VACATIONS_CREATE permission.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /v1/vacations with valid payload — expect 201
2. POST /v1/sick-leaves with valid payload — expect 403
test_cases.expected_result: Vacation created (Pattern A: hasAnyAuthority AUTHENTICATED_USER OR VACATIONS_CREATE). Sick leave fails (Pattern B: hasAuthority AUTHENTICATED_USER only).
test_cases.notes: Key inconsistency: sick leave uses Pattern B, vacation uses Pattern A

test_cases.title: API token permission REPORTS_EDIT allows report creation
test_cases.preconditions: API token with REPORTS_EDIT permission.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /v1/reports with valid task report payload
2. Verify 201 Created
test_cases.expected_result: Report created. REPORTS_EDIT permission enables create, update, and delete for task reports via Pattern A endpoints.

test_cases.title: API token permission REPORTS_APPROVE allows report state changes
test_cases.preconditions: API token with REPORTS_APPROVE permission.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH /v1/reports/{id} with state=APPROVED
2. Verify state change
test_cases.expected_result: Report approved. REPORTS_APPROVE grants confirmation operations via API token.

test_cases.title: All 21 ApiPermission enum values map to correct endpoint groups
test_cases.preconditions: API token with single permission at a time.
test_cases.steps:
For each of the 21 ApiPermission values:
1. Create token with only that permission
2. Test relevant endpoint group
3. Verify access granted for matching endpoints only
4. Verify 403 for non-matching endpoints
test_cases.expected_result: Each permission grants access to its designated endpoint group only: EMPLOYEES_VIEW->employee endpoints, OFFICES_VIEW->office endpoints, STATISTICS_VIEW->statistics, etc.
test_cases.notes: 21 permissions: EMPLOYEES, OFFICES, STATISTICS, SUGGESTIONS, PROJECTS, TASKS, ASSIGNMENTS, REPORTS, VACATIONS, VACATION_DAYS, CALENDAR, FILES + VIEW/EDIT/APPROVE/CREATE variants

test_cases.title: API token for deleted/deactivated employee token owner
test_cases.preconditions: EMPLOYEE-type token whose owner employee is deactivated.
test_cases.steps:
1. Note employee with active API token
2. Deactivate employee
3. Use token for API call
4. Check response
test_cases.expected_result: Token should be invalidated or access denied. Verify DatabaseApiTokenResolver checks owner activation status.

test_cases.title: Simultaneous JWT and API token — JWT takes precedence
test_cases.preconditions: Valid JWT and valid API token for different users.
test_cases.steps:
1. Send request with TTT_JWT_TOKEN for userA and API_SECRET_TOKEN for userB
2. Check which identity resolves
3. Verify authorities match JWT user
test_cases.expected_result: JWT filter executes first (filter chain order). Request processed as userA with AUTHENTICATED_USER + roles. API token ignored.
test_cases.notes: Same as TC-SEC-011 but emphasizing identity resolution

test_cases.title: API token permission boundary: CALENDAR_VIEW on calendar service
test_cases.preconditions: API token with CALENDAR_VIEW permission.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/calendar/v1/offices with API token
2. Check response
3. Note calendar service uses compound auth: hasAnyRole AND hasAnyAuthority
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar service uses Pattern C (compound): requires BOTH a role AND an authority. API tokens may fail if compound check requires role that API token doesn't carry.
test_cases.notes: Pattern C is stricter — unique to calendar service

test_cases.title: Employee (ROLE_EMPLOYEE) — default pages accessible
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as user with only ROLE_EMPLOYEE.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to My Tasks (/)
2. Navigate to Planner (/planner)
3. Navigate to FAQ (/faq)
4. Navigate to Statistics (/statistics)
5. Navigate to My Vacations (/vacation/my)
6. Navigate to Vacation Chart (/vacation/chart)
7. Navigate to Account (/admin/account)
test_cases.expected_result: All 7 pages load successfully. These are available to all authenticated users without specific permission requirements.

test_cases.title: Employee cannot access manager/admin pages
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as user with only ROLE_EMPLOYEE (no PM, DM, ACC, ADM).
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to /approve (Confirmation)
2. Navigate to /admin/projects
3. Navigate to /admin/employees
4. Navigate to /admin/settings
5. Navigate to /admin/calendar
6. Navigate to /admin/api
test_cases.expected_result: All pages blocked. PrivateRoute redirects to home page or shows 'Access Denied'. Requires TASKS:VIEW_APPROVES, PROJECTS:VIEW, EMPLOYEES:VIEW, SETTINGS:VIEW, CALENDARS:VIEW, TOKENS:VIEW respectively.

test_cases.title: Project Manager page access (ROLE_PROJECT_MANAGER)
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as PM-only user.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to /approve — expect access (TASKS:VIEW_APPROVES)
2. Navigate to /admin/projects — expect access (PROJECTS:VIEW)
3. Navigate to /admin/employees — expect access (EMPLOYEES:VIEW)
4. Navigate to /vacation/request — expect access (VACATIONS:VIEW_APPROVES)
5. Navigate to /admin/settings — expect denied (SETTINGS:VIEW = ADM only)
6. Navigate to /admin/calendar — expect denied (CALENDARS:VIEW = CACC/ADM)
test_cases.expected_result: PM has access to Confirmation, Projects, Employees, Vacation Requests. Denied for Settings, Calendar, API Tokens, Export.

test_cases.title: Accountant page access (ROLE_ACCOUNTANT)
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as ACC-only user.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to /approve — expect access (TASKS:VIEW_APPROVES)
2. Navigate to /vacation/payment — expect access (VACATIONS:VIEW_PAYMENTS)
3. Navigate to /vacation/days-correction — expect access (VACATIONS:VIEW_DAYS)
4. Navigate to /vacation/sick-leaves-of-employees — expect access (SICK_LEAVE_VIEW)
5. Navigate to /admin/offices — expect access (OFFICES:VIEW)
6. Navigate to /admin/projects — expect denied (PROJECTS:VIEW = PM/DM/ADM)
test_cases.expected_result: Accountant has accounting-oriented access: payments, days correction, sick leaves, offices. No project management access.

test_cases.title: Admin has access to all pages (ROLE_ADMIN)
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as ROLE_ADMIN user.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to all 23+ pages listed in page access matrix
2. Verify each page loads with full functionality
test_cases.expected_result: Admin can access every page including Settings, Calendar, API Tokens, Export. All permission classes granted.
test_cases.notes: 8 admin users in DB

test_cases.title: VIEW_ALL role grants read-only access to all pages
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as ROLE_VIEW_ALL user.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to all view-related pages
2. Attempt to create/edit on pages that allow it
3. Verify readOnly flag blocks writes
test_cases.expected_result: VIEW_ALL user can view all pages (same view permissions as admin). Write operations blocked by readOnly flag. Create buttons disabled/hidden.
test_cases.notes: 13 VIEW_ALL users in DB. readOnly=true in JWT claims.

test_cases.title: SECURITY GAP: Accounting subroutes lack frontend permission checks
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as regular employee (ROLE_EMPLOYEE only).
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate directly to /accounting/salary
2. Navigate to /accounting/vacation-payout
3. Navigate to /accounting/days-correction
4. Navigate to /accounting/periods
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: All 4 pages load despite no ACCOUNTING:VIEW permission. Frontend routes missing permission guards. Backend API still requires auth, so data may not load, but page renders.
test_cases.notes: Known gap: 4 of 5 accounting subroutes unprotected at frontend level

test_cases.title: SECURITY GAP: Sick leave route lacks permission check (TODO in code)
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as regular employee.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to /sick-leave
2. Check if page loads
3. Check source code comment
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: Page loads without permission check. Source code contains Russian TODO comment: 'Add permission check. Component created for future sprints.' Functional but unguarded.
test_cases.notes: Known issue DI-33. TODO remains in code.

test_cases.title: Department Manager (ROLE_DEPARTMENT_MANAGER) — vacation employees page
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as DM user.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to /vacation/vacation-days — expect access (VACATIONS:VIEW_EMPLOYEES, DM exclusive)
2. Navigate to /budget-notifications — expect access (BUDGET_NOTIF:VIEW)
3. Navigate to /admin/projects — expect access (PROJECTS:VIEW)
4. Navigate to /admin/settings — expect denied
test_cases.expected_result: DM has unique access to Vacation Days page (DM-exclusive). Budget Notifications also accessible. Settings denied.
test_cases.notes: 29 DMs in DB

test_cases.title: Contractor (ROLE_CONTRACTOR) — minimal access
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as user with ROLE_CONTRACTOR only.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to My Tasks, Planner, FAQ, Statistics — expect access
2. Navigate to /vacation/my — check access
3. Navigate to /approve, /admin/projects, /vacation/request — expect denied
test_cases.expected_result: Contractor gets basic personal access only. No explicit permissions granted in any PermissionProvider. Access limited to pages with no permission requirement.
test_cases.notes: 159 contractors in DB. Unclear permission model is a gap.

test_cases.title: Chief Accountant (ROLE_CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT) — calendar admin access
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as CACC user.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to /admin/calendar — expect access (CALENDARS:VIEW = CACC/ADM/VALL)
2. Navigate to /admin/offices — expect access (OFFICES:VIEW)
3. Navigate to /admin/salary — expect access (ACCOUNTING:NOTIFY)
4. Navigate to /admin/projects — expect denied
test_cases.expected_result: CACC has accounting + calendar admin access. Calendar admin is unique to CACC/ADM/VALL.
test_cases.notes: Only 2 CACC users in DB

test_cases.title: Multi-role user gets union of all role permissions
test_cases.preconditions: User with ROLE_ADMIN + ROLE_ACCOUNTANT + ROLE_PROJECT_MANAGER.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as multi-role user
2. Navigate to all pages
3. Verify access is union (not intersection) of all assigned roles
test_cases.expected_result: All pages accessible. Permission is additive — having ROLE_ADMIN already grants all permissions. Multi-role grants union of permission sets.

test_cases.title: Employee Reports page — self-access without EMPLOYEES:VIEW
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as regular employee (no EMPLOYEES:VIEW permission).
test_cases.expected_result: Own reports accessible (self-access exception). Other employees' reports blocked — requires EMPLOYEES:VIEW permission.
test_cases.notes: Self-access bypass for personal data

test_cases.title: Statistics page — tab visibility based on permissions
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as user with specific role (e.g. PM vs employee).
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to /statistics
2. Check which tabs are visible
3. Compare with statisticsPermissionsTabName mapping
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: Most tab-to-permission mappings commented out in code. currentTab defaults to null. Users with specific permissions see no default tab selected on page load.
test_cases.notes: Known DI-35: statisticsPermissionsTabName mappings commented out

test_cases.title: Pattern A endpoints accept both JWT and API token
test_cases.preconditions: Valid JWT and valid API token (with matching permission).
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /v1/reports with JWT token — expect 200
2. GET /v1/reports with API token (REPORTS_VIEW) — expect 200
3. GET /v1/vacations with JWT — expect 200
4. GET /v1/vacations with API token (VACATIONS_VIEW) — expect 200
test_cases.expected_result: Both auth methods work. Pattern A: hasAnyAuthority('AUTHENTICATED_USER', 'SPECIFIC_PERMISSION') — correct dual-auth pattern.
test_cases.notes: Pattern A is the recommended pattern per auth doc

test_cases.title: Pattern B endpoints reject API token with 403
test_cases.preconditions: API token with all 21 permissions.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /v1/sick-leaves/{id} with API token — expect 403
2. POST /v1/sick-leaves with API token — expect 403
3. PATCH /v1/sick-leaves/{id} with API token — expect 403
4. DELETE /v1/sick-leaves/{id} with API token — expect 403
5. GET /v1/reports/accounting with API token — expect 403
6. GET /v1/task-report-warnings with API token — expect 403
test_cases.expected_result: All endpoints return 403. Pattern B: hasAuthority('AUTHENTICATED_USER') only — API tokens excluded by design (no AUTHENTICATED_USER).
test_cases.notes: Major inconsistency: sick leave CRUD inaccessible via API automation

test_cases.title: Pattern C (calendar service) compound role+authority check
test_cases.preconditions: JWT user with calendar-related roles. API token with CALENDAR_VIEW.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/calendar/v1/offices with JWT — check access
2. GET /api/calendar/v1/offices with API token (CALENDAR_VIEW) — check access
3. Compare behavior
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar service uses compound auth: hasAnyRole(...) AND hasAnyAuthority(...). Stricter than Pattern A — requires both conditions met simultaneously.
test_cases.notes: Unique to calendar service — different from TTT and vacation services

test_cases.title: Missing @PreAuthorize on /reports/effort endpoint
test_cases.preconditions: Any valid authenticated request (minimal permissions).
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: Access granted despite no REPORTS permission. Endpoint missing @PreAuthorize annotation entirely. Any authenticated request can access.
test_cases.notes: Known DI-53. Missing auth annotation.

test_cases.title: Missing @PreAuthorize on /reports/employee-projects endpoint
test_cases.preconditions: Any valid authenticated request (minimal permissions).
test_cases.steps:
1. Create API token with only EMPLOYEES_VIEW
2. GET /v1/reports/employee-projects
3. Check response
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: Access granted despite no REPORTS permission. Endpoint missing @PreAuthorize annotation.
test_cases.notes: Same issue as TC-SEC-042

test_cases.title: Sick leave search+count work via API token but CRUD does not
test_cases.preconditions: API token with VACATIONS_VIEW permission.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /v1/sick-leaves (search) — expect 200
2. GET /v1/sick-leaves/count — expect 200
3. GET /v1/sick-leaves/{id} — expect 403
4. POST /v1/sick-leaves — expect 403
test_cases.expected_result: Search and count use Pattern A (AUTHENTICATED_USER OR VACATIONS_VIEW) — works. CRUD uses Pattern B (AUTHENTICATED_USER only) — blocks API token. Inconsistent permission design.
test_cases.notes: Known DI-45. Blocks API test automation for entire sick leave module.

test_cases.title: Period API permission inconsistency: report min/max vs approve min/max
test_cases.preconditions: API token with OFFICES_VIEW permission.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /v1/offices/periods/report/min — expect 403 (JWT only)
2. GET /v1/offices/periods/report/max — expect 403
3. GET /v1/offices/periods/approve/min — expect 200 (accepts API token)
4. GET /v1/offices/periods/approve/max — expect 200
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: Report period min/max missing OFFICES_VIEW fallback, unlike approve counterparts. Inconsistent API behavior.
test_cases.notes: Known DI-92/97. Permission mismatch between symmetric endpoints.

test_cases.title: Day-off endpoints — all AUTHENTICATED_USER only
test_cases.preconditions: API token with VACATIONS_VIEW and VACATIONS_CREATE permissions.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /v1/employee-dayOff (search) with API token
2. POST /v1/employee-dayOff with API token
3. PUT /v1/employee-dayOff/approve/{id} with API token
test_cases.expected_result: Verify day-off endpoint auth pattern. Check if Pattern A or Pattern B. If Pattern B, same gap as sick leave module.
test_cases.notes: Determine actual pattern — not yet verified for all day-off endpoints

test_cases.title: Vacation CRUD endpoints accept API token (Pattern A)
test_cases.preconditions: API token with VACATIONS_CREATE permission.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /v1/vacations with valid payload — expect 201
2. PUT /v1/vacations/approve/{id} — expect 200
3. PUT /v1/vacations/reject/{id} — expect 200
4. DELETE /v1/vacations/{id} — expect 200 (if status allows)
test_cases.expected_result: All vacation lifecycle endpoints use Pattern A: hasAnyAuthority('AUTHENTICATED_USER', 'VACATIONS_CREATE'). API token access works correctly.
test_cases.notes: Reference implementation of correct Pattern A usage

test_cases.title: Report confirmation endpoints — REPORTS_APPROVE permission required
test_cases.preconditions: API token with REPORTS_APPROVE permission.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH /v1/reports/{id} with state=APPROVED — expect 200
2. PATCH /v1/reports (batch) with state changes — expect 200
test_cases.expected_result: Confirmation operations work via API token with REPORTS_APPROVE. Pattern A correctly allows both JWT and API token access.

test_cases.title: Token management endpoints — JWT only
test_cases.preconditions: API token (not JWT). Admin API token with all permissions.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /v1/tokens with API token — expect 403
2. POST /v1/tokens with API token — expect 403
3. PATCH /v1/tokens with API token — expect 403
4. DELETE /v1/tokens with API token — expect 403
test_cases.expected_result: All token management endpoints require hasAuthority('AUTHENTICATED_USER') — JWT only. API tokens cannot self-manage. Security-correct design.
test_cases.notes: Pattern B used intentionally — correct for token self-management

test_cases.title: Statistics endpoints — 10 JSON + 10 CSV, all with proper auth
test_cases.preconditions: API token with STATISTICS_VIEW permission.
test_cases.steps:
1. Test each of 10 JSON statistic endpoints
2. Test each of 10 CSV mirror endpoints
3. Verify consistent auth pattern across all 20
test_cases.expected_result: All 20 statistics endpoints should use consistent auth pattern. Verify no gaps in the 10+10 mirror pattern.
test_cases.notes: 20 endpoints total — verify all have @PreAuthorize

test_cases.title: Suggest/autocomplete endpoints — permission mapping
test_cases.preconditions: API token with different permission levels.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /v1/suggest/employees with EMPLOYEES_VIEW — expect 200
2. GET /v1/suggest/projects with PROJECTS_VIEW — expect 200
3. GET /v1/suggest/tasks without TASKS_VIEW — expect 403
4. Verify each suggest endpoint maps to correct permission
test_cases.expected_result: 6 suggest endpoints each mapped to their respective VIEW permission. Verify consistent enforcement.
test_cases.notes: 6 suggest endpoints: employees, projects, tasks, customers, offices

test_cases.title: Vacation self-approval by DEPARTMENT_MANAGER
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as user with ROLE_DEPARTMENT_MANAGER who is their own manager.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /v1/vacations to create vacation request
2. Observe auto-assigned approver = self
3. PUT /v1/vacations/approve/{id}
4. Check status
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: Self-approval succeeds. Same person creates and approves. No segregation of duties check. Approver auto-assigned to self for DM.
test_cases.notes: Known DI-44. No creator != approver validation.

test_cases.title: Report self-approval via API — executor approves own report
test_cases.preconditions: API token owner (pvaynmaster) is both executor and approver.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /v1/reports with executorLogin=pvaynmaster
2. PATCH /v1/reports/{id} with state=APPROVED
3. Verify approverLogin = executorLogin
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: Self-approval works. Same person submits and approves their time report. No executor != approver check.
test_cases.notes: Known DI-50. Same pattern as vacation module.

test_cases.title: Direct create-as-APPROVED bypasses approval workflow
test_cases.preconditions: API token with REPORTS_EDIT permission.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /v1/reports with state=APPROVED in request body
2. Verify report created in APPROVED state
3. Check approverLogin = creator
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: Report created directly in APPROVED state, completely bypassing the approval workflow. No validation that initial state must be REPORTED.
test_cases.notes: Known DI-51. Most severe SoD violation.

test_cases.title: Cross-employee reporting — API token owner reports for another
test_cases.preconditions: API token owned by pvaynmaster. Target employee: sterekhin.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /v1/reports with executorLogin=sterekhin (different from token owner)
2. Verify report created
3. Check reporter vs executor fields
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: Report created with executor=sterekhin, reporter=pvaynmaster. No validation that API token owner can only report for themselves.
test_cases.notes: Known DI-52. Any API token owner can report hours for any employee.

test_cases.title: Day-off CPO self-approval pattern
test_cases.preconditions: User with PROJECT role (CPO). Self is own manager.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /v1/employee-dayOff to create day-off request
2. Check request status immediately
3. Verify auto-approval
test_cases.expected_result: Request may be auto-approved on creation for CPO users (approver=self). Same SoD concern as vacation. Verify whether manual approval step is skipped.
test_cases.notes: CPO self-approve: approver=self, manager->optional

test_cases.title: Sick leave — any user can create for any employee
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as regular employee with no management role. Target: unrelated employee.
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: Sick leave created for any employee regardless of requestor's relationship. No creation permission check beyond AUTHENTICATED_USER.
test_cases.notes: Known DI-26. Access control gap — no org-hierarchy check.

test_cases.title: Batch report approval — self-approve multiple reports at once
test_cases.preconditions: API token owner is executor of multiple pending reports.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST multiple reports with executorLogin=self
2. PATCH /v1/reports (batch) with state=APPROVED for all
3. Verify all approved by self
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: Batch self-approval works. Self-approval bug compounds with batch operations — single API call approves many self-reported hours.
test_cases.notes: Amplifies DI-50 impact — batch scale self-approval

test_cases.title: Re-approval of rejected vacation without edit
test_cases.preconditions: Vacation in REJECTED state.
test_cases.steps:
1. Check permissions field on REJECTED vacation
2. Verify APPROVE is available
3. PUT /v1/vacations/approve/{id} without editing
4. Check status changes to APPROVED
test_cases.expected_result: REJECTED vacations can be re-approved without any modification. Permissions still show APPROVE action. May be intentional but bypasses rejection reason.
test_cases.notes: Potential design issue: rejection can be overridden without addressing reason

test_cases.title: Approval change — verify old approver loses approval capability
test_cases.preconditions: Vacation with approver=userA. Change to approver=userB.
test_cases.expected_result: After approver change, old approver cannot approve. New approver has full approval capability. Old approver moved to optional approvers.
test_cases.notes: Proper SoD enforcement after delegation

test_cases.title: Stack trace in 400 error — vacation payment dates
test_cases.preconditions: Missing required parameter.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /v1/paymentdates without required params
2. Inspect response body
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: Full Java stack trace returned in 400 response. Exposes class names (VacationPaymentDatesController), method names, package structure, framework versions.
test_cases.notes: Known DI-77. Multiple endpoints affected.

test_cases.title: Stack trace in 400 error — period patch with invalid date
test_cases.preconditions: Invalid date format in PATCH request.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH /v1/offices/{id}/periods/report with start='invalid-date'
2. Inspect response body
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: 98+ frame Java stack trace in response body. Reveals OfficePeriodController, Spring internals, Jackson deserialization chain.
test_cases.notes: Known DI-96. Server should return structured error without stack trace.

test_cases.title: Stack trace in 500 error — vacation status=ALL NPE
test_cases.preconditions: status=ALL query parameter.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /v2/vacations?status=ALL
2. Inspect 500 response body
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: NullPointerException stack trace returned. Reveals VacationRepositoryCustomImpl.buildCommonCondition:433, JPA/Hibernate internals.
test_cases.notes: Known accounting API bug. 500 + full stack trace = double issue.

test_cases.title: CORS allows all origins — cross-origin API request
test_cases.preconditions: Request from external origin.
test_cases.steps:
1. From JavaScript on external site (e.g. localhost:8080), make fetch() to TTT API
2. Check CORS headers in response
3. Verify Access-Control-Allow-Origin
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: CORS enabled for ALL origins without restriction. Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * (or reflects any origin). Any website can make cross-origin API requests.
test_cases.notes: Known DI-47. Enables CSRF-style attacks if tokens are accessible.

test_cases.title: API token visible in URL query parameter
test_cases.preconditions: Valid API token.
test_cases.steps:
1. Make API call with ?token={secret_key} in URL
2. Check server access logs for token value
3. Check browser history for URL with token
4. Check HTTP Referer header on subsequent navigation
test_cases.expected_result: Token accepted via URL but visible in: server access logs, browser history, HTTP Referer headers, proxy logs, CDN logs. Information disclosure risk.
test_cases.notes: Known DI-46. Token exposure in URL. Should be header-only.

test_cases.title: Credentials committed to source control
test_cases.preconditions: Access to Git repository.
test_cases.steps:
1. Check vacation/app/application.yml in repository
2. Look for api-token and password values
3. Verify if values are actual secrets vs placeholders
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: API token value and DB password (password=123456) committed to source control in application.yml. Should use environment variables or vault.
test_cases.notes: Known DI-4. Credentials in source code.

test_cases.title: Error response format inconsistency — TTT vs Vacation service
test_cases.preconditions: Trigger errors on both services.
test_cases.steps:
1. Trigger 400 on TTT service — check response format
2. Trigger 400 on Vacation service — check response format
3. Compare error structures
test_cases.expected_result: TTT returns structured errors with errorCode. Vacation sometimes includes 'trace' field, sometimes doesn't. Non-existent vacation IDs return 400 (not 404), non-existent offices return 200 with default data.
test_cases.notes: Inconsistent error handling across services

test_cases.title: Invalid office ID returns 200 with default period data
test_cases.preconditions: Non-existent office ID.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /v1/offices/99999/periods/report
2. GET /v1/offices/0/periods/report
3. GET /v1/offices/-1/periods/report
4. Check responses
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: All return 200 with default period data instead of 404. Silent fallback masks invalid input. Could cause downstream logic errors.
test_cases.notes: Known accounting API bug. Invalid input accepted silently.

test_cases.title: GraalVM sandbox bypass potential — tracker script injection
test_cases.preconditions: Admin access to configure tracker integration scripts.
test_cases.steps:
1. Create custom script for tracker with obfuscated loop (avoiding keyword detection)
2. Attempt script variants: Unicode homoglyphs for 'while'/'for', eval-based construction
3. Check if infinite loop or resource exhaustion is possible
test_cases.expected_result: GraalVM JavaScript sandbox uses string keyword matching (while, for, do, goto) not AST analysis. Potentially bypassable with Unicode or eval tricks. Resource limit should prevent infinite execution.
test_cases.notes: Known DI-110. String matching vs AST analysis for loop prevention.

test_cases.title: Vacation days list — no pagination on 1609 records
test_cases.preconditions: API call to vacation days endpoint.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /v1/vacationdays — returns all employees
2. Check response size
3. Check for pagination parameters
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 1609 employee records in single response without pagination. Potential for information over-exposure — any authenticated user can see all employees' vacation day balances.
test_cases.notes: Known accounting finding. No pageSize/page params.

test_cases.title: Planner close-tag: admin can create/edit/delete
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as ROLE_ADMIN user. Existing project with tags.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /v1/planner/close-tags — create new tag for project
2. PATCH /v1/planner/close-tags/{id} — rename tag
3. DELETE /v1/planner/close-tags/{id}
4. Verify all operations succeed
test_cases.expected_result: Admin has CREATE+EDIT+DELETE permissions on all project close-tags. All-or-nothing grant model.
test_cases.notes: #2724 Sprint 15

test_cases.title: Planner close-tag: project manager can manage own project tags
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as PM of specific project.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /v1/planner/close-tags for own project — expect 201
2. POST /v1/planner/close-tags for different project — expect 403
3. Verify object-level scoping
test_cases.expected_result: PM can manage tags for their own project only. Permission granted via employee.id == project.managerId check.

test_cases.title: Planner close-tag: senior manager and project owner access
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as senior manager (project.seniorManagerId) or owner (project.ownerId).
test_cases.steps:
1. As senior manager, create tag for project — expect 201
2. As project owner, create tag — expect 201
3. Verify both roles get full CREATE+EDIT+DELETE
test_cases.expected_result: Both senior manager and project owner have full tag management permissions. Same all-or-nothing model as admin and PM.

test_cases.title: Planner close-tag: regular employee denied (403)
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as regular employee who is NOT PM/senior manager/owner/admin.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /v1/planner/close-tags — list tags — expect 200 (read allowed)
2. POST /v1/planner/close-tags — create — expect 403
3. PATCH /v1/planner/close-tags/{id} — edit — expect 403
4. DELETE /v1/planner/close-tags/{id} — delete — expect 403
test_cases.expected_result: Employee can list/view tags (read access) but cannot create, edit, or delete. Empty permission set returned.
test_cases.notes: Key test: non-authorized user blocked

test_cases.title: Planner close-tag: readOnly user denied write operations
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as user with readOnly=true flag (e.g. VIEW_ALL).
test_cases.steps:
1. Attempt to create close-tag — expect 403
2. Attempt to edit — expect 403
3. Attempt to delete — expect 403
test_cases.expected_result: readOnly users get empty permission set regardless of role. Write operations blocked.
test_cases.notes: readOnly check comes before role check

test_cases.title: Planner close-tag: cross-project manipulation blocked
test_cases.preconditions: PM of Project A. Tag belongs to Project B.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH /v1/planner/close-tags/{tagOfProjectB} with projectId=A — expect error
2. DELETE /v1/planner/close-tags/{tagOfProjectB} — expect 403
test_cases.expected_result: Cross-project validation prevents modifying tags from other projects. Tag verified to belong to specified project.
test_cases.notes: Cross-project guard in delete/update

test_cases.title: Vacation approval — only assigned approver can approve
test_cases.preconditions: Vacation assigned to approver=userA. Logged in as userB (PM but not assigned).
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT /v1/vacations/approve/{id} as userB (not assigned approver)
2. Check response
test_cases.expected_result: Approval denied. Only the assigned primary approver (or admin) can approve. Object-level permission on vacation entity.
test_cases.notes: Object-level: vacation.approver check

test_cases.title: Vacation permissions field varies by status and role
test_cases.preconditions: Different users viewing same vacation at different statuses.
test_cases.steps:
1. As owner, check permissions on NEW vacation — expect EDIT, DELETE
2. As approver, check permissions — expect APPROVE, REJECT, CHANGE_APPROVER
3. On PAID vacation, check permissions — expect empty (immutable)
4. As unrelated user — check permissions — expect empty
test_cases.expected_result: Permissions field dynamically computed per user+status combination. PAID status = fully immutable (empty permissions for all).
test_cases.notes: Dynamic permission resolution per request

test_cases.title: Report approval — only assigned managers can approve in UI
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as PM. Viewing confirmation page.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to /approve
2. Check which employees' reports are visible
3. Verify only reports from project members (PM's projects) are shown
test_cases.expected_result: Confirmation page scoped to PM's project members only. Object-level filtering based on project_member relationship.
test_cases.notes: UI-level scoping — API-level may differ

test_cases.title: Report effort upper bound — no maximum validation
test_cases.preconditions: API token with REPORTS_EDIT.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /v1/reports with effort=1500 (25 hours)
2. POST /v1/reports with effort=99999 (1666 hours)
3. Check both accepted
4. Verify summary counts
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: No upper bound validation on effort field. Values exceeding 24h/day accepted. Min validated (>=1 min), max is not.
test_cases.notes: Known BUG-REPORT-1. Minimum enforced, maximum missing.

test_cases.title: Report future date — accepted without restriction
test_cases.preconditions: API token with REPORTS_EDIT.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /v1/reports with reportDate=2026-12-31 (9 months ahead)
2. Verify accepted
3. Check period validation (only closed periods blocked)
test_cases.expected_result: Future dates accepted without restriction. Only closed-period dates rejected. No forward-looking date limit.
test_cases.notes: Known BUG-REPORT-5. May be intentional design.

test_cases.title: Vacation payment — reversed date range accepted
test_cases.preconditions: Valid auth.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /v1/paymentdates?startDate=2026-06-01&endDate=2026-01-01 (end before start)
2. Check response
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: Reversed dates accepted without validation. Response may contain incorrect payment dates or unexpected results.
test_cases.notes: Known accounting bug.

test_cases.title: Day-off past personalDate accepted
test_cases.preconditions: Valid auth, existing public holiday.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH /v1/employee-dayOff/{id} with personalDate=2026-02-01 (past date)
2. Check response
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: Past date accepted. No server-side validation that personalDate >= today. May allow backdating compensatory days.
test_cases.notes: Known BUG-DO-4.

test_cases.title: Day-off weekend personalDate accepted
test_cases.preconditions: Valid auth.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /v1/employee-dayOff with personalDate on Saturday/Sunday
2. Check response
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: Weekend date accepted. No working-day validation. Employee could take compensatory day on non-working day.
test_cases.notes: Known BUG-DO-5.

test_cases.title: Period PATCH with null start — NPE
test_cases.preconditions: Admin/accountant auth.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH /v1/offices/{id}/periods/report with body {} (null start)
2. Check response
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: 500 NullPointerException. DTO has @NotNull annotation but @Valid missing on @RequestBody. Server crashes instead of 400 validation error.
test_cases.notes: Known period bug. @NotNull present but @Valid missing.

test_cases.title: Approve period — first-day-of-month validation missing
test_cases.preconditions: Admin/accountant auth.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH /v1/offices/{id}/periods/approve with start=2026-03-15 (mid-month)
2. Compare with PATCH periods/report with same date — expect 400
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: Approve period accepts any day of month. Report period validates first-day-of-month. Inconsistent validation between symmetric endpoints.
test_cases.notes: Known period bug-1. Report validates at line 91, approve missing at line 104.

test_cases.title: Day-off findAll without type parameter — NPE
test_cases.preconditions: Valid auth.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /v1/employee-dayOff without type parameter
2. Check response
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: 500 NullPointerException at EmployeeDayOffSearchServiceImpl.java:134. ordinal() called on null EmployeeDayOffTypeFilter. Server crashes on missing optional parameter.
test_cases.notes: Known BUG-DO-1. NPE on null enum.

test_cases.title: Day-off list endpoint — NPE in cache
test_cases.preconditions: Valid auth.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /v1/employee-dayOff/list
2. Check response
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: 500 NPE in Caffeine cache computeIfAbsent (InternalEmployeeService.java:160). Null key passed to cache.
test_cases.notes: Known BUG-DO-2.

test_cases.title: Vacation create — null paymentMonth causes NPE
test_cases.preconditions: Valid auth.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /v1/vacations with paymentMonth omitted (null)
2. Check response
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: NPE at VacationAvailablePaidDaysCalculatorImpl.java:73 — paymentDate.getYear() on null. Field documented as optional but effectively required.
test_cases.notes: Known BUG-VACATION-2.

test_cases.title: Vacation create — null optionalApprovers causes NPE
test_cases.preconditions: Valid auth.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /v1/vacations without optionalApprovers field (null)
2. Check response
test_cases.expected_result: BUG: NPE at VacationServiceImpl.java:155 — getOptionalApprovers().add() on null list. Service assumes list is initialized.
test_cases.notes: Known BUG-VACATION-3.

test_cases.title: SQL injection attempt on search endpoints
test_cases.preconditions: Valid auth.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /v1/suggest/employees?query='; DROP TABLE employee;--
2. GET /v1/reports?executorLogin='; SELECT 1;--
3. Check responses for DB errors
test_cases.expected_result: No SQL injection. Spring Data JPA uses parameterized queries. Verify: no raw SQL concatenation, proper escaping, no DB error messages in response.
test_cases.notes: Standard OWASP check. JPA should prevent, but verify.

test_cases.title: XSS attempt in comment/reason fields
test_cases.preconditions: Valid auth.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /v1/reports with comment='<script>alert(1)</script>'
2. POST /v1/vacations with comment containing HTML tags
3. View data in UI — check if script executes
test_cases.expected_result: No XSS execution. React auto-escapes JSX output. Verify: stored data is rendered safely, no dangerouslySetInnerHTML on user data.
test_cases.notes: React provides default XSS protection via JSX escaping.

test_cases.title: Create sick leave with minimum required fields
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as employee. No active sick leaves overlapping target dates.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves
2. Body: {login: <own>, startDate: '2026-03-16', endDate: '2026-03-19', force: false}
3. Verify response status 200
4. GET /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id} to confirm
test_cases.expected_result:
Sick leave created with status=OPEN, accounting_status=NEW.
total_days calculated (4 calendar days).
work_days calculated from production calendar.
Appears in employee's /sick-leave/my list.
test_cases.notes: Core happy path. DB: SELECT * FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave WHERE id=<new_id>

test_cases.title: Create sick leave with all optional fields
test_cases.preconditions:
Logged in as employee.
2 valid colleague logins for notifyAlso.
1 uploaded file UUID from POST /v1/files/upload.
test_cases.expected_result:
Sick leave created with number, 1 file attached (sick_leave_file junction),
2 notifyAlso recipients (sick_leave_notify_also records).
All fields visible in detail modal.
test_cases.notes: Verify file and notifyAlso via DB queries

test_cases.title: Calendar days auto-calculation (total_days = endDate - startDate + 1)
test_cases.preconditions: Create modal open.
test_cases.steps:
1. Select Start date = 2026-03-16 (Monday)
2. Select End date = 2026-03-22 (Sunday)
3. Observe Calendar days field
test_cases.expected_result:
Calendar days shows 7 (all calendar days including weekends).
Field is read-only, auto-updated on date change.
test_cases.notes: total_days = end - start + 1 (calendar, not working)

test_cases.title: Work days auto-calculation excludes weekends per production calendar
test_cases.preconditions: Sick leave created: Mon 2026-03-16 to Sun 2026-03-22 (7 calendar days).
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id}
2. Check work_days field in response
test_cases.expected_result:
work_days = 5 (Mon-Fri, excluding Sat/Sun per office production calendar).
Uses CalendarService for office-specific holiday computation.
test_cases.notes: work_days visible only in accounting view column

test_cases.title: Create single-day sick leave (startDate = endDate)
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as employee.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves
2. Body: {login, startDate: '2026-03-16', endDate: '2026-03-16', force: false}
test_cases.expected_result:
Sick leave created with total_days=1, work_days=1 (if weekday).
Boundary: minimum possible duration.
test_cases.notes: Frontend: selecting start auto-fills end with same date

test_cases.title: Create long-duration sick leave (>30 days, no upper limit)
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as employee.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves
2. Body: {login, startDate: '2026-01-01', endDate: '2026-04-30', force: false}
test_cases.expected_result:
Sick leave created successfully. total_days=120.
No maximum duration validation exists.
DB shows max observed = 140-141 days in production data.
test_cases.notes: No upper limit on duration. Boundary: extreme values.

test_cases.title: Create future sick leave -- computed SCHEDULED status
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as employee. Start date > today.
test_cases.expected_result:
DB stores status=OPEN.
API returns computed status=SCHEDULED (CASE WHEN in SQL query).
UI shows State = 'Planned'.
test_cases.notes: Computed at query time, never persisted

test_cases.title: Edit sick leave -- change dates, days recalculated
test_cases.preconditions: Existing OPEN sick leave.
test_cases.expected_result:
Dates updated. total_days and work_days recalculated.
NOTIFY_SICKLEAVE_DATES_CHANGED email sent.
test_cases.notes: PatchDTO extends CreateDTO -- all create validations re-run

test_cases.title: Edit sick leave -- add/change certificate number
test_cases.preconditions: Existing OPEN sick leave without number.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id}
2. Body: {login, startDate, endDate, force: false, number: 'BL-123'}
test_cases.expected_result:
Number field updated. NOTIFY_SICKLEAVE_NUMBER_CHANGED email sent.
Number visible in detail modal and accounting view.
test_cases.notes: Number optional until close

test_cases.title: Edit sick leave -- add files (two-step update)
test_cases.preconditions: Existing sick leave with no attachments.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/files/upload (multipart, 2 PDFs)
2. PATCH /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id} with filesIds: [uuid1, uuid2]
3. Verify file junction records
test_cases.notes: Two-step: upload then patch. FilesAddedEvent on patch only.

test_cases.title: Edit sick leave -- remove file (diff-and-sync)
test_cases.preconditions: Existing sick leave with 2 file attachments.
test_cases.expected_result:
Diff-and-sync: removed file's junction record deleted.
Remaining file intact. Original file record NOT deleted (only junction).

test_cases.title: Upload maximum 5 files -- boundary
test_cases.preconditions: Create sick leave with 5 file UUIDs.
test_cases.steps:
1. Upload 5 files via POST /v1/files/upload
2. POST /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves with filesIds: [5 UUIDs]
test_cases.expected_result: All 5 files accepted. @Size(max=5) on filesIds passes.
test_cases.notes: Max 5 files per sick leave

test_cases.title: Upload 6th file -- @Size(max=5) violation
test_cases.preconditions: 6 uploaded file UUIDs.
test_cases.steps: 1. POST /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves with filesIds: [6 UUIDs]
test_cases.notes: BUG note: @Size on DTO but NOT enforced in service layer

test_cases.title: Upload non-allowed file type via API -- no backend MIME check
test_cases.preconditions: Bypass frontend. Use curl directly.
test_cases.steps:
1. curl -X POST /api/vacation/v1/files/upload -F 'file=@malicious.exe'
2. Include returned UUID in sick leave PATCH
test_cases.expected_result:
SECURITY: Backend accepts ANY file type.
No MIME type validation on server side.
Only frontend checks file types (PDF, PNG, JPEG).
test_cases.notes: No backend MIME validation -- potential malicious upload vector

test_cases.title: Close (end) sick leave -- number required for close
test_cases.preconditions: Existing OPEN sick leave.
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400: exception.validation.sickLeave.number.empty
Number is required when closing.
Frontend enforces via Yup schema mode switch.
test_cases.notes: Number becomes required (*) during close flow

test_cases.title: Close sick leave -- with valid number
test_cases.preconditions: Existing OPEN sick leave.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id}
2. Body: {login, startDate, endDate, force: false, status: 'CLOSED', number: 'BL-CLOSE-001'}
test_cases.expected_result:
Status changes to CLOSED. State shows 'Ended'.
NOTIFY_SICKLEAVE_CLOSED email sent.
Number stored permanently.
test_cases.notes: Core close flow

test_cases.title: Close dialog title bug -- says 'Delete' instead of 'End'
test_cases.preconditions: Existing OPEN sick leave. UI close flow.
test_cases.steps:
1. Click end/close action button on sick leave row
2. Observe dialog title text
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: Dialog title says 'Delete the sick note?' instead of 'End the sick note?'.
Functionally closes (not deletes). Confusing UX.
test_cases.notes: Known bug from session 31 UI exploration

test_cases.title: Reopen closed sick leave via PATCH status=OPEN
test_cases.preconditions: Existing CLOSED sick leave (accounting_status=NEW).
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id}
2. Body: {login, startDate, endDate, force: false, status: 'OPEN'}
test_cases.expected_result:
Status changes back to OPEN.
State recalculated based on dates (Started/Overdue/Planned).
No guard conditions on reopen.
test_cases.notes: Direct status overwrite, no intermediate validation

test_cases.title: Delete sick leave -- soft delete (status=DELETED)
test_cases.preconditions: Existing non-PAID sick leave.
test_cases.expected_result:
Soft delete: status=DELETED in DB. Record not physically removed.
NOTIFY_SICKLEAVE_DELETE email sent.
Row disappears from default list view.
test_cases.notes: Soft-delete only

test_cases.title: Delete PAID sick leave -- blocked
test_cases.preconditions: Sick leave with accounting_status=PAID.
test_cases.steps:
1. DELETE /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id}
2. Check error response
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400: exception.validation.sickLeave.delete.closed
Cannot delete PAID sick leave. Guard in service layer.
test_cases.notes: Guard: accounting_status != PAID

test_cases.title: Delete leaves orphaned file and notifyAlso records
test_cases.preconditions: Sick leave with 2 files + 2 notifyAlso recipients.
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: Junction records remain orphaned after soft-delete.
sick_leave_file and sick_leave_notify_also NOT cleaned up.
Only status changed to DELETED.
test_cases.notes: Known tech debt: orphaned junction records

test_cases.title: Create overlapping sick leave -- force=false (409 Conflict)
test_cases.preconditions: Existing OPEN sick leave: 2026-03-10 to 2026-03-15.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves
2. Body: {login, startDate: '2026-03-12', endDate: '2026-03-18', force: false}
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 409: exception.validation.sickLeave.dates.crossing
Frontend shows overlap dialog with force/cancel options.
test_cases.notes: Overlap detected against active (non-DELETED, non-REJECTED) sick leaves

test_cases.title: Create overlapping sick leave -- force=true (allowed)
test_cases.preconditions: Existing overlap detected. Overlap dialog shown.
test_cases.steps:
1. Resend POST with force=true
2. Verify creation succeeds
test_cases.expected_result:
Sick leave created despite overlap.
Both active sick leaves coexist in DB.

test_cases.title: Create sick leave crossing active vacation -- SickLeaveCrossingVacationException
test_cases.preconditions: Employee has active vacation 2026-03-10 to 2026-03-20.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves
2. Body: {login, startDate: '2026-03-12', endDate: '2026-03-18', force: false}
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 409: exception.sick.leave.crossing.vacation
If force=true: creates and publishes VacationOverlap event.
NOTIFY_EMPLOYEE_SICKLEAVE_OVERLAPS_VACATION email sent.
test_cases.notes: Vacation crossing check separate from sick leave overlap check

test_cases.title: Notification: create -- NOTIFY_SICKLEAVE_OPEN (employee creates own)
test_cases.preconditions: Employee creates own sick leave.
test_cases.steps:
1. Create sick leave as employee
2. Check email via GET /api/email/v1/emails?to=<manager>
test_cases.expected_result:
NOTIFY_SICKLEAVE_OPEN template sent.
Recipients: employee's manager + notifyAlso + per-office receivers.
test_cases.notes: Async after @TransactionalEventListener

test_cases.title: Notification: create by supervisor -- different template
test_cases.preconditions: Manager creates sick leave for subordinate.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves as manager with subordinate's login
2. Check email template used
test_cases.expected_result:
NOTIFY_SICKLEAVE_OPEN_BY_SUPERVISOR template sent (not OPEN).
Editor type should be SUPERVISOR but BUG: getEditorType() uses ==.
test_cases.notes: Editor type determines template variant. See TC-SL-108 for bug.

test_cases.title: Notification: close -- NOTIFY_SICKLEAVE_CLOSED
test_cases.preconditions: Close an OPEN sick leave with number.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH status=CLOSED with number
2. Check email
test_cases.expected_result:
NOTIFY_SICKLEAVE_CLOSED template sent.
Includes sick leave details and closing number.
test_cases.notes: Chain-of-responsibility dispatch

test_cases.title: Notification: dates changed -- NOTIFY_SICKLEAVE_DATES_CHANGED
test_cases.preconditions: Edit sick leave dates.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH with changed endDate
2. Check email
test_cases.expected_result:
NOTIFY_SICKLEAVE_DATES_CHANGED template sent.
Includes old and new date values.

test_cases.title: Notification: files added -- triggered on PATCH only, not create
test_cases.preconditions: Add files to existing sick leave.
test_cases.steps:
1. Upload file, PATCH with filesIds
2. Check email
3. Create NEW sick leave with filesIds
4. Check if FilesAdded email sent
test_cases.expected_result:
PATCH: NOTIFY_SICKLEAVE_FILES_ADDED sent.
CREATE: no FilesAdded notification (only SickLeaveCreatedEvent).
FilesAddedEvent published only on patch path.
test_cases.notes: Asymmetric: files on create do NOT trigger FilesAdded notification

test_cases.title: Notification: delete -- NOTIFY_SICKLEAVE_DELETE
test_cases.preconditions: Delete a sick leave.
test_cases.steps:
1. DELETE /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id}
2. Check email
test_cases.expected_result:
NOTIFY_SICKLEAVE_DELETE template sent.
Variant: _BY_SUPERVISOR if manager deletes.

test_cases.title: Notification recipients -- full list verification
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with manager, 2 notifyAlso, per-office receivers configured.
test_cases.steps:
1. Create sick leave with notifyAlso: ['colleague1', 'colleague2']
2. Query: SELECT * FROM ttt_vacation.office_sick_leave_notification_receiver
3. Verify all email recipients
test_cases.expected_result:
Email sent to: employee's manager + 2 notifyAlso + per-office receivers.
All receive same template.
test_cases.notes: Per-office receivers configured in admin panel

test_cases.title: Initial status on creation -- OPEN + NEW
test_cases.preconditions: Create a new sick leave.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves (valid body)
2. SELECT status, accounting_status FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave WHERE id={new_id}
test_cases.expected_result:
status='OPEN', accounting_status='NEW'.
Employee view shows State = 'Started' (if current dates).

test_cases.title: Computed SCHEDULED -- future start_date, OPEN in DB
test_cases.preconditions: OPEN sick leave with start_date > today.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves?statuses=SCHEDULED
2. Verify returned records
test_cases.expected_result:
Records with OPEN + future start_date returned as status=SCHEDULED.
UI shows State = 'Planned'.
Computed via CASE WHEN in SQL query, never stored.

test_cases.title: Computed OVERDUE -- past end_date, OPEN in DB
test_cases.preconditions: OPEN sick leave with end_date < today.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves?statuses=OVERDUE
2. Check UI manager view
test_cases.expected_result:
Records with OPEN + past end_date returned as status=OVERDUE.
UI shows State = 'Overdue'. Green checkmark action in manager view.
OverdueSickLeaveCommand per-request check.

test_cases.title: Read/write asymmetry -- PATCH rejects SCHEDULED and OVERDUE
test_cases.preconditions: OPEN sick leave with computed status SCHEDULED.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id} with status=SCHEDULED
2. PATCH with status=OVERDUE
test_cases.expected_result:
Error: SCHEDULED and OVERDUE are not valid write values.
Only OPEN and CLOSED accepted for PATCH status field.
SickLeavePatchRequestStatusTypeDTO enum has only OPEN/CLOSED.
test_cases.notes: Design debt: computed statuses not writable

test_cases.title: Accounting: NEW -> PROCESSING transition
test_cases.preconditions: Sick leave with accounting_status=NEW. Logged in as accountant.
test_cases.steps:
1. Open /accounting/sick-leaves
2. Click Status dropdown on row
3. Select 'Pending' (=PROCESSING)
test_cases.expected_result:
accounting_status updated to PROCESSING.
accountant FK set to current user.
Main status remains OPEN.
test_cases.notes: Pending in UI = PROCESSING in DB

test_cases.title: Accounting: PROCESSING -> PAID -- auto-closes main status
test_cases.preconditions: Sick leave: status=OPEN, accounting_status=PROCESSING.
test_cases.steps:
1. Change Status dropdown to 'Paid'
2. Verify both statuses via API and DB
test_cases.expected_result:
accounting_status = PAID.
Main status auto-changed to CLOSED (coupling rule).
State shows 'Ended'.
test_cases.notes: CRITICAL coupling: PAID -> auto-close

test_cases.title: Accounting: PROCESSING -> REJECTED -- sets main REJECTED
test_cases.preconditions: Sick leave: status=OPEN, accounting_status=PROCESSING.
test_cases.steps:
1. Change Status dropdown to 'Rejected'
2. Verify both statuses
test_cases.expected_result:
accounting_status = REJECTED.
Main status auto-changed to REJECTED.
NOTIFY_SICKLEAVE_REJECTED email sent.
test_cases.notes: Coupling: REJECTED -> main REJECTED

test_cases.title: Accounting: NEW/PROCESSING on closed SL reopens to OPEN
test_cases.preconditions: Sick leave: status=CLOSED, accounting_status=NEW.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH accounting_status to PROCESSING
2. Verify main status
test_cases.expected_result:
accounting_status = PROCESSING.
Main status changes from CLOSED to OPEN (reopened).
Coupling: NEW/PROCESSING -> main status forced to OPEN.
test_cases.notes: Unexpected reopen behavior

test_cases.title: BUG: Unrestricted PAID -> NEW transition (no guardrails)
test_cases.preconditions: Sick leave: accounting_status=PAID (main=CLOSED).
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: Transition succeeds. accounting_status=NEW.
Main status may remain CLOSED (inconsistent orphaned state).
No state machine guardrails prevent backward transitions.
test_cases.notes: Any-to-any accounting transitions allowed

test_cases.title: BUG: REJECTED -> PAID transition (skips PROCESSING)
test_cases.preconditions: Sick leave: accounting_status=REJECTED, status=REJECTED.
test_cases.steps:
1. Change Status dropdown from Rejected to Paid
2. Verify statuses
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: Transition succeeds. REJECTED -> PAID.
Main status auto-set to CLOSED (coupling rule).
No intermediate PROCESSING required.

test_cases.title: Status combination matrix -- valid data patterns
test_cases.preconditions: Database access to timemachine.
test_cases.steps:
1. SELECT status, accounting_status, COUNT(*) FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave GROUP BY 1,2 ORDER BY 3 DESC
2. Compare with expected combinations
test_cases.expected_result:
Valid combinations:
- OPEN/NEW: active, not processed
- CLOSED/NEW: ended but never processed (62% backlog)
- CLOSED/PAID: fully processed
- REJECTED/REJECTED: both rejected
- DELETED/NEW: soft-deleted
Total: 348 records in timemachine.
test_cases.notes: Data quality observation

test_cases.title: Employee view -- State column, no accounting Status visible

Employees cannot see accounting status.

Managers see but cannot change accounting status.

test_cases.title: BUG: Status filter shows 'Rejected Rejected' duplicate label

10 columns: Employee, Sick leave dates, Days, Work days,
Sick note, Accountant, Salary office, State, Status, Actions.

test_cases.title: BUG: No creation permission check -- any user for any employee
test_cases.preconditions: API access as any authenticated user.

2. Use JWT of user who is NOT manager/TL/accountant of target employee

test_cases.title: BUG: No owner check on delete -- any user can delete any non-PAID SL
test_cases.preconditions: API access. Two users: A (owner) and B (unrelated).
test_cases.steps:
1. Create sick leave as user A
2. DELETE /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id} as user B
test_cases.expected_result:
SECURITY BUG: Delete succeeds. No owner check.
Any AUTHENTICATED_USER can delete any non-PAID sick leave.
Only guard: cannot delete PAID.
test_cases.notes: Missing authorization on delete endpoint

test_cases.title: BUG: No PM check on delete -- PM from other project can delete
test_cases.preconditions: PM from project X. Sick leave owned by employee in project Y.
test_cases.steps: 1. DELETE /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id} as unrelated PM
test_cases.expected_result:
SECURITY BUG: Delete succeeds.
PM authorization not checked on delete path.
Contrast with update path which does check PM access.
test_cases.notes: Delete path skips PM access check that update path enforces

test_cases.title: PM access check on update -- VacationSecurityException if not related
test_cases.preconditions: PM who is NOT employee's manager/TL/accountant.
test_cases.steps: 1. PATCH /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id} as unrelated PM
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 403: exception.vacation.no.permission
VacationSecurityException thrown.
Update path correctly checks PM relationship (unlike delete).
test_cases.notes: Shared exception class with vacation module

test_cases.steps: 1. PATCH /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id} with accountingStatus change

Any authenticated user can access the page.

test_cases.title: API token -- CRUD endpoints return 403

SECURITY: 403 Forbidden returned BUT full Java stack trace (90+ frames)
leaked in error response body.

test_cases.title: Start date required -- empty field validation (Yup)

test_cases.steps:

2. Fill End date
3. Try to save
test_cases.expected_result:
Validation error: Start date is required.
Save blocked by Yup schema (create mode).
Backend: @NotNull on startDate.

test_cases.title: End date required -- empty field validation (Yup)

2. Leave End date empty
3. Try to save

Validation error: End date is required.
Save blocked by Yup schema.
Backend: @NotNull on endDate.

test_cases.title: Start date > end date -- backend error code
test_cases.preconditions: Bypass frontend or use API directly.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves
2. Body: {login, startDate: '2026-03-20', endDate: '2026-03-15', force: false}
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400: validation.sickLeave.dates.order
SickLeaveCreateValidator checks startDate <= endDate.
Same-day allowed (start = end).
test_cases.notes: Frontend auto-corrects (end < start -> start = end) so rarely hits backend

test_cases.title: Frontend auto-adjustment: end < start -> start = end
test_cases.preconditions: Create modal with Start date already set.
test_cases.steps:
1. Select End date earlier than Start date
2. Observe Start date field
test_cases.expected_result:
Start date auto-adjusts to match End date.
No validation error shown -- UI auto-corrects.
Calendar days = 1 after adjustment.
test_cases.notes: Confirmed behavior: prevents date order error at UI level

test_cases.title: Start date auto-fills end date on first selection
test_cases.preconditions: Create modal open, no dates selected.
test_cases.steps:
1. Select Start date = 2026-03-16
2. Check End date field
test_cases.expected_result:
End date auto-fills with same value (2026-03-16).
Calendar days = 1.
test_cases.notes: Convenience auto-fill

test_cases.title: Calendar days live recalculation on date change
test_cases.preconditions: Create modal with dates set.
test_cases.steps:
1. Set Start=2026-03-01, End=2026-03-10 (10 days)
2. Change End to 2026-03-15
3. Observe Calendar days
test_cases.expected_result:
Calendar days updates live: 10 -> 15.
Read-only field, auto-calculated as endDate - startDate + 1.

test_cases.title: Number field -- max 40 characters (@Size(max=40))
test_cases.preconditions: Create/edit modal.
test_cases.steps:
1. Enter 41-character string in Number field
2. Save via API: PATCH with number='A' * 41
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400 validation error: number exceeds max length 40.
Backend: @Size(max=40) on SickLeaveCreateRequestDTO.number.
Frontend may truncate or show client-side error.
test_cases.notes: Gap: frontend validates max 40 but backend @Size(max=40) exists

test_cases.title: Number field -- exactly 40 characters (boundary)
test_cases.preconditions: API test.
test_cases.steps: 1. PATCH with number = 'A' * 40
test_cases.expected_result:
Request succeeds. Number stored as exactly 40 characters.
Boundary: at maximum allowed length.

test_cases.title: Number required on close -- not on create (Yup mode switch)
test_cases.preconditions: Create modal and Close dialog.
test_cases.steps:
1. Create sick leave WITHOUT number -> observe field is optional (no asterisk)
2. Open close dialog for same sick leave -> observe field is required (asterisk)
test_cases.expected_result:
Create: number optional. Close: number required (asterisk).
Yup switches validation mode based on isCloseMode prop.
Backend: exception.validation.sickLeave.number.empty on close without number.
test_cases.notes: Mode-dependent validation

test_cases.title: Number whitespace trimming
test_cases.preconditions: Edit modal with Number field.
test_cases.steps:

2. GET and verify stored value
test_cases.expected_result:
Number stored as 'BL-123' (trimmed).
Backend trims whitespace.

test_cases.title: Overlap detection -- only active sick leaves checked
test_cases.preconditions: 1 OPEN + 1 DELETED sick leave covering same dates.
test_cases.steps:
1. Create new sick leave overlapping both
2. Observe overlap check result
test_cases.expected_result:
Overlap check only considers active sick leaves (OPEN, CLOSED).
DELETED and REJECTED excluded from overlap detection.

test_cases.title: BUG: Manager overlap bypass -- client-side 100-record cap + force=true
test_cases.preconditions: Manager creating sick leave for employee with many existing records.
test_cases.steps:
1. Manager creates sick leave (overlap exists beyond first 100 records)
2. Monitor API request: force=true hardcoded
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: Client-side overlap pre-fetches max 100 records.
If overlap with record #101+ it is missed.
force=true hardcoded bypasses backend check too.
Result: no overlap validation for manager-created sick leaves.
test_cases.notes: Combined bugs: 100-record cap + force=true

test_cases.title: Vacation crossing -- force=false triggers 409, force=true creates with event
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with active vacation overlapping target dates.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST with force=false -> expect 409: exception.sick.leave.crossing.vacation
2. Resend with force=true -> expect 200
test_cases.expected_result:
force=false: HTTP 409 SickLeaveCrossingVacationException.
force=true: Created + VacationOverlap event published.
NOTIFY_EMPLOYEE_SICKLEAVE_OVERLAPS_VACATION sent.

test_cases.title: BUG: Vacation overlap dialog re-triggers on every save
test_cases.preconditions: Sick leave overlapping vacation. Overlap dialog already dismissed.
test_cases.steps:
1. Create sick leave overlapping vacation -> dismiss overlap dialog
2. Edit same sick leave (no date change)
3. Save
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: Overlap dialog re-triggers on every save (create AND edit).
Not just when dates actually change/overlap.
Nuisance dialog on every edit.

test_cases.title: Date picker -- readonly inputs, calendar widget only
test_cases.preconditions: Create modal date pickers.
test_cases.steps:

2. Click date picker calendar instead
test_cases.expected_result:
Input fields are readonly -- must use calendar widget.
No keyboard date entry possible.
<input readonly> attribute confirmed.
test_cases.notes: Verified via Playwright: <input readonly>

test_cases.title: force flag @NotNull on create -- must be explicitly set

1. POST /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves
2. Body: {login, startDate, endDate} -- omit force field
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400: @NotNull validation error on force field.
force is required Boolean, not optional.
Frontend always sends force=false initially.
test_cases.notes: Risk: NPE if @NotNull somehow bypassed at deserialization

test_cases.title: Error: validation.sickLeave.dates.order (startDate > endDate)

errorCode: validation.sickLeave.dates.order

errorCode: exception.validation.sickLeave.dates.crossing

HTTP 409
errorCode: exception.sick.leave.crossing.vacation

test_cases.title: Error: exception.vacation.no.permission (shared with vacation module)

errorCode: exception.vacation.no.permission
VacationSecurityException -- shared class with vacation module.

test_cases.title: BUG: getEditorType() == identity comparison always false
test_cases.preconditions: Create sick leave as employee. Check notification dispatch.
test_cases.steps:
1. Create sick leave as employee
2. Inspect notification template via email API or logs
3. Check if OPEN or OPEN_BY_SUPERVISOR template used
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: getEditorType() uses == (reference equality) on BO instances.
`employee == currentEmployee` always false for separately loaded objects.
Self-edits classified as ACCOUNTANT/SUPERVISOR instead of EMPLOYEE.
Wrong notification template variant selected.
test_cases.notes: Should use .equals() instead of ==

test_cases.title: BUG: PatchDTO extends CreateDTO -- all create validations re-run on patch
test_cases.preconditions: Existing sick leave. PATCH with minimal change.
test_cases.expected_result:
Design issue: PatchDTO extends CreateDTO.
All create-time validations (@NotNull login, @NotNull force, etc.)
re-run on every patch. Must send full create payload even for minor edits.
force @NotNull on patch is particularly awkward.
test_cases.notes: Inheritance forces full payload on every patch

test_cases.title: BUG: Accounting status can reverse lifecycle (NEW after PAID -> OPEN)
test_cases.preconditions: Sick leave: accounting_status=PAID, status=CLOSED.
test_cases.steps:
1. Change accounting_status from PAID to NEW via accountant dropdown
2. Observe main status
test_cases.expected_result:
BUG: accounting_status reverts to NEW.
Coupling rule: NEW/PROCESSING -> main status forced to OPEN.
Result: was PAID/CLOSED, now NEW/OPEN -- fully reversed lifecycle.
No state machine prevents this backward transition.
test_cases.notes: Critical: lifecycle can be fully reversed
