@FileUuidExists custom validator rejects non-existent UUID.

test_cases.title: Notification: REJECTED -- NOTIFY_SICKLEAVE_REJECTED on accounting reject
test_cases.preconditions: Accountant rejects sick leave.
test_cases.steps:
1. Change accounting_status to REJECTED
2. Check email via GET /api/email/v1/emails
test_cases.expected_result:
NOTIFY_SICKLEAVE_REJECTED template sent.
Main status also changes to REJECTED.
Dual status coupling in action.

test_cases.title: Chain-of-responsibility notification -- multi-field edit
test_cases.preconditions: Edit sick leave changing both dates AND number.
test_cases.steps:
1. PATCH with changed endDate + changed number
2. Check emails sent
test_cases.expected_result:
Chain dispatches both DATES_CHANGED and NUMBER_CHANGED notifications.
Two separate emails (not combined).
Handler chain pattern in notification service.

test_cases.title: Concurrent update -- race condition on accounting status change
test_cases.preconditions: Two accountants viewing same sick leave.
test_cases.steps:
1. Accountant A changes status to PROCESSING
2. Accountant B simultaneously changes status to PROCESSING
3. Check final state and accountant FK
test_cases.expected_result:
Last-write-wins. No optimistic locking.
accountant FK points to whichever write completed last.
No write lock acquired for sick leave updates (unlike vacation).
test_cases.notes: No findByIdAndAcquireWriteLock pattern on sick leave

test_cases.title: Open/overdue count endpoint -- used for sidebar badge

test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/count
2. Cross-reference with DB: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave WHERE status='OPEN' AND accounting_status NOT IN ('PAID','REJECTED')
test_cases.expected_result:
Count matches DB query for active, unprocessed sick leaves.
Badge shows on sidebar for accountant/manager views.

Unit mapping verified:
  /reports/summary: HOURS
  /reports/total: MINUTES
  /reports/effort: MINUTES
  /reports/employees-over-reported: HOURS
  /statistic/* tree endpoints: MINUTES
  /statistic/export/*: HOURS
  DB statistic_report: HOURS
  DB task_report.actual_efforts: MINUTES

test_cases.title: Cross-environment field comparison — TM vs Stage

2. GET same on stage
3. Compare field sets and values
test_cases.expected_result:
Field differences:
  Stage has 'id' and 'normForDate' — TM does not.
  TM: 15 fields, Stage: 17 fields.
Decimal: TM=3 places (223.250), Stage=2 (223.25).
Russian name format: TM='Last First', Stage='First Last'.
Data values (effort, norm): identical.
test_cases.notes: release/2.1 removed id + normForDate from response vs stage branch

test_cases.title: GET /v1/reports/effort — no auth required (security bug)

Verify whether endpoint requires authentication.
Expected: HTTP 401 Unauthorized without auth.
If HTTP 200: SECURITY BUG — endpoint accessible without auth.
test_cases.notes: Check @PreAuthorize or security config for this endpoint

test_cases.title: Statistics export endpoint — CSV format verification
test_cases.preconditions: API key auth.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/ttt/v1/statistic/export/employees
   With startDate, endDate params
2. Verify CSV format, headers, data types

HTTP 200. Content-Type: text/csv.
Headers: EmployeeLogin, EmployeeName, Contractor,
  DepartmentManagerLogin, DepartmentManagerName,

10 export endpoints under /v1/statistic/export/.

test_cases.title: Personal norm — employee with no absences
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with no vacations, sick leaves, or day-offs in target month.
Office calendar configured with working hours.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /v1/statistic/report/employees for target employee/month
2. Compare 'norm' field with office calendar working hours
3. Verify via DB: SELECT * FROM statistic_report
   WHERE employee_login = ? AND report_date = ?
test_cases.expected_result:
personalNorm = totalNorm (office calendar hours for month).
budgetNorm = totalNorm (same, since no admin vacation).
No subtraction. personalNorm == budgetNorm.
Example: 22 working days * 8h = 176 hours.

test_cases.title: Personal norm — employee with regular vacation
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with approved paid vacation (5 working days) in target month.
test_cases.steps:
1. Verify vacation exists via vacation service
2. GET /v1/statistic/report/employees for employee
3. Calculate expected: monthNorm - (5 * 8) = monthNorm - 40
test_cases.expected_result:
personalNorm = monthNorm - 40 (5 days * 8h subtracted).
budgetNorm = monthNorm - 40 (paid vacation subtracted from both).
personalNorm == budgetNorm for paid vacation.
Off-periods from vacation service merged before calculation.

test_cases.title: Personal norm — employee with admin (unpaid) vacation
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with administrative (unpaid) vacation in target month.
test_cases.steps:
1. Verify admin vacation exists
2. GET /v1/statistic/report/employees
3. Compare personalNorm vs budgetNorm
test_cases.expected_result:
personalNorm = monthNorm - adminVacationHours (subtracted).
budgetNorm = monthNorm (admin vacation NOT subtracted from budget).
KEY DIFFERENCE: personalNorm < budgetNorm.
Budget norm filters out administrative vacations before building off-periods.
test_cases.notes: Core business rule: admin vacation affects personal but not budget norm

test_cases.title: Personal norm — employee with sick leave
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with sick leave in target month.
test_cases.steps:
1. Verify sick leave exists via vacation service
2. GET /v1/statistic/report/employees
3. Calculate expected norm reduction
test_cases.expected_result:
personalNorm = monthNorm - sickLeaveHours.
budgetNorm = monthNorm - sickLeaveHours.
Sick leave treated same as paid vacation for norm calculation.
Both norms reduced equally.

test_cases.title: Personal norm — overlapping absences merged
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with overlapping vacation and sick leave periods.
test_cases.steps:
1. Create scenario: vacation Mar 10-15, sick leave Mar 13-18
2. GET norm for March
3. Verify overlapping days NOT double-counted
test_cases.expected_result:
Overlapping off-periods merged before subtraction.
Merged range: Mar 10-18 (9 calendar days, ~6 working days).
personalNorm = monthNorm - mergedWorkingHours.
No double-counting of Mar 13-15.
test_cases.notes: Merging logic in BaseStatistic.mergeOverlappingPeriods

test_cases.title: Personal norm — clamped to employee work period
test_cases.preconditions: Employee hired mid-month (e.g. effectiveDate = March 15).
test_cases.steps:
1. GET norm for March for mid-month hire
2. Verify norm covers only working days from hire date
test_cases.expected_result:
personalNorm = working hours from Mar 15 to Mar 31 only.
Clamped by effectiveBounds (start = max(periodStart, hireDate),
  end = min(periodEnd, terminationDate)).
Pro-rated norm: ~50% of full month for mid-month hire.
test_cases.notes: Step 1 of norm calc: clamp date range to employee work period

test_cases.title: Budget norm — admin vacation excluded from off-periods
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with both paid and admin vacation in same month.
test_cases.steps:
1. Verify both vacation types exist
2. GET norm data
3. Verify: personalNorm includes both, budgetNorm excludes admin
test_cases.expected_result:
personalNorm subtracted by: paid vac + admin vac + sick leave.
budgetNorm subtracted by: paid vac + sick leave ONLY.
Difference = admin vacation hours.
Code path: budgetNorm filters ADMINISTRATIVE type before building off-periods.

test_cases.title: Deviation formula verification
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with known reported and budgetNorm values.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /v1/statistic/report/employees for employee
2. Note: reported, budgetNorm, excess values
3. Verify: excess = (reported - budgetNorm) / budgetNorm * 100
test_cases.expected_result:
Formula: (reported - budgetNorm) / budgetNorm * 100.
ExcessStatus:
  HIGH: excess > 0% (over-reported)
  LOW: excess < 0% (under-reported)
  NEUTRAL: excess == 0%
  NA: budgetNorm == 0
NOTE: Uses budgetNorm, not personalNorm (design issue #3).
test_cases.notes: Design issue: admin vacation employee penalized in deviation calc

test_cases.title: budgetNorm null fallback to monthNorm
test_cases.preconditions: Scenario where budgetNorm is null in statistic_report table.
test_cases.steps:
1. Check DB for records with budget_norm IS NULL
2. GET /v1/statistic/report/employees for those employees
3. Verify API returns monthNorm as fallback
test_cases.expected_result:
When budgetNorm is null, service falls back to monthNorm.
API response still shows a valid budgetNorm value.
Design issue #2: silent fallback may mask data problems.
test_cases.notes: budgetNorm null fallback is implicit — no logging or warning

test_cases.title: Norm cache — Caffeine 5min TTL
test_cases.preconditions: Multiple rapid requests for same employee norm.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET norm for employee X
2. Immediately GET again (within 5 min)
3. Wait 6 minutes, GET again
4. Compare response times
test_cases.expected_result:
First request: slower (calendar service call + computation).
Second request (cached): faster response.
After TTL (5 min): cache expired, full recalculation.
Cache: Caffeine in-memory, max 1000 entries.
Cache key: reportingNorm per employee/month.
test_cases.notes: Caffeine TTL 5min, max 1000 entries for calendar service calls

test_cases.title: statistic_report — nightly sync creates/updates records

2. Wait for nightly sync (or trigger via test API)
3. Verify records updated for current + previous month
4. Check last_updated_time timestamps
test_cases.expected_result:
Nightly sync (4:00 AM, ShedLock distributed lock):
  Recalculates current + previous month for ALL employees.
  Deletes records for terminated employees.
  Updates reported_effort, month_norm, budget_norm.
Records count matches active employee count * 2 months.
test_cases.notes: ShedLock prevents duplicate execution across instances

test_cases.title: statistic_report — task report event triggers update

4. Re-check statistic_report.reported_effort
test_cases.notes: Async event: TaskReportEventListener

test_cases.title: statistic_report — RabbitMQ vacation change event
test_cases.preconditions: Vacation created/modified/cancelled for employee.
test_cases.steps:
1. Note employee's statistic_report norms
2. Create/modify vacation via vacation service
3. Wait for MQ event processing
4. Re-check statistic_report norms
test_cases.expected_result:
RabbitMQ event triggers norm recalculation.
Event types: INITIAL_SYNC, VACATION_CHANGES, SICK_LEAVE_CHANGES.
INITIAL_SYNC: deletes extras. Others: upsert only.
month_norm and budget_norm recalculated.
test_cases.notes: MQ topic: TTT_BACKEND_STATISTIC_REPORT

test_cases.title: statistic_report — data consistency with task_report
test_cases.preconditions: Database access to both tables.
test_cases.steps:
1. SELECT SUM(actual_efforts) FROM task_report
   WHERE executor_login = ? AND report_date BETWEEN ? AND ?
2. SELECT reported_effort FROM statistic_report
   WHERE employee_login = ? AND report_date = ?
3. Compare: statistic.reported_effort (HOURS) =
   SUM(task_report.actual_efforts) (MINUTES) / 60
test_cases.expected_result:
reported_effort in statistic_report (HOURS) =
SUM(task_report.actual_efforts) (MINUTES) / 60.
Verified: 152 hours = 9120 minutes.
Any discrepancy indicates sync lag or race condition.

test_cases.title: statistic_report — 2-month sync window limitation
test_cases.preconditions: Nightly sync scope: current + previous month only.
test_cases.steps:
1. Check statistic_report for 3 months ago
2. Modify a task_report from 3 months ago
3. Wait for nightly sync
4. Check if statistic_report updated
test_cases.expected_result:
statistic_report NOT updated for months outside 2-month window.
Historical data frozen after 2-month window passes.
Design issue #4: no historical back-fill mechanism.
Manual intervention required for corrections.
test_cases.notes: 2-month window means retroactive corrections not reflected

test_cases.title: statistic_report — terminated employee cleanup
test_cases.preconditions: Employee terminated (work period ended).
test_cases.steps:
1. Note employee's statistic_report records
2. Trigger nightly sync (or wait)
3. Verify records deleted for terminated employee
test_cases.expected_result:
Nightly sync deletes statistic_report records for
employees no longer active.
INITIAL_SYNC MQ event also removes extras.
Employee disappears from Employee Reports after cleanup.

test_cases.title: Race condition — concurrent MQ + task report event
test_cases.preconditions: Simultaneous vacation change (MQ) and report submission (event).
test_cases.steps:
1. Submit task report while vacation change MQ is processing
2. Check final statistic_report state
3. Verify no data corruption
test_cases.expected_result:
POTENTIAL ISSUE: No pessimistic locking between MQ handler
and task report event handler.
Design issue #1: race condition possible.
Last-write-wins may cause norm or effort to be stale.
Low probability in practice due to async timing.
test_cases.notes: No pessimistic lock — theoretical race condition

test_cases.title: QA-1 environment — no statistic_report table (on-the-fly)
test_cases.preconditions: QA-1 environment access.
test_cases.steps:

test_cases.expected_result:
QA-1: NO statistic_report table (or empty).

Timemachine: table exists (9662+ rows), batch sync.
Both environments return identical data values.
Cache = performance optimization only.
test_cases.notes: Verified in S11 API testing

test_cases.title: Hardcoded CEO login — special handling
test_cases.preconditions: Database access. CEO login = 'ilnitsky'.
test_cases.steps:

3. Check if CEO excluded from certain calculations
test_cases.expected_result:
CEO_LOGIN = 'ilnitsky' hardcoded in BaseStatistic.
Special handling: CEO may be excluded from certain
aggregate calculations or reports.
Design issue #5: hardcoded business logic.
test_cases.notes: Hardcoded: should be config or role-based

2. Verify each returns HTTP 200 with CSV content

test_cases.title: Export — timeUnit parameter for largest-customers
test_cases.preconditions: API key auth.

   ?startDate=...&endDate=...&timeUnit=HOURS
2. GET same with timeUnit=DAYS
3. Compare effort values
test_cases.expected_result:
HOURS: effort in hours (e.g. 152.0).
DAYS: effort in days (e.g. 19.0 = 152/8).
Default: HOURS.

test_cases.title: Effective bounds — employee hired mid-month (prorated personalNorm)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee hired mid-month (e.g., Feb 15).
Find test data:
  SELECT e.login, e.full_name, ewp.first_working_day, ewp.last_working_day
  FROM employee e
  JOIN employee_work_period ewp ON e.id = ewp.employee_id
  WHERE EXTRACT(DAY FROM ewp.first_working_day) > 1
    AND EXTRACT(DAY FROM ewp.first_working_day) <= 20
    AND e.enabled = true
  ORDER BY ewp.first_working_day DESC LIMIT 5;

Identify the month containing the hire date.
Verify employee_work_period record exists for this employee.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/ttt/v1/statistic/report?employeeId={id}&year={Y}&month={M}
   where M = hire month.
2. Check response field personalNorm (or month_norm in DB).
3. Calculate expected: count working days from hire date to end of month
   using production calendar, multiply by 8.
   E.g., Feb 15-28 in Russian calendar = 10 working days x 8h = 80h.
4. DB: SELECT month_norm, budget_norm FROM statistic_report
   WHERE employee_id = {id} AND year = {Y} AND month = {M}.
5. Navigate to Statistics > Employee Reports for the employee.
6. Verify UI displays the prorated norm, not full month norm.
test_cases.expected_result:
personalNorm is prorated to actual working days within effective bounds.
For Feb 15-28 hire: ~80h (not full 160h).
statistic_report.month_norm matches the prorated value.
UI Employee Reports table shows the clamped norm.
My Tasks counter uses the effective-bounded norm for percentage calculation.
test_cases.notes: Core feature: effectiveFrom = max(monthStart, firstWorkingDay). Verify NormForDateCalculator.getEffectiveBounds() applies correctly.

test_cases.title: Effective bounds — employee dismissed mid-month (norm capped at dismissal)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee dismissed mid-month.
Find test data:
  SELECT e.login, e.full_name, ewp.first_working_day, ewp.last_working_day
  FROM employee e
  JOIN employee_work_period ewp ON e.id = ewp.employee_id
  WHERE ewp.last_working_day IS NOT NULL
    AND EXTRACT(DAY FROM ewp.last_working_day) < 28
    AND EXTRACT(DAY FROM ewp.last_working_day) > 1
    AND e.enabled = true
  ORDER BY ewp.last_working_day DESC LIMIT 5;

Identify the month containing the dismissal date.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/ttt/v1/statistic/report?employeeId={id}&year={Y}&month={M}
   where M = dismissal month.
2. Check personalNorm value.
3. Calculate expected: count working days from month start to dismissal date
   using production calendar, multiply by 8.
4. DB: SELECT month_norm FROM statistic_report
   WHERE employee_id = {id} AND year = {Y} AND month = {M}.
5. Compare month_norm with full-month norm for that office.
6. Verify month_norm < full-month norm.
test_cases.expected_result:
personalNorm covers only working days up to dismissal date.
statistic_report.month_norm reflects the truncated range.
E.g., if dismissed Feb 14: norm = working days Feb 1-14 x 8h.
Norm is strictly less than full month norm for the office.
effectiveTo = min(monthEnd, lastWorkingDay) applied correctly.
test_cases.notes: effectiveTo = min(monthEnd, lastWorkingDay). Verify last_working_day from employee_work_period is used, not employee.dismissal_date directly.

test_cases.title: Effective bounds — employee not yet employed for target month (norm = 0)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee hired in a future month relative to the queried month.
Find test data:
  SELECT e.login, ewp.first_working_day
  FROM employee e
  JOIN employee_work_period ewp ON e.id = ewp.employee_id
  WHERE ewp.first_working_day > CURRENT_DATE
    AND e.enabled = true
  LIMIT 5;
If none found, use an employee hired this month and query the previous month.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/ttt/v1/statistic/report?employeeId={id}&year={Y}&month={M}
   where the month is BEFORE the employee's first_working_day.
2. Check personalNorm value.
3. DB: SELECT month_norm FROM statistic_report
   WHERE employee_id = {id} AND year = {Y} AND month = {M}.
4. Verify norm is 0 or that no record exists for that month.
5. Check that the API does not return an error (graceful handling).
test_cases.expected_result:
personalNorm = 0 for months before employee's first working day.
API returns norm=0 (not an error or null).
No statistic_report record may exist, OR record exists with month_norm=0.
Effective bounds: effectiveFrom > monthEnd means no overlap, norm=0.
test_cases.notes: Edge case: when effectiveFrom > monthEnd, the date range is empty. Verify no division-by-zero or NPE in norm calculation.

test_cases.title: Effective bounds — re-hired employee with gap in employment
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with multiple work periods (gap in employment).
Find test data:
  SELECT e.login, e.full_name, COUNT(*) AS period_count
  FROM employee e
  JOIN employee_work_period ewp ON e.id = ewp.employee_id
  WHERE e.enabled = true
  GROUP BY e.id
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
  LIMIT 5;

Then: SELECT * FROM employee_work_period
WHERE employee_id = {id} ORDER BY first_working_day;
Identify the gap month(s) and the re-hire month.
test_cases.steps:
1. For the re-hire month, GET /api/ttt/v1/statistic/report for the employee.
2. Check which work period is used for norm calculation.
3. For a month in the gap (between periods), query the same endpoint.
4. DB: SELECT month_norm FROM statistic_report for gap month.
5. For the re-hire month, verify norm is prorated from the new first_working_day.
6. Verify gap months have norm = 0.
test_cases.expected_result:
Re-hire month: personalNorm prorated from new first_working_day to month end.
Gap months: personalNorm = 0 (no active work period).
Most recent matching work period used for current calculations.
Old work period does not bleed into gap months.
test_cases.notes: Multiple employee_work_period records. The system should match the work period that overlaps with the target month. Verify no period confusion when multiple records exist.

test_cases.title: Effective bounds — no work period data (fallback to full month)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with no records in employee_work_period table.
Find test data:
  SELECT e.login, e.full_name
  FROM employee e
  LEFT JOIN employee_work_period ewp ON e.id = ewp.employee_id
  WHERE e.enabled = true AND ewp.id IS NULL
  LIMIT 5;

These employees may exist if work period sync has not run or data is missing.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/ttt/v1/statistic/report?employeeId={id}&year={Y}&month={M}.
2. Check personalNorm value.
3. Compare with the full month norm for the employee's office.
4. DB: SELECT month_norm FROM statistic_report for the employee/month.
5. Verify the system does not crash or return an error.
6. Check application logs for warnings about missing work period.
test_cases.expected_result:
System uses full month range as fallback when no work period data exists.
personalNorm = full month norm for the employee's office/calendar.
No crash, NPE, or 500 error.
Behavior matches pre-#3353 behavior (backward compatible).
Optional: warning logged about missing work period.
test_cases.notes: Fallback behavior: if employee_work_period is empty, effectiveFrom = monthStart and effectiveTo = monthEnd. Verify graceful degradation.

test_cases.title: Budget norm — employee with admin vacation (budgetNorm > personalNorm)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee who had administrative vacation (leave type = ADMIN) during the target month.
Find test data:
  SELECT sr.employee_id, e.login, sr.month_norm, sr.budget_norm,
         sr.year, sr.month
  FROM statistic_report sr
  JOIN employee e ON sr.employee_id = e.id
  WHERE sr.budget_norm > sr.month_norm
    AND sr.budget_norm > 0
  ORDER BY sr.year DESC, sr.month DESC
  LIMIT 5;

Also verify admin vacation exists:
  SELECT * FROM vacation
  WHERE employee_id = {id}
    AND type = 'ADMINISTRATIVE'
    AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM start_date) = {Y}
    AND EXTRACT(MONTH FROM start_date) = {M};
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/ttt/v1/statistic/report?employeeId={id}&year={Y}&month={M}.
2. Check both personalNorm and budgetNorm fields in response.
3. Verify budgetNorm = personalNorm + (admin_vacation_hours).
4. Navigate to Statistics > Employee Reports for the employee/month.
5. Check UI display format: should show '{personalNorm} ({budgetNorm})'.
6. Verify the excess percentage uses budgetNorm as denominator.
test_cases.expected_result:
budgetNorm > personalNorm (includes admin vacation hours).
UI shows dual display: e.g., '144 (160)' where 144 = personalNorm, 160 = budgetNorm.
Budget norm = personalNorm + admin_vacation_working_days * 8.
Excess calculation: (reported - budgetNorm) / budgetNorm * 100.
test_cases.notes: budgetNorm is a new field added by #3381. Verify it appears in both API response and UI. The dual display format is specific to employee reports.

test_cases.title: Budget norm — no admin vacation (budgetNorm equals personalNorm)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with no administrative vacation in the target month.
Find test data:
  SELECT sr.employee_id, e.login, sr.month_norm, sr.budget_norm,
         sr.year, sr.month
  FROM statistic_report sr
  JOIN employee e ON sr.employee_id = e.id
  WHERE sr.budget_norm = sr.month_norm
    AND sr.month_norm > 0
    AND e.enabled = true
  ORDER BY sr.year DESC, sr.month DESC
  LIMIT 5;
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/ttt/v1/statistic/report?employeeId={id}&year={Y}&month={M}.
2. Check personalNorm and budgetNorm fields.
3. Verify budgetNorm = personalNorm.
4. Navigate to Statistics > Employee Reports for the employee.
5. Check UI display: should show single number (no parenthetical).
6. DB: Confirm no ADMINISTRATIVE vacation for that employee/month.
test_cases.expected_result:
budgetNorm equals personalNorm when no admin vacation exists.
UI shows single norm value (no dual display, no parentheses).
E.g., just '160' not '160 (160)'.
Excess calculation: (reported - personalNorm) / personalNorm * 100.
test_cases.notes: When budgetNorm = personalNorm, UI should not show redundant parenthetical. Verify frontend conditionally renders the dual format.

test_cases.title: Budget norm + effective bounds — partial-month employee with admin vacation
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee hired mid-month who also has admin vacation in that same month.
Find test data:
  SELECT sr.employee_id, e.login, sr.month_norm, sr.budget_norm,
         ewp.first_working_day
  FROM statistic_report sr
  JOIN employee e ON sr.employee_id = e.id
  JOIN employee_work_period ewp ON e.id = ewp.employee_id
  WHERE sr.budget_norm > sr.month_norm
    AND sr.month_norm < 160
    AND EXTRACT(DAY FROM ewp.first_working_day) > 1
  ORDER BY sr.year DESC, sr.month DESC
  LIMIT 5;

If none found, this is a manufactured scenario: use timemachine clock to create it.
test_cases.steps:
1. DB: Get employee's first_working_day and admin vacation dates for the month.
2. Calculate expected personalNorm: working days within [firstWorkingDay, monthEnd] * 8.
3. Calculate expected budgetNorm: personalNorm + admin_vacation_hours_within_bounds.
4. GET /api/ttt/v1/statistic/report for the employee/month.
5. Compare API personalNorm with expected.
6. Compare API budgetNorm with expected.
7. Verify admin vacation hours are clamped to within effective bounds
   (not the full month).
test_cases.expected_result:
Both effective bounds AND admin vacation exclusion apply correctly.
personalNorm: prorated to actual work period (< full month).
budgetNorm: personalNorm + admin vacation hours WITHIN the effective bounds.
Admin vacation days outside the effective bounds are NOT counted.
E.g., hired Feb 15, admin vacation Feb 10-12 (before hire) should not add to budget.
test_cases.notes: Interaction test: effective bounds clamping intersected with admin vacation period. The admin vacation hours should only count for days within the employee's effective work period, not the full calendar month.

test_cases.title: Excess calculation uses budgetNorm as denominator
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with budgetNorm > personalNorm (has admin vacation).
Employee has reported hours for the month.
Reuse employee from TC-STAT-117.
  SELECT sr.employee_id, e.login, sr.month_norm, sr.budget_norm,
         sr.month_hours, sr.excess
  FROM statistic_report sr
  JOIN employee e ON sr.employee_id = e.id
  WHERE sr.budget_norm > sr.month_norm
    AND sr.month_hours > 0
  ORDER BY sr.year DESC, sr.month DESC
  LIMIT 5;
test_cases.steps:
1. DB: Get month_hours, month_norm, budget_norm, excess for the employee.
2. Calculate: expected_excess = (month_hours - budget_norm) / budget_norm * 100.
3. Compare with stored excess value.
4. Also calculate incorrect version: (month_hours - month_norm) / month_norm * 100.
5. Verify stored excess matches budgetNorm-based calculation, not personalNorm-based.
6. Navigate to Statistics > Employee Reports and check displayed excess percentage.
test_cases.expected_result:
excess = (reported - budgetNorm) / budgetNorm * 100.
NOT: (reported - personalNorm) / personalNorm * 100.
The denominator is budgetNorm, which includes admin vacation hours.
This means employees with admin vacation show lower excess percentages.
UI excess display matches the DB value.
test_cases.notes: Critical: if excess still uses personalNorm as denominator, admin vacation employees will show artificially high excess. Verify the formula change.

test_cases.title: normForDate — current month progressive accumulation within effective bounds
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee currently active (not dismissed, hired before current month).
Current date is mid-month (e.g., March 15).
Employee hired before this month (full effective bounds for current month).
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/ttt/v1/statistic/report?employeeId={id}&year={Y}&month={M}
   where M = current month.
2. Note the personalNorm (full month) and normForDate (progressive).
3. Calculate expected normForDate: working days from month start to today * 8.
4. Verify normForDate <= personalNorm.
5. Check the next day: normForDate should increase by 8 (if working day)
   or stay the same (if weekend/holiday).
6. Verify normForDate is clamped within effective bounds:
   max(monthStart, firstWorkingDay) to min(today, lastWorkingDay).
test_cases.expected_result:
normForDate accumulates day by day within effective bounds.
On March 15: normForDate = working days [monthStart..March 15] * 8.
normForDate <= personalNorm always.
normForDate increases on working days, stays flat on weekends/holidays.
Effective bounds applied: normForDate never exceeds what the clamped range allows.
test_cases.notes: normForDate = progressive norm up to current date. Used for 'current status' indicators. Verify it respects effective bounds, not just calendar month.

test_cases.title: normForDate — employee hired after the selected date (norm = 0)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee hired mid-month (e.g., Feb 15).
Query normForDate for a date BEFORE the hire date (e.g., Feb 10).
Reuse employee from TC-STAT-112.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/ttt/v1/statistic/report?employeeId={id}&year={Y}&month={M}&date={D}
   where D = a date before the employee's first_working_day within the same month.
   (If API does not accept date param, check via DB or timemachine clock.)
2. DB: SELECT * FROM statistic_report WHERE employee_id = {id}
   AND year = {Y} AND month = {M}.
3. Calculate expected normForDate: effective range = [firstWorkingDay, date].
   Since date < firstWorkingDay, effective range is empty.
4. Verify normForDate = 0.
5. Check that personalNorm (full month value) is still correctly prorated.
test_cases.expected_result:
normForDate = 0 when the selected date is before the employee's first working day.
Effective range is empty: max(monthStart, firstWorkingDay) > min(date, monthEnd).
personalNorm is still correctly prorated for the full month.
No error or negative value returned.
test_cases.notes: Tests the case where the normForDate range collapses to empty. The progressive norm should be 0, not negative or full-month.

test_cases.title: normForDate — employee dismissed before the selected date (norm capped at dismissal)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee dismissed mid-month (e.g., Feb 10).
Query normForDate for a date AFTER the dismissal (e.g., Feb 15).
Reuse employee from TC-STAT-113.
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/ttt/v1/statistic/report?employeeId={id}&year={Y}&month={M}
   for the dismissal month, using a date after dismissal.
2. DB: SELECT month_norm FROM statistic_report
   WHERE employee_id = {id} AND year = {Y} AND month = {M}.
3. Calculate expected normForDate:
   effective range = [monthStart, min(date, lastWorkingDay)].
   Since date > lastWorkingDay, clamped to [monthStart, lastWorkingDay].
4. Verify normForDate = personalNorm (fully accumulated up to dismissal).
5. Verify normForDate does NOT continue growing after dismissal date.
test_cases.expected_result:
normForDate is capped at the dismissal date.
normForDate = personalNorm once the selected date passes lastWorkingDay.
No additional hours accumulate after dismissal.
E.g., Feb 10 dismissal: normForDate on Feb 15 = same as normForDate on Feb 10.
effectiveTo = min(date, lastWorkingDay) correctly applied.
test_cases.notes: After dismissal, normForDate should plateau at personalNorm. Verify the progressive calculation stops at lastWorkingDay.

test_cases.title: Sync path — norm updated via vacation event (create/modify vacation)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with existing statistic_report for current month.
No admin vacation yet for this month.
Find test data:
  SELECT sr.employee_id, e.login, sr.month_norm, sr.budget_norm
  FROM statistic_report sr
  JOIN employee e ON sr.employee_id = e.id
  WHERE sr.month_norm = sr.budget_norm
    AND sr.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)
    AND sr.month = EXTRACT(MONTH FROM CURRENT_DATE)
    AND e.enabled = true
  LIMIT 5;
test_cases.steps:
1. DB: Record current month_norm and budget_norm for the employee.
2. Create an admin vacation for this employee in the current month:
   POST /api/vacation/v1/vacation/create (type=ADMINISTRATIVE, 2 working days).
3. Wait for event propagation (MQ message: VACATION_CREATED).
4. DB: Re-query statistic_report for the employee/month.
5. Verify budget_norm increased by 16 (2 days * 8h).
6. Verify month_norm unchanged (effective bounds not affected by vacation).
7. Check that effective bounds are still correctly applied to month_norm.
test_cases.expected_result:
After vacation creation:
  - budget_norm = previous_budget_norm + 16 (2 admin days * 8h).
  - month_norm unchanged (effective bounds clamping unaffected).
  - Event-driven update path correctly recalculates budget_norm.
  - Effective bounds still applied to month_norm in the recalculation.
If effective bounds are NOT applied during event-driven recalc,
month_norm may revert to full-month value (regression).
test_cases.notes: Tests the MQ event-driven update path (StatisticReportService.onVacationEvent). Effective bounds must be applied during event-triggered recalculation, not just during cron sync. Requires allow_api_mutations=true or manual action.

test_cases.title: Sync path — norm updated via task report submission
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with existing statistic_report for current month.
Employee has fewer reported hours than norm.
  SELECT sr.employee_id, e.login, sr.month_norm, sr.month_hours
  FROM statistic_report sr
  JOIN employee e ON sr.employee_id = e.id
  WHERE sr.month_hours < sr.month_norm
    AND sr.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)
    AND sr.month = EXTRACT(MONTH FROM CURRENT_DATE)
    AND e.enabled = true
  LIMIT 5;
test_cases.steps:
1. DB: Record current month_norm, budget_norm, month_hours for the employee.
2. Submit a time report for the employee (8h on a working day):
   POST /api/ttt/v1/report (or via UI).
3. Wait for statistic_report update (event-driven or poll DB).
4. DB: Re-query statistic_report.
5. Verify month_hours increased by 8.
6. Verify month_norm still reflects effective bounds (not recalculated to full month).
7. Verify excess recalculated with updated hours but same norm values.
test_cases.expected_result:
After task report submission:
  - month_hours increased by 8.
  - month_norm unchanged (effective bounds still applied).
  - budget_norm unchanged.
  - excess recalculated: (new_month_hours - budget_norm) / budget_norm * 100.
If month_norm reverts to full-month during report-triggered recalc,
this is a regression in effective bounds.
test_cases.notes: Tests the task-report-driven update path. The critical check is that month_norm is NOT recalculated to full-month value during this path. Requires allow_api_mutations=true or manual action.

test_cases.title: Sync path — norm updated via daily cron sync (04:00 job)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employees with statistic_report records.
At least one employee hired mid-month (for effective bounds verification).
At least one employee with admin vacation (for budget norm verification).
  -- Mid-month hire:
  SELECT sr.employee_id, e.login, sr.month_norm
  FROM statistic_report sr
  JOIN employee e ON sr.employee_id = e.id
  JOIN employee_work_period ewp ON e.id = ewp.employee_id
  WHERE EXTRACT(DAY FROM ewp.first_working_day) > 1
    AND sr.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)
    AND sr.month = EXTRACT(MONTH FROM CURRENT_DATE)
  LIMIT 3;
  -- Admin vacation:
  SELECT sr.employee_id, e.login, sr.budget_norm
  FROM statistic_report sr
  JOIN employee e ON sr.employee_id = e.id
  WHERE sr.budget_norm > sr.month_norm
    AND sr.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)
    AND sr.month = EXTRACT(MONTH FROM CURRENT_DATE)
  LIMIT 3;
test_cases.steps:
1. DB: Record month_norm and budget_norm for selected employees.
2. Trigger the nightly sync manually:
   POST /v1/test/trigger-optimized-statistic-report-sync
   (or wait for the 04:00 cron job).
3. DB: Re-query statistic_report for the same employees.
4. For mid-month hire: verify month_norm is still prorated (not full month).
5. For admin vacation: verify budget_norm still includes admin vacation hours.
6. Verify both previous month and current month records are correct.
7. Check that the sync did not reset effective bounds.
test_cases.expected_result:
After daily cron sync:
  - Mid-month hires: month_norm remains prorated (effective bounds preserved).
  - Admin vacation employees: budget_norm remains > month_norm.
  - Previous month records: fully finalized with correct effective bounds.
  - Current month records: progressive normForDate correctly bounded.
Cron sync applies the same effective bounds logic as event-driven updates.
No regression: norms not reset to full-month values.
test_cases.notes: Tests the nightly cron sync path (OptimizedStatisticReportSyncJob). All three norm components (personalNorm, budgetNorm, normForDate) must respect effective bounds after sync. This is the most common recalculation trigger.

test_cases.title: POST /v1/statistic bulk request returns vacation statistics for multiple employees
test_cases.preconditions:
Multiple employees with reported hours in the target month.
Find test data:
  SELECT DISTINCT sr.employee_login
  FROM statistic_report sr
  WHERE sr.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)
    AND sr.month = EXTRACT(MONTH FROM CURRENT_DATE) - 1
  LIMIT 20;

Collect 5-10 employee logins for the request body.
Use timemachine or qa-1 env.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/ttt/v1/statistic
   Body: {"employeesLogins": ["login1","login2",...],
          "startDate": "YYYY-MM-01", "endDate": "YYYY-MM-28"}
2. Verify response contains statistics for each requested employee.
3. Verify response includes fields: login, personalNorm, budgetNorm,
   reportedEffort (or reported), excess.
4. Verify OLD endpoint no longer works:
   GET /api/ttt/v1/statistic?employeeLogin=X&startDate=...&endDate=...
   Should return 405 Method Not Allowed (or similar).
5. DB: Cross-verify with statistic_report table:
   SELECT employee_login, month_norm, reported_effort
   FROM statistic_report
   WHERE employee_login IN ('login1','login2',...)
     AND report_date = 'YYYY-MM-01';
6. Verify response data matches DB cached values.
test_cases.expected_result:
POST /v1/statistic returns bulk statistics for all requested employees.
Response data matches pre-computed values in statistic_report table.
No cross-service calls at query time (fast response).
Old GET endpoint returns 405 or 404 (endpoint changed to POST).
Response contains: personalNorm, budgetNorm, reported, excess per employee.
reported_effort field type is BigDecimal (not double).
test_cases.notes: MR !5150 changed GET→POST. MR !5013 changed backend to read from statistic_report table. Verify both: endpoint method AND data source.

test_cases.title: POST /v1/statistic/sick-leaves bulk request returns sick leave stats
test_cases.preconditions:
Multiple employees with sick leaves in the target period.
Find test data:
  SELECT DISTINCT sl.employee_login
  FROM sick_leave sl
  WHERE sl.start_date >= 'YYYY-01-01'
  LIMIT 10;

Collect 5-10 logins with sick leaves.
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/ttt/v1/statistic/sick-leaves
   Body: {"employeesLogins": ["login1","login2",...],
          "startDate": "YYYY-01-01", "endDate": "YYYY-12-31"}
2. Verify response contains sick leave data per employee.
3. Verify OLD endpoint no longer works:
   GET /api/ttt/v1/statistic/sick-leaves?...
   Should return 405.
4. Test with empty logins array: {"employeesLogins": []}
   Expect empty response, not error.
5. Test with single login: {"employeesLogins": ["login1"]}
   Verify equivalent to per-employee query.
6. Verify sickLeaves field is an object (not string) in response.
test_cases.expected_result:
POST /v1/statistic/sick-leaves returns bulk sick leave data.
Empty logins array → empty response (not error).
Single login → correct per-employee data.
Old GET endpoint returns 405.
sickLeaves field type is object (MR !5155 fixed PropTypes).
Response time significantly faster than old batched GET approach.
test_cases.notes: MR !5150 changed sick-leaves endpoint from GET to POST. MR !5155 fixed frontend PropTypes (string→object). Verify both.

test_cases.title: Statistics: Admin/Chief Accountant sees ALL employees in report
test_cases.preconditions:
User with ADMIN or CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT role.
Find admin user:
  SELECT e.login FROM employee e
  JOIN employee_role er ON e.id = er.employee_id
  WHERE er.role = 'ROLE_ADMIN'
    AND e.enabled = true
  LIMIT 3;
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN user.
2. Navigate to Statistics > Employee Reports.
3. Select a reporting period (e.g., previous month).
4. Count total employees shown in the table.
5. DB: Count all enabled employees:
   SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employee WHERE enabled = true;
6. Verify the table shows ALL employees (not filtered).
7. POST /api/ttt/v1/statistic with all employee logins.
8. Verify all logins are returned in response.
9. Repeat for CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT role — same result expected.
test_cases.expected_result:
ADMIN and CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT see all employees in statistics.
No filtering applied: employee count matches total enabled employees.
API returns data for every requested login.
Both roles have identical visibility scope.
test_cases.notes: MR !5101 added getEmployeeLoginsByCurrentUserRole() which returns null for ADMIN and CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT (null = no filter = see all).

test_cases.title: Statistics: Department Manager sees only direct reports
test_cases.preconditions:
User with DEPARTMENT_MANAGER role who manages a team.
Find DM user:
  SELECT e.login, e.full_name FROM employee e
  JOIN employee_role er ON e.id = er.employee_id
  WHERE er.role = 'ROLE_DEPARTMENT_MANAGER'
    AND e.enabled = true
  LIMIT 3;

Find their direct reports:
  SELECT e.login FROM employee e
  WHERE e.manager_id = {dm_id}
    AND e.enabled = true;
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as DEPARTMENT_MANAGER user.
2. Navigate to Statistics > Employee Reports.
3. Select a reporting period.
4. Note the employees shown in the table.
5. DB: Get DM's direct reports:
   SELECT e.login FROM employee e
   WHERE e.manager_id = (SELECT id FROM employee WHERE login = '{dm_login}')
   AND e.enabled = true;
6. Verify the table shows ONLY the DM's direct reports.
7. Verify the DM cannot see employees from other departments.
8. API: POST /v1/statistic with a login NOT in DM's reports.
   Verify that employee is excluded from response.
test_cases.expected_result:
Department Manager sees ONLY their direct reports.
Employee count matches DB query for manager_id = DM's ID.
Employees from other departments NOT visible.
API filters out non-managed employees from response.
DM cannot access other managers' team statistics.
test_cases.notes: MR !5101: ROLE_DEPARTMENT_MANAGER → filter by managerId. Uses EmployeeRepository.findAllByOfficesOrManagers() JPQL query.

test_cases.title: Statistics: Tech Lead sees only their assigned employees
test_cases.preconditions:
User with TECH_LEAD role.
Find TL user:
  SELECT e.login FROM employee e
  JOIN employee_role er ON e.id = er.employee_id
  WHERE er.role = 'ROLE_TECH_LEAD'
    AND e.enabled = true
  LIMIT 3;

Find their employees:
  SELECT e.login FROM employee e
  WHERE e.tech_lead_id = {tl_id}
    AND e.enabled = true;
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as TECH_LEAD user.
2. Navigate to Statistics > Employee Reports.
3. Select a reporting period.
4. Note the employees shown.
5. DB: Get TL's employees:
   SELECT e.login FROM employee e
   WHERE e.tech_lead_id = (SELECT id FROM employee WHERE login = '{tl_login}')
   AND e.enabled = true;
6. Verify the table shows ONLY the TL's employees.
7. Compare with OFFICE_DIRECTOR/ACCOUNTANT visibility:
   - Login as OFFICE_DIRECTOR → sees employees in their profit centers.
   - Login as ACCOUNTANT → sees employees in their profit centers.
8. Verify each role sees its expected scope.
test_cases.expected_result:
Tech Lead sees ONLY employees where tech_lead_id matches.
Office Director sees employees in their profit center offices.
Accountant sees employees in their profit center offices.
Each role's filter is applied independently.
Scopes may overlap (same employee visible to multiple roles).
test_cases.notes: MR !5101: ROLE_TECH_LEAD → filter by techLeadId. ROLE_OFFICE_DIRECTOR/ROLE_ACCOUNTANT → filter by officeIds. ROLE_OFFICE_HR → filter by hrId. Tests multiple role scopes.

test_cases.title: Cache: Vacation create/delete updates month_norm via RabbitMQ event
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with statistic_report record for current month.
No administrative vacation in current month.
  SELECT sr.employee_login, sr.month_norm, sr.budget_norm
  FROM statistic_report sr
  WHERE sr.month_norm = sr.budget_norm
    AND sr.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)
    AND sr.month = EXTRACT(MONTH FROM CURRENT_DATE)
  LIMIT 5;

Use timemachine env with allow_api_mutations=true.
test_cases.steps:
1. DB: Record current month_norm, budget_norm for employee.
2. Create an administrative vacation for this month:
   POST /api/vacation/v1/vacation/create
   (type=ADMINISTRATIVE, 3 working days in current month).
3. Wait 5-10 seconds for RabbitMQ event propagation.
4. DB: Re-query statistic_report:
   SELECT month_norm, budget_norm, reported_effort
   FROM statistic_report
   WHERE employee_login = '{login}'
     AND year = {Y} AND month = {M};
5. Verify budget_norm increased by 24 (3 days × 8h).
6. Verify month_norm unchanged.
7. Delete the vacation.
8. Wait for event propagation.
9. Re-query: verify budget_norm reverted to original value.
10. Navigate to Statistics page — verify UI reflects changes.
test_cases.expected_result:
After vacation creation:
  - budget_norm = original + 24 (3 admin days × 8h).
  - month_norm unchanged (not affected by admin vacation).
  - reported_effort unchanged.
After vacation deletion:
  - budget_norm reverts to original value.
  - Event-driven: update happens within seconds (not waiting for 4am cron).
MQ path: Vacation service publishes EmployeeMonthNormContextCalculated
event → RabbitMQ → TTT service consumes and updates statistic_report.
test_cases.notes: MR !5013: RabbitMQ topic exchange TTT_BACKEND_EMPLOYEE_TOPIC. Vacation service publishes event, TTT service consumes. This tests the complete cross-service event-driven update path.

test_cases.title: Cache: Sick leave create/change/delete updates month_norm
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with statistic_report record for current month.
No existing sick leave in current month.
  SELECT sr.employee_login, sr.month_norm
  FROM statistic_report sr
  WHERE sr.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)
    AND sr.month = EXTRACT(MONTH FROM CURRENT_DATE)
  LIMIT 5;

Use timemachine env with allow_api_mutations=true.
test_cases.steps:
1. DB: Record current month_norm for employee.
2. Create a sick leave for the employee in current month (5 days):
   POST /api/vacation/v1/sick-leave/create
3. Wait 5-10 seconds for event propagation.
4. DB: Re-query statistic_report month_norm.
5. Verify month_norm decreased (sick leave reduces working days).
6. Change the sick leave (extend to 7 days):
   PATCH /api/vacation/v1/sick-leave/{id}
7. Wait, re-query. Verify month_norm decreased further.
8. Delete the sick leave.
9. Wait, re-query. Verify month_norm reverted to original.
10. For sick leave spanning two months (e.g., Jan 28 - Feb 3):
    Verify BOTH months' statistic_report records updated.
test_cases.expected_result:
Sick leave creation reduces month_norm (fewer working days).
Sick leave change (extend/shorten) updates month_norm accordingly.
Sick leave deletion restores original month_norm.
Cross-month sick leave: events published for EACH affected month.
Updates happen event-driven (within seconds), not waiting for cron.
BUG (pre-fix, MR !5200): month_norm did NOT update on sick leave —
only updated by daily cron. Verify this is now fixed.
test_cases.notes: MR !5200 added sick leave event handlers: SickLeaveCreated/Changed/DeletedEventListener → sendUpdateMonthNormEvent(sickLeave). Multi-month sick leaves publish separate events per month. QA Bug #2 from omaksimova — verify fix.

test_cases.title: Cache: Task report add/patch/delete updates reported_effort
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with statistic_report record for current month.
Employee has reported some hours this month.
  SELECT sr.employee_login, sr.reported_effort, sr.month_norm
  FROM statistic_report sr
  WHERE sr.reported_effort > 0
    AND sr.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)
    AND sr.month = EXTRACT(MONTH FROM CURRENT_DATE)
  LIMIT 5;

Use timemachine env with allow_api_mutations=true.
test_cases.steps:
1. DB: Record current reported_effort for employee.
2. Add a task report (8h) for the employee in current month:
   POST /api/ttt/v1/report (effort: 8, date in current month).
3. DB: Re-query reported_effort immediately.
4. Verify reported_effort increased by 8.000.
5. Patch the report to 4h:
   PATCH /api/ttt/v1/report/{id} (effort: 4).
6. Verify reported_effort decreased by 4.000.
7. Delete the report:
   DELETE /api/ttt/v1/report/{id}.
8. Verify reported_effort decreased by 4.000.
9. Verify month_norm was NOT changed by task report events.
10. Navigate to Statistics page — verify UI shows updated reported.
test_cases.expected_result:
Task report add: reported_effort increases by effort amount.
Task report patch: reported_effort adjusts to new effort.
Task report delete: reported_effort decreases accordingly.
reported_effort type is DECIMAL(10,3) — values like 8.000.
month_norm NOT affected by task report events.
Update is synchronous via TaskReportEventListener (not MQ).
Excess recalculated: (reported_effort - budget_norm) / budget_norm × 100.
test_cases.notes: MR !5013: TaskReportEventListener calls statisticReportSyncService.updateMonthlyReportedEffortForEmployee() on add/patch/delete. This is direct service call, not MQ. Verify update is immediate, not deferred.

test_cases.title: Cache: Event type discrimination — absence events do NOT delete unrelated records
test_cases.preconditions:
Multiple employees with statistic_report records.
Employee A has vacation. Employee B has NO vacation but has statistic_report.
Find test data:
  -- Employee A (with vacation):
  SELECT DISTINCT sr.employee_login
  FROM statistic_report sr
  JOIN vacation v ON sr.employee_login = (
    SELECT login FROM employee WHERE id = v.employee_id)
  WHERE sr.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)
  LIMIT 3;

  -- Employee B (no vacation, has stat report):
  SELECT sr.employee_login FROM statistic_report sr
  WHERE sr.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)
    AND sr.employee_login NOT IN (
      SELECT e.login FROM employee e
      JOIN vacation v ON e.id = v.employee_id
      WHERE v.status NOT IN ('DELETED','CANCELED'))
  LIMIT 3;
test_cases.steps:
1. DB: Record statistic_report records for Employee B.
   SELECT * FROM statistic_report
   WHERE employee_login = '{B_login}';
2. Create a vacation for Employee A (not B).
3. Wait for event propagation (5-10 sec).
4. DB: Re-query statistic_report for Employee B.
   Verify ALL records still exist (not deleted).
5. Verify Employee A's records were correctly updated.
6. DB: Count total statistic_report records before and after.
   SELECT COUNT(*) FROM statistic_report;
7. Verify total count did not decrease (no unrelated deletions).
8. Repeat with sick leave event — same verification.
test_cases.expected_result:
Vacation/sick leave events for Employee A do NOT delete
Employee B's statistic_report records.
Only INITIAL_SYNC event type triggers deleteReportsWithEmploymentChanged().
VACATION_CHANGES and SICK_LEAVE_CHANGES event types skip deletion.
Total statistic_report record count stable after absence events.
BUG (pre-fix, MR !5203): absence events triggered deletion of
unrelated employees' records via deleteReportsWithEmploymentChanged().
This was the most critical bug in the caching implementation.
test_cases.notes: MR !5203: New enum StatisticReportUpdateEventType with INITIAL_SYNC, VACATION_CHANGES, SICK_LEAVE_CHANGES. deleteReportsWithEmploymentChanged() only on INITIAL_SYNC. QA Bug #3 / dev comment by Quyen Nguyen.

test_cases.title: Frontend: Empty/null statistics response clears loading state
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with NO statistic_report records for a specific month.
Find months with no data:
  SELECT DISTINCT year, month FROM statistic_report
  ORDER BY year, month;
  -- Pick a month NOT in this list, or use a month far in the future.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to Statistics > Employee Reports.
2. Select a reporting period with data — verify table loads normally.
3. Switch to a period with NO data (e.g., far future month).
4. Verify:
   a. Loading spinner appears briefly.
   b. Loading spinner disappears (not infinite).
   c. Table shows empty state (no rows, or 'No data' message).
   d. No stale data from previous period displayed.
5. Switch back to a period with data — verify table loads correctly.
6. Rapidly switch between periods (stress test saga cancellation).
7. Verify no JavaScript console errors.
test_cases.expected_result:
Empty/null response: loading state cleared, table shows empty.
No infinite loading spinner.
No stale data from previous period retained.
Period switching: previous request cancelled, new data loaded.
No console errors or uncaught promise rejections.
BUG (pre-fix, MR !5194): empty response left isLoadingReports=true
(infinite spinner) and stale reports[] data displayed.
test_cases.notes: MR !5194: On empty/null response, dispatches fetchReportsFailureAction(). Reducer now clears state.reports = [] on failure. QA Bug #1 from omaksimova (infinite loading on out-of-employment period).

test_cases.title: Frontend: Rapid period switching cancels pending requests (saga cancellation)
test_cases.preconditions:
Logged-in user with access to Statistics > Employee Reports.
Multiple reporting periods with data available.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to Statistics > Employee Reports.
2. Select period Jan 2026 — wait for table to start loading.
3. IMMEDIATELY switch to Feb 2026 (before Jan data arrives).
4. IMMEDIATELY switch to Mar 2026 (before Feb data arrives).
5. Wait for final request to complete.
6. Verify:
   a. Table shows March 2026 data (the last requested period).
   b. No mixing of Jan/Feb/Mar data in the table.
   c. No duplicate rows from multiple responses.
   d. Loading indicator properly reflects final state.
7. Open browser DevTools > Network tab.
8. Repeat the rapid switching.
9. Verify cancelled requests show as 'cancelled' in Network tab.
10. Check browser console for errors.
test_cases.expected_result:
Final table shows ONLY the last requested period data.
Previous requests are cancelled (visible in Network tab).
No data mixing between periods.
No duplicate rows or partial data.
Redux saga uses task.isRunning() check before starting new fetch.
Map<string, Task> properly tracks and cancels previous tasks.
No unhandled promise rejections from cancelled requests.
test_cases.notes: MR !5155: Rewrote sagas with task.isRunning() and Map<string, Task> for improved cancellation. Previous implementation used raw objects and was prone to race conditions on rapid period switching.

test_cases.title: Daily cron sync (4am) correctly populates statistic_report for current + previous month
test_cases.preconditions:
statistic_report table exists with some records.
At least one employee has reported hours in current month.
At least one employee has a vacation in previous month.
Use timemachine env (can trigger sync manually).
test_cases.steps:
1. DB: Record current statistic_report state:
   SELECT employee_login, year, month, reported_effort, month_norm,
          budget_norm
   FROM statistic_report
   WHERE (year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)
     AND month = EXTRACT(MONTH FROM CURRENT_DATE))
   OR (year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)
     AND month = EXTRACT(MONTH FROM CURRENT_DATE) - 1)
   ORDER BY employee_login, year, month;
2. Trigger sync via test endpoint:
   POST /api/ttt/v1/test/trigger-optimized-statistic-report-sync
   (or POST /api/ttt/v1/test/statistic-reports for direct sync).
3. Wait for sync to complete (may take 30-60 seconds).
4. Re-query statistic_report for current + previous month.
5. Verify:
   a. All active employees have records for both months.
   b. reported_effort matches actual task reports:
      SELECT employee_id, SUM(effort) FROM task_report
      WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM report_date) = {Y}
        AND EXTRACT(MONTH FROM report_date) = {M}
      GROUP BY employee_id;
   c. month_norm reflects effective bounds (prorated for partial months).
   d. budget_norm includes admin vacation hours.
6. Check ShedLock table for scheduled job state:
   SELECT * FROM shedlock WHERE name LIKE '%statistic%';
test_cases.expected_result:
Cron sync populates statistic_report for current AND previous month.
All active employees have records for both months.
reported_effort matches SUM(task_report.effort) for the month.
month_norm reflects effective bounds (not full calendar month).
budget_norm includes admin vacation hours where applicable.
ShedLock prevents concurrent execution.
Cron schedule: 0 0 4 * * ? (daily at 4:00 AM).
StatisticReportSyncLauncher syncs previous + current month.
Uses StatisticReportSyncService.saveMonthNormAndReportedEffortForEmployees()
with eventType=INITIAL_SYNC (triggers deleteReportsWithEmploymentChanged).
test_cases.notes: MR !5013: StatisticReportScheduler at 0 0 4 * * ? with ShedLock. StatisticReportSyncLauncher runs for previous and current month. Uses INITIAL_SYNC event type which DOES trigger employment change deletion. Test endpoint: POST /v1/test/statistic-reports.

test_cases.expected_result:
Update MAY succeed (update validator does NOT call isNextVacationAvailable)
Difference from create: next-year check only on creation
Note: Other validations (available days) may still block
test_cases.notes: Code: Update validator does NOT call isNextVacationAvailable()

test_cases.title: Update paymentType REGULAR → ADMINISTRATIVE
test_cases.preconditions: Existing REGULAR vacation in NEW status
test_cases.expected_result:
PaymentType updated
Duration validation now uses ADMINISTRATIVE rules (min 1 day)
Available days recalculated
Previously consumed REGULAR days returned to pool
test_cases.notes: Type change triggers recalculation of day pool

test_cases.title: Update non-existing vacation ID

errorCode: exception.not.found or vacation id not found
EntityNotFoundException from repository

Note: Approver edits may not reset status (depends on field changed)

test_cases.title: Update payment month to closed accounting period

errorCode: validation.vacation.dates.payment

test_cases.title: NEW → APPROVED (approver approves)

test_cases.title: NEW → REJECTED (approver rejects)

test_cases.steps:

test_cases.title: NEW → CANCELED (employee cancels)

test_cases.title: NEW → DELETED (employee deletes)

test_cases.title: REJECTED → APPROVED (re-approval without edit)

Status changes REJECTED → APPROVED
Transition allowed in VacationStatusManager map
Re-approval works without editing vacation first

test_cases.notes: Confirmed possible in live testing. add(REJECTED, APPROVED, PM/DM/ADMIN)

test_cases.title: APPROVED → NEW (employee edits dates)

test_cases.expected_result:

test_cases.title: APPROVED → CANCELED (employee cancels)

test_cases.steps:
1. PUT /api/vacation/v1/vacations/cancel/{id}
2. Verify response
test_cases.expected_result:
Status changes to CANCELED
Days returned to pool
Recalculation triggered
FIFO redistribution among remaining vacations
test_cases.notes: canBeCancelled guard determines if cancel is available

test_cases.title: APPROVED → CANCELED blocked by canBeCancelled guard
test_cases.preconditions:
APPROVED REGULAR vacation where reportPeriod > paymentDate
Test data: vacation with paymentDate in a past closed period
test_cases.steps:
1. PUT /api/vacation/v1/vacations/cancel/{id}
2. Check error response
test_cases.expected_result:
Cancel blocked — permission not available
canBeCancelled returns false when: REGULAR + APPROVED + reportPeriod after paymentDate
Protects accounting integrity after period close
test_cases.notes: Critical accounting guard. Same logic blocks reject and delete too

test_cases.title: APPROVED → REJECTED (approver rejects after approval)
test_cases.preconditions:
APPROVED vacation, authenticated as approver
canBeCancelled=true
test_cases.steps:

test_cases.expected_result:

vacation_payment record created
No further transitions possible
Balance unchanged (days deducted at approval, not payment)
test_cases.notes: Terminal state. See TS-Vac-Payment for detailed payment tests

test_cases.title: CANCELED → NEW (employee re-opens)
test_cases.preconditions: Own CANCELED vacation
test_cases.expected_result:
Status changes CANCELED → NEW
Vacation re-enters active lifecycle
Days recalculated
Note: CANCELED is in FINAL_STATUSES but CANCELED→NEW is explicitly in transition map
test_cases.notes: Edge case: CANCELED in FINAL_STATUSES but explicit transition exists

test_cases.title: PAID → any transition (terminal — blocked)
test_cases.preconditions: PAID vacation
test_cases.steps:
1. Try POST approve, PUT reject, PUT cancel, DELETE on PAID vacation

test_cases.expected_result:
All transitions blocked
HTTP 400: exception.vacation.status.notAllowed
PAID has no outgoing transitions in map
Permission service returns empty set for PAID
test_cases.notes: Verify truly terminal — no way to modify PAID vacation

test_cases.title: DELETED → any transition (terminal — blocked)

DELETED vacation (soft-deleted)

2. Check error responses
test_cases.expected_result:
All transitions blocked
DELETED is terminal (not in transition map as source)
Vacation not returned in list queries
test_cases.notes: DELETED is a soft-delete terminal state

test_cases.title: Invalid transition: NEW → PAID (skipping approval)

test_cases.steps:
1. PUT /api/vacation/v1/vacations/pay/{id}
2. Check error response
test_cases.expected_result:

test_cases.notes: Transition map enforces workflow order

test_cases.title: Non-approver tries to approve vacation
test_cases.preconditions: Vacation in NEW status, authenticate as non-approver employee
test_cases.steps:
1. POST /api/vacation/v1/vacations/approve/{id} as non-approver
2. Check error response
test_cases.expected_result:
HTTP 400: exception.vacation.status.notAllowed
hasAccess() returns false — not owner (for employee transitions) and not approver
Transition map requires PM/DM/ADMIN roles
test_cases.notes: Security: only designated approver can approve

test_cases.title: Concurrent status change — write lock behavior

test_cases.steps:
1. Prepare two parallel approve requests for same vacation
2. Send both simultaneously (use async/threading)
3. Check results
test_cases.expected_result:
One succeeds, one fails or blocks
findByIdAndAcquireWriteLock uses SELECT ... FOR UPDATE
Prevents double-approval or race conditions
Second request gets exception.vacation.status.notAllowed (already APPROVED)
test_cases.notes: Pessimistic locking via findByIdAndAcquireWriteLock

test_cases.title: Status transition — verify event published

test_cases.expected_result:

test_cases.title: AV=false: basic accrual formula — mid-year calculation
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee in AV=false office (Russia)
Clock set to June (month 6)
Test data: employee with known norm (e.g. 24 days)
test_cases.steps:
1. Set clock to June 15
2. GET /api/vacation/v1/vacationdays/available?employeeLogin=X&paymentDate=2026-06-01&newDays=0
3. Calculate expected: accruedDays = 6 * (24/12) = 12, minus consumed
4. Compare with API response
test_cases.expected_result:
availablePaidDays matches formula:
X = (month × norm/12) + yearRemainder + priorYears - norm + futureDays + editedDays
For month 6, norm 24: accrued = 12 days base
Negative result shown as 0 (AV=false never shows negative)
test_cases.notes: Core calculation for Russian offices

test_cases.title: AV=false: negative balance shows 0
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee in AV=false office with consumed days > accrued
Test data: employee early in year who already used vacation
test_cases.steps:
1. Set clock to February
2. GET available days for employee with high consumption
3. Verify result
test_cases.expected_result:
availablePaidDays = 0 (not negative)
AV=false: if calculated < 0, display 0
UI shows 0 available, blocks further REGULAR vacation creation
test_cases.notes: AV=false: never show negative balance to user

test_cases.title: AV=true: full year available immediately
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee in AV=true office (Cyprus/Germany)
Test data: employee in advance_vacation=true office
test_cases.steps:
1. GET /api/vacation/v1/vacationdays/available
2. Verify full annual norm is available (not prorated by month)
test_cases.expected_result:
availablePaidDays = currentYearDays + pastYearDays + futureDays + editedDays
No monthly accrual — full year balance from Jan 1
Can exceed prorated amount (advance vacation)
test_cases.notes: AV=true: no accrual calculation, full year immediately

test_cases.title: AV=true: negative balance allowed
test_cases.preconditions: Employee in AV=true office who has consumed more than available
test_cases.steps:
1. Create enough vacations to exceed current year balance
2. GET available days
3. Verify negative value returned
test_cases.expected_result:
availablePaidDays can be negative for current year
AV=true allows negative balance (advance vacation concept)
Negative balance rolls into next year
UI should display negative value (not 0)
test_cases.notes: Key difference from AV=false: negative is valid and displayed

test_cases.title: AV=true: norm deviation recalculation — overtime adds days
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee in AV=true office who reported more than personal norm
Test data: employee with reported hours > personalNorm for a month
test_cases.steps:
1. Ensure employee has overtime for a completed month
2. Check employee_vacation table for deviation adjustment
3. GET available days
4. Formula: daysDelta = (reported - personalNorm) / 8
test_cases.expected_result:
Overtime adds fractional days to earliest year balance
deviation = (reportedHours - personalNorm) / REPORTING_NORM(8)
Positive deviation increases available days
Recalculated monthly by cron/trigger
test_cases.notes: REPORTING_NORM is hardcoded to 8 hours

test_cases.title: AV=true: norm deviation recalculation — undertime deducts days
test_cases.preconditions: Employee in AV=true office who reported less than personal norm
test_cases.steps:
1. Ensure employee has undertime for a completed month
2. Check deviation calculation
3. Verify deduction from earliest year
test_cases.expected_result:
Undertime deducts from earliest year balance first (FIFO)
If earliest year exhausted, deducts from next year
If all years exhausted, current year goes negative
deviation = negative value
test_cases.notes: Undertime uses FIFO year consumption like vacations

test_cases.title: FIFO day consumption — earliest year first
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with balances in multiple years (prior + current)
Test data: SELECT employee_id, year, days FROM employee_vacation WHERE employee_id=<id> ORDER BY year
test_cases.steps:
1. Create a multi-day vacation crossing year boundary
2. Check vacation_days_distribution table
3. DB: SELECT * FROM vacation_days_distribution WHERE vacation_id=<id>
test_cases.expected_result:
Days consumed from earliest year first
vacation_days_distribution shows per-year breakdown
Example: 3 days from 2025 + 2 days from 2026 for 5-day vacation
Earlier year depleted before later year touched
test_cases.notes: FIFO = First In, First Out — consume oldest days first

test_cases.title: FIFO: cancel returns days to pool, redistributes
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with multiple vacations, cancel one in the middle
test_cases.steps:
1. Create vacation A (5 days), vacation B (5 days)
2. Cancel vacation A
3. Check available days and B's day distribution
test_cases.expected_result:
A's days returned to balance
FIFO redistribution triggered for remaining vacations
B may get re-distributed days from earlier years
Recalculation returns ALL regular days then re-distributes
test_cases.notes: Recalculation is comprehensive: returns all, then re-distributes

test_cases.title: FIFO: insufficient days → auto-convert to ADMINISTRATIVE
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with low balance who has multiple pending vacations
Test data: create vacations that collectively exceed available days
test_cases.steps:
1. Create vacation A (10 days REGULAR, near limit)
2. Manually reduce employee_vacation days (accountant correction)
3. Trigger recalculation
4. Check vacation types
test_cases.expected_result:
Later vacations auto-converted to ADMINISTRATIVE when insufficient REGULAR days
FIFO: earliest vacation gets REGULAR days, later ones become ADMINISTRATIVE
Conversion happens via recalculation service
Notification sent for auto-conversion
test_cases.notes: Sprint 15 #3347: smart reallocation without unnecessary conversion

test_cases.title: Manual day correction — positive (AV=false)

test_cases.title: Manual day correction — negative blocked (AV=false)

test_cases.expected_result:

test_cases.title: Manual day correction — negative allowed (AV=true)

3. Check event feed

test_cases.title: Days by years endpoint verification
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with balances in multiple years
test_cases.steps:
